# Roadmap — tusq.dev Docs & Website Platform

> **M37 Charter Bound — 2026-04-27 in run_0b366d58febc99be (turn_741c43114034bab7, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `f2a153b`.** Intake charter `intent_1777286540380_ef16` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Candidate pool: The Promise, Code And API Surface, Database, Warehouse, And Data Model, Frontend, Design System, And Product UX, Domain Model And Workflows (+25 more). Current roadmap checked through M36: Static Capability Confidence Tier Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.17 (SHIPPED)."). Independently verified on HEAD `f2a153b` that M36 V1.17 is substantively shipped with all 20 ROADMAP checkboxes flipped to `[x]`; `npm test` exits 0 with "Smoke tests passed" and "Eval regression harness passed (27 scenarios)"; the 20-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, **auth**, **confidence**, diff, domain, effect, method, policy, redaction, sensitivity, surface, version, help) is intact with `confidence` between `auth` and `diff`. The charter's `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open` classification is correct — the candidate pool from VISION.md still contains unbound material, particularly **§ Data Leakage** (lines 705–707, under § Risks) which has NOT been the primary aggregation-axis source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity for sensitivity-class derivation/aggregation but neither aggregates capabilities by PII-volume tier; M25 derives `pii_fields[]` per capability but no shipped command rolls those array lengths into a per-tier bucket index). Charter honored by binding exactly one new bounded testable milestone — **M37: Static Capability PII Field Count Tier Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.18 (PROPOSED)** — derived from VISION sources NOT yet primary aggregation-axis source for any shipped milestone: **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 705–707 (`### Data Leakage` under § Risks)** ("Runtime traces, support tickets, docs, and databases can contain customer secrets. Imports must minimize raw value capture, redact aggressively, preserve provenance, and make unsafe source handling visible.") and re-cited cross-axis flag justification: **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 108–114 (`### Data, Risk, And Sensitivity`)** ("PII, payment data, secrets, regulated data, and customer-sensitive fields ... capability risk tags and review recommendations"). Scope: M37 introduces a new top-level noun `pii` with single subcommand `index`, growing the CLI surface from 20 → 21 commands with `pii` inserted alphabetically between `method` and `policy` (`method` vs `pii`: `m` < `p`; `pii` vs `policy`: `pi` < `po` because `i` < `o`). The closed five-value `pii_field_count_tier` bucket-key enum is `none | low | medium | high | unknown`; the closed two-value `aggregation_key` enum is `tier | unknown`; the tier function is PM-frozen (`none if pii_fields.length === 0`, `low if 1 <= length <= 2`, `medium if 3 <= length <= 5`, `high if length >= 6`, `unknown if pii_fields is null/undefined/missing/not-an-array OR contains any non-string element OR contains the empty string`). Boundaries `0/2/5/6` are immutable once M37 ships; any threshold or enum change is a material governance event under its own ROADMAP milestone. The frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape mirrors M31/M32/M33/M34/M35/M36 (`pii_field_count_tier`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`); a top-level `warnings[]` array is added (only in `--json`) capturing per-capability `pii_fields`-malformed warnings (non-array, non-string element, or empty-string element). Bucket iteration order is the closed-enum order `none → low → medium → high → unknown` (NOT manifest first-appearance, NOT count-numeric-ascending — the order is a deterministic stable-output convention only and MUST NOT be described as "PII-exposure-ranked," "data-leakage-ranked," or "redaction-priority-ordered"). Within each bucket, capability names follow manifest declared order. The `--tier` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase values like `HIGH` exit 1 (mirroring M35/M36 precedent). Read-only invariants mirror M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35/M36 plus the explicit non-persistence rule: `pii_field_count_tier` MUST NOT be persisted into `tusq.manifest.json` (it is reviewer-aid metadata only; persistence is reserved for a deferred M-PII-Persistence-1 milestone). The mutually exclusive alternative forms (`tusq pii-volume index` (hyphenated noun rejected per M33 precedent), `tusq pii-fields index`, `tusq leakage index` (overscopes — leakage includes runtime trace handling, log redaction, and database-egress filtering), `tusq capability index --by pii_field_count_tier` (axis-flag rejected per M27/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35/M36 single-aggregation-axis precedent), extending `tusq redaction review` with a tier filter (would conflate per-capability filter with per-bucket roll-up)) are explicitly rejected. Eval regression scenario `pii-field-count-tier-index-determinism` brings the harness from 27 → **≥28** scenarios. Constraint 30 (proposed) codifies the planning-aid framing and forbids "runtime PII detection / runtime data-leakage prevention / GDPR/HIPAA/PCI/PHI compliance certification / runtime field redaction enforcement / alteration of M25 canonical PII name set / persistence of `pii_field_count_tier` into the manifest / cross-axis statistical aggregation" framings in docs/README/CLI-help. Six Key Risk rows added (misread as runtime PII detector / data-leakage gate / compliance certifier; silent threshold drift on `0/2/5/6`; silent enum drift on the five-value `pii_field_count_tier` OR two-value `aggregation_key`; manifest mutation through index generation; `--out` path traversal into `.tusq/`; closed-enum order mistaken as PII-exposure-precedence + case-folding drift on `--tier`). Deferred successor milestones (M-PII-Category-Index-1 multi-valued aggregation by individual PII category, M-Risk-1 cross-axis risk tier, M-PII-Statistics-1 distribution roll-up with `min`/`max`/`mean`/percentiles/histograms, M-PII-Persistence-1 if persistence is later authorized) ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts. The MCP-descriptor candidate (the long-standing outstanding charter sketch in `.planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md` § "MCP Server Static Descriptor Export") remains unbound this turn — the form-decision A/B/C blocker is unresolved and is reserved for a future PM turn. Independent baseline re-verified on HEAD `f2a153b`: `npm test` exits 0 with "Smoke tests passed" and "Eval regression harness passed (27 scenarios)"; the 20-command CLI surface is intact (M37 is unchecked planned work, not shipped — the 21st command `pii` lands in implementation). The full SYSTEM_SPEC § M37 and command-surface § M37 detail blocks are owned by the dev role's implementation phase per the M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35/M36 PM-vs-dev ownership split (PM charters; dev materializes the full spec/algorithm details before any code lands). M37 is implementation-ready planned work, not shipped. **Acceptance contract response to intake charter `intent_1777286540380_ef16`:** (1) New unchecked milestone items added to `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — § M37 below adds 20 unchecked `[ ]` items + 6 Key Risk rows. (2) Milestone cites at least one concrete VISION.md source section from the unplanned backlog — § M37 charter origin block cites `.planning/VISION.md` lines 705–707 (**Data Leakage** under § Risks), which has NOT been the primary aggregation-axis source for any shipped milestone. (3) Milestone is bounded, testable, and does not duplicate existing checked milestones — Bounded: ~0.5 day, single new noun `pii`, single subcommand `index`, four flags, no new dependency. Testable: 23-case smoke matrix and one new eval scenario `pii-field-count-tier-index-determinism`. Non-duplicating: M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35/M36 all bucket on different axes; none aggregates by an evidence-volume tier derived from the per-capability `pii_fields[]` array length. Modified four PM-owned planning artifacts: `.planning/ROADMAP.md` (M37 milestone block + 6 Key Risk rows + this preamble), `.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md` (M37 Charter Bound preamble prepended), `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md` (M37 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended), `.planning/command-surface.md` (M37 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended). Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (`.agentxchain/state.json`, `history.jsonl`, `decision-ledger.jsonl`, `lock.json`). Did NOT modify dev-owned `src/`, `bin/`, `tests/`, `website/`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, or `.planning/IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md`. Did NOT modify QA-owned `acceptance-matrix.md` / `ship-verdict.md` / `RELEASE_NOTES.md`. Did NOT modify launch-owned `MESSAGING.md` / `LAUNCH_PLAN.md` / `CONTENT_CALENDAR.md` / `ANNOUNCEMENT.md`. > **M36 Charter Bound — 2026-04-26 in run_8580d828f0e1cc1e (turn_f657958213bbfb9d, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `7c6b47e`.** Intake charter `intent_1777245829806_cf3c` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Candidate pool: The Promise, Code And API Surface, Database, Warehouse, And Data Model, Frontend, Design System, And Product UX, Domain Model And Workflows (+25 more). Current roadmap checked through M35: Static Capability Auth Scheme Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.16 (PROPOSED)."). Independently verified that M35 is substantively shipped at V1.16 with all 20 ROADMAP checkboxes flipped to `[x]` (closed in `run_152b21c8bbaa78d9` synchronous-mismatch-guard refinement); npm test exits 0 with 26 eval scenarios; the 19-command CLI surface is intact; the M35 V1.16 boundary is the correct anchor for the next charter. The charter is honored by binding exactly one new bounded testable milestone — **M36: Static Capability Confidence Tier Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.17 (PROPOSED)** — derived from VISION sources NOT yet primary aggregation-axis source for any shipped milestone: **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 128–141 (`### Source Inventory And Confidence`)** ("tusq.dev should produce an input inventory before it claims understanding ... confidence by domain ... The engine should not pretend all inputs are equal.") and **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 709–711 (`### False Governance Confidence` under § Risks)** ("Labels such as `approved`, `restricted`, `confidential`, or `auth_scheme: bearer` are not magic. The product must distinguish reviewer-aid metadata from runtime enforcement."). Scope: M36 introduces a new top-level noun `confidence` with single subcommand `index`, growing the CLI surface from 19 → 20 commands with `confidence` inserted alphabetically between `auth` and `diff`. The closed four-value `confidence_tier` bucket-key enum is `high | medium | low | unknown`; the closed two-value `aggregation_key` enum is `tier | unknown`; the tier function is PM-frozen at `high if confidence >= 0.85`, `low if confidence < 0.6`, `medium if 0.6 <= confidence < 0.85`, `unknown if confidence is null/missing/non-numeric/NaN/+Infinity/-Infinity/out-of-[0,1]`. Boundaries `0.85` and `0.6` are immutable once M36 ships; any threshold or enum change is a material governance event under its own ROADMAP milestone with a fresh re-approval expectation. The frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape mirrors M31/M32/M33/M34/M35 (`confidence_tier`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`); a top-level `warnings[]` array is added (only in `--json`) capturing per-capability out-of-range/non-numeric `confidence` warnings. Bucket iteration order is the closed-enum order `high → medium → low → unknown` (NOT confidence-numeric-descending — the order is a deterministic stable-output convention only and MUST NOT be described as "evidence-strength-ranked," "trust-ranked," "highest-quality-first"). Within each bucket, capability names follow manifest declared order. The `--tier` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase values like `HIGH` exit 1 (mirroring M35 `--scheme` precedent). Read-only invariants mirror M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35 plus the explicit non-persistence rule: `confidence_tier` MUST NOT be persisted into `tusq.manifest.json` (it is reviewer-aid metadata only; persistence is reserved for a deferred M-Confidence-Persistence-1 milestone with its own acceptance contract). The mutually exclusive alternative forms (`tusq quality index`, `tusq evidence index`, `tusq capability index --by confidence_tier`, extending `tusq review` with a tier filter) are explicitly rejected: `quality` overscopes (correctness/completeness/freshness/contradiction-resolution); `evidence` is ambiguous (M28/M29/M30/M27 all use "evidence" elsewhere); an `--by` axis flag would couple multiple aggregation modes and violate the M27/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35 single-aggregation-axis precedent; extending `tusq review` would conflate per-capability filter with per-bucket roll-up. Eval regression scenario `confidence-tier-index-determinism` brings the harness from 26 → **≥27** scenarios. Constraint 29 (proposed) codifies the planning-aid framing and forbids "runtime confidence enforcement / evidence-quality scoring engine / automated re-classification / alteration of scanner `confidence` derivation rules / persistence of `confidence_tier` into the manifest / cross-axis statistical aggregation" framings in docs/README/CLI-help. Six Key Risk rows added (misread as runtime gate / quality engine / re-classifier; silent threshold drift on `0.85`/`0.6`; silent enum drift on the four-value `confidence_tier` OR two-value `aggregation_key`; manifest mutation through index generation; `--out` path traversal into `.tusq/`; closed-enum order mistaken as quality-precedence + case-folding drift on `--tier`). Deferred successor milestones (M-Risk-1 cross-axis risk tier, M-Confidence-Statistics-1 distribution roll-up with `min`/`max`/`mean`/percentiles/histograms, M-Confidence-Persistence-1 if persistence into the manifest is later authorized) ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts. The MCP-descriptor candidate (the long-standing outstanding charter sketch in `.planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md` § "MCP Server Static Descriptor Export") remains unbound this turn — the form-decision A/B/C blocker is unresolved and is reserved for a future PM turn. Independent baseline re-verified on HEAD `7c6b47e`: `npm test` exits 0 with "Smoke tests passed" and "Eval regression harness passed (26 scenarios)"; the 19-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, **auth**, diff, domain, effect, method, policy, redaction, sensitivity, surface, version, help) is intact (M36 is unchecked planned work, not shipped — the 20th command `confidence` lands in implementation). The full SYSTEM_SPEC § M36 and command-surface § M36 detail blocks are owned by the dev role's implementation phase per the M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35 PM-vs-dev ownership split (PM charters; dev materializes the full spec/algorithm details before any code lands). M36 is implementation-ready planned work, not shipped. **Acceptance contract response to intake charter `intent_1777245829806_cf3c`:** (1) New unchecked milestone items added to `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — § M36 below adds 20 unchecked `[ ]` items + 6 Key Risk rows. (2) Milestone cites at least one concrete VISION.md source section from the unplanned backlog — § M36 charter origin block cites `.planning/VISION.md` lines 128–141 (**Source Inventory And Confidence**) and lines 709–711 (**False Governance Confidence**), neither of which has been the primary aggregation-axis source for any shipped milestone. (3) Milestone is bounded, testable, and does not duplicate existing checked milestones — Bounded: ~0.5 day, single new noun `confidence`, single subcommand `index`, four flags, no new dependency. Testable: 22-case smoke matrix and one new eval scenario `confidence-tier-index-determinism`. Non-duplicating: M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34/M35 all bucket on different axes; none aggregates by an evidence-quality tier derived from the numeric `confidence` field. Modified four PM-owned planning artifacts: `.planning/ROADMAP.md` (M36 milestone block + 6 Key Risk rows + this preamble), `.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md` (M36 Charter Bound preamble prepended), `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md` (M36 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended), `.planning/command-surface.md` (M36 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended). > **M35 Synchronous Mismatch Guard Refinement — 2026-04-26 in run_152b21c8bbaa78d9 (turn_ec2716f4b8fe4ff4, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `dd91aa2`.** Intake charter `intent_1777244614389_af70` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_open_work_detected`) re-injected the M35 V1.16 (PROPOSED) `auth_scheme` bucket-key enum line as if unshipped — the 9th recurrence of the vision_scan stale-checkbox false-positive pattern (M28 → M30 → M31 → M32 → M33 → M33-again → M34-again → M34-again-again → M35-again). Independent re-verification on HEAD `dd91aa2`: `npm test` exits 0 with "Smoke tests passed" and "Eval regression harness passed (26 scenarios)"; the 19-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, **auth**, diff, domain, effect, method, policy, redaction, sensitivity, surface, version, help) is intact with `auth` between `approve` and `diff`; `tusq auth index --help` exits 0 with the planning-aid framing callout and closed-enum bucket iteration order `bearer → api_key → session → basic → oauth → none → unknown`; `node bin/tusq.js auth index --scheme BEARER --manifest tests/fixtures/express-sample/tusq.manifest.json` exits 1 with `Unknown auth scheme: BEARER` (case-sensitive lowercase enforcement confirmed). M35 is substantively shipped at V1.16. Reconciled documentation drift this turn by flipping 19 of 20 M35 ROADMAP checkboxes from `[ ]` to `[x]`. **One bullet (line 629, the bucket-key enum bullet) deliberately remains `[ ]` because the charter's sharpened sub-requirement "M35 MUST reference the M29 `AUTH_SCHEMES` constant directly (not redeclare an independent enum) so any future M29 classifier change surfaces as a synchronous mismatch rather than silent drift" is NOT strictly satisfied by the current implementation.** `src/cli.js:108` declares `AUTH_SCHEME_INDEX_BUCKET_ORDER` as an independent frozen literal array `['bearer', 'api_key', 'session', 'basic', 'oauth', 'none']` with only an alignment comment at line 107 (`NOTE: bucket-key enum aligns with AUTH_SCHEMES (M29, line 9) — the seven-value enum IS the AUTH_SCHEMES set.`); there is no runtime mismatch guard that fires if M29's `AUTH_SCHEMES` (line 9: `['unknown', 'bearer', 'api_key', 'session', 'basic', 'oauth', 'none']`) is ever extended (e.g., to `mtls`, `aws_sigv4`, `digest`) without a corresponding update to `AUTH_SCHEME_INDEX_BUCKET_ORDER`. The substantive 19 bullets (CLI noun/subcommand, command implementation, aggregation_key enum, default behavior, per-bucket entry shape, read-only invariants, deterministic output, empty-capabilities exit-0 rule, I/O stream discipline, --help framing, --out path rules, tusq help insertion, smoke matrix, eval regression scenario, Constraint 28, SYSTEM_SPEC § M35, two website docs, npm test continues to exit 0) are all genuinely shipped (`auth` is the 9th alphabetic command between `approve` and `diff` — note: `diff` (`d`) sorts before `compile` (`c`) was a typo in the original charter; the actual current alphabetic placement is `approve → auth → diff` and is correct). **Dev-implementation directive for this run:** close line 629 by (a) deriving `AUTH_SCHEME_INDEX_BUCKET_ORDER` from `AUTH_SCHEMES` (e.g., `Object.freeze(AUTH_SCHEMES.filter((s) => s !== 'unknown'))` or equivalent that puts the named six in `bearer → api_key → session → basic → oauth → none` order), AND (b) adding a synchronous module-init guard that throws if the named-six set + `unknown` ever diverges from `AUTH_SCHEMES`. The guard implementation is bounded (~10 lines), preserves all 26 eval scenarios as exit-0, preserves the 19-command CLI surface, preserves all per-bucket entry shape semantics, and preserves the closed-enum bucket iteration order. After dev closes line 629, qa re-verifies and launch closes V1.16. Modified four PM-owned planning artifacts this turn: `.planning/ROADMAP.md` (this preamble + 19 checkbox flips), `.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md` (re-affirmation block prepended), `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md` (re-affirmation line prepended), `.planning/command-surface.md` (re-affirmation line prepended). Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state, dev-owned source/tests/website/package, QA-owned acceptance-matrix/ship-verdict/RELEASE_NOTES, launch-owned MESSAGING/LAUNCH_PLAN/CONTENT_CALENDAR/ANNOUNCEMENT, VISION.md, or ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md. Phase transition to implementation requested under the run's auto_approve policy.

> **M35 Charter Bound — 2026-04-26 in run_0b373a30d182816a (turn_d7fc926d1c177c66, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `b129ca9`.** This turn binds **M35: Static Capability Auth Scheme Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.16 (PROPOSED)** as the next bounded roadmap increment, derived from the intake charter `intent_1777242416896_42c9` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Candidate pool: The Promise, Code And API Surface, Database, Warehouse, And Data Model, Frontend, Design System, And Product UX, Domain Model And Workflows (+25 more). Current roadmap checked through M34: Static Capability HTTP Method Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.15 (PROPOSED)."). Challenged the charter explicitly rather than rubber-stamping: the charter correctly identifies that the roadmap is checked through M34 (V1.15 SHIPPED in run_bf8efb6b9c733000 across all four phases planning → implementation → qa → launch with `run_completion_request=true`, then re-affirmed in run_9b4197b36f01ca42 as a stale-checkbox cycle that was correctly absorbed by the prior PM/dev/qa/launch loop) and that the candidate pool from VISION.md still contains unbound material — particularly the **Auth, IAM, Permissions, And Tenant Boundaries** section (lines 56–64), whose "respect the same access rules" promise is operationally answered by a per-`auth_scheme` capability roll-up. The charter is honored by binding exactly one new bounded testable milestone (M35), not by re-running M34, not by leaving the roadmap idle, and not by binding multiple competing candidates in one turn. VISION sources cited: **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 56–64 (`### Auth, IAM, Permissions, And Tenant Boundaries`)** — explicitly within the charter's "+25 more" candidate pool; the section enumerates "user authentication, session boundaries, OAuth and OIDC scopes, generated MCP-server adapters, multi-tenant boundaries, role-based and attribute-based access control" and closes with "The generated AI layer must respect the same access rules the logged-in user would have inside the SaaS portal." The per-`auth_scheme` index is the smallest reviewer-aid that lets an operator see, at a glance, "how many capabilities are behind no auth at all, how many are behind unknown auth, how many are bearer-token-protected" — the central question that operationalizes the section's "respect the same access rules" promise. Plus **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 689–697 (`### Capability Overexposure` under § Risks)** — surfacing per-bucket cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` per auth bucket so reviewers can see if the `none` or `unknown` auth bucket contains destructive or restricted-sensitivity capabilities (the highest-attention combination). Shipped reality M1–M34 covers the compiler/governance half plus the M27 redaction-review reviewer-aid, the M30 surface-plan planner, the M31 per-domain index, the M32 per-side-effect-class index, the M33 per-sensitivity-class index, and the M34 per-HTTP-method index. The per-auth-scheme capability index — the parallel reviewer-aid that buckets capabilities by their `auth_requirements.auth_scheme` value (M29's frozen seven-value enum) — has not shipped. M29 produced the per-capability `auth_scheme` value and a `--auth-scheme` filter on the existing `tusq review` command, but does NOT aggregate capabilities into a per-scheme roll-up; reviewers asking "how many capabilities are in the `none` or `unknown` auth bucket" must currently re-run `tusq review --auth-scheme <value>` once per scheme and manually count. M35 is the smallest first increment that addresses the auth-scheme-visibility dimension of VISION § Auth, IAM, Permissions, And Tenant Boundaries **without** committing the project to runtime authentication enforcement, runtime AAA validation, OAuth/OIDC/SAML compliance certification, generated auth-adapter code, IAM-context derivation, or alteration of M29's `auth_scheme` derivation rules (M29 derives `auth_scheme` from middleware-name regex evidence; M35 buckets capabilities by the manifest's verbatim `auth_scheme` value and MUST NOT touch M29 rules). Scope decision resolved this turn: M35 introduces a new top-level noun `auth` with a single subcommand `index` (mirroring the M27 `redaction review`, M30 `surface plan`, M31 `domain index`, M32 `effect index`, M33 `sensitivity index`, and M34 `method index` precedents). The CLI surface grows from 18 → 19 commands with `auth` inserted alphabetically between `approve` and `compile` (`approve` vs `auth`: `app` < `aut` because `p` < `u`; `auth` vs `compile`: `a` < `c`). The mutually exclusive alternative forms (`tusq iam index`, `tusq scheme index`, `tusq capability index --by auth_scheme`, extending `tusq review --auth-scheme`) are explicitly rejected for V1.16: `iam` overscopes the milestone (IAM includes roles, scopes, tenancy, attributes — not just the auth-scheme dimension); `scheme` is ambiguous against unrelated uses (URL scheme in route field, schema fields from M24); an `--by` axis flag would couple multiple aggregation modes and violate the M27/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34 single-aggregation-axis precedent; extending `tusq review --auth-scheme` would conflate the per-capability filter (one capability per row) with the per-bucket roll-up (one bucket per row). The closed seven-value `auth_scheme` bucket-key enum (`bearer | api_key | session | basic | oauth | none | unknown`) aligns 1:1 with the existing M29 `AUTH_SCHEMES` constant in `src/cli.js` (`['unknown', 'bearer', 'api_key', 'session', 'basic', 'oauth', 'none']`) — note that `unknown` is already a member of M29 AUTH_SCHEMES (unlike M34 where `unknown` was a synthesized catchall outside the canonical five), so M35 has no separate "synthesized catchall" — the seven-value enum IS the bucket-key enum and IS the M29 AUTH_SCHEMES set; extending the bucket-key enum to additional values (e.g., `mtls`, `aws_sigv4`, `digest`, `hmac`, `webhook_signature`) would require a corresponding extension to M29 AUTH_SCHEMES, which is itself a material governance event under its own ROADMAP milestone with a fresh re-approval expectation. The closed two-value `aggregation_key` enum is `scheme | unknown` (parallel to M31 `domain | unknown`, M32 `class | unknown`, M33 `class | unknown`, M34 `method | unknown`). The frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape (`auth_scheme`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`) is intentionally name-and-counters-only and intentionally **omits** cross-axis roll-up arrays (`domains_represented[]`, `auth_scopes_represented[]`, `auth_roles_represented[]`, `methods_represented[]`, `side_effect_classes_represented[]`, `sensitivity_classes_represented[]`) and **omits** any derived `risk_tier` or `iam_posture` — both reserved for the deferred M-Risk-1 / M-IAM-Posture-1 milestones with their own enums, their own acceptance contracts, and their own re-approval expectations. Bucket iteration order is the **closed-enum order** `bearer → api_key → session → basic → oauth → none → unknown` (NOT manifest first-appearance — M31's first-appearance rule applies to open-string `domain` values, not to closed enums; NOT IAM-strength-precedence — the closed-enum order is a deterministic stable-output convention only and MUST NOT be described as "strength-ordering," "trust-ranking," "security-ascending," or any phrase implying security semantics; the `unknown` bucket is appended last, mirroring M32/M33/M34). Within each bucket, capability names follow manifest declared order (mirroring M31/M32/M33/M34 within-bucket ordering). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear (a `bearer` bucket appears only if at least one capability has `auth_scheme === "bearer"`). Read-only invariants mirror M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34: zero manifest mutation, zero `capability_digest` flips, zero new dependency in `package.json`, byte-identical `tusq compile`/`tusq serve`/`tusq policy verify`/`tusq redaction review`/`tusq surface plan`/`tusq domain index`/`tusq effect index`/`tusq sensitivity index`/`tusq method index` output pre and post-M35. Empty-capabilities exits 0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) and `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, schemes: [] }` (`--json`); every error path empties stdout and writes exclusively to stderr. The `--out <path>` flag rejects any path resolving inside `.tusq/` (mirroring M30/M31/M32/M33/M34). The `--scheme <value>` filter limits output to a single bucket (string match against one of the seven enum values: `bearer`, `api_key`, `session`, `basic`, `oauth`, `none`, `unknown`); an unrecognized filter value exits 1 with `Unknown auth scheme:` followed by the value, empty stdout. The `--scheme` flag value matching is **case-sensitive** (lowercase only) — mirroring the manifest's verbatim lowercase `auth_scheme` field convention and the M29 AUTH_SCHEMES constant; uppercase values like `BEARER` or `bearer ` (with whitespace) exit 1 (rather than being lower-cased or trimmed silently) so reviewer scripts cannot accidentally drift from the canonical lowercase form. Eval regression scenario `auth-scheme-index-determinism` brings the harness from 25 → **≥26** scenarios. Constraint 28 (proposed) codifies the planning-aid framing and forbids "runtime authentication enforcement / AAA validation / OAuth/OIDC/SAML compliance certification / auth-adapter code generation / IAM-context derivation / alteration of M29 `auth_scheme` derivation rules / alteration of the M30 `gated_reason: auth_scheme_unknown` or `gated_reason: auth_scheme_oauth_pending_v2` surface-eligibility rules" framings in docs/README/CLI-help. Six Key Risk rows added (misread as runtime auth enforcer / AAA validator / compliance certifier; silent enum drift on `auth_scheme` bucket-key OR `aggregation_key`; manifest mutation through index generation; `--out` path traversal into `.tusq/`; closed-enum order mistaken as IAM-strength-precedence; case-folding drift on `--scheme` filter relative to manifest lowercase convention). The deferred successor milestones (M-IAM-Posture-1 cross-axis IAM-posture roll-up, M-Auth-Adapter-1 generated auth adapter, M-Runtime-Auth-1 runtime auth validator) for actual policy/runtime artifacts remain deferred — each ships under its own ROADMAP entry with a fresh acceptance contract. The MCP-descriptor candidate (the long-standing outstanding charter sketch in `.planning/ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md` § "MCP Server Static Descriptor Export") remains unbound this turn — the form-decision A/B/C blocker is unresolved and is reserved for a future PM turn. Independent baseline re-verified on HEAD `b129ca9`: `npm test` exits 0 with "Smoke tests passed" and "Eval regression harness passed (25 scenarios)"; the 18-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, diff, domain, effect, method, policy, redaction, sensitivity, surface, version, help) is intact (M35 is unchecked planned work, not shipped — the 19th command `auth` lands in implementation). The full SYSTEM_SPEC § M35 and command-surface § M35 detail blocks are owned by the dev role's implementation phase per the M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33/M34 PM-vs-dev ownership split (PM charters; dev materializes the full spec/algorithm details before any code lands). M35 is implementation-ready planned work, not shipped. Modified four PM-owned planning artifacts: `.planning/ROADMAP.md` (M35 milestone block + 6 Key Risk rows + this preamble), `.planning/PM_SIGNOFF.md` (M35 Charter Bound preamble prepended), `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md` (M35 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended), `.planning/command-surface.md` (M35 Charter Sketch Reservation line prepended). Did NOT modify reserved orchestrator state files (`.agentxchain/state.json`, `history.jsonl`, `decision-ledger.jsonl`, `lock.json`). Did NOT modify dev-owned `src/`, `bin/`, `tests/`, `website/`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, or `.planning/IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md`. Did NOT modify QA-owned `acceptance-matrix.md` / `ship-verdict.md` / `RELEASE_NOTES.md`. Did NOT modify launch-owned `MESSAGING.md` / `LAUNCH_PLAN.md` / `CONTENT_CALENDAR.md` / `ANNOUNCEMENT.md`. Did NOT modify `VISION.md` (operator-owned) or `ROADMAP_NEXT_CANDIDATES.md`. Phase transition to implementation requested under the run's auto_approve policy.

> **M34 Charter Bound — 2026-04-26 in run_bf8efb6b9c733000 (turn_8656b25a486eaa6d, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `8921229`.** This turn binds **M34: Static Capability HTTP Method Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.15 (PROPOSED)** as the next bounded roadmap increment, derived from the intake charter `intent_1777239733034_e0fb` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Candidate pool: The Promise, Code And API Surface, Database, Warehouse, And Data Model, Frontend, Design System, And Product UX, Domain Model And Workflows (+25 more). Current roadmap checked through M33: Static Capability Sensitivity Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.14 (PROPOSED)."). Challenged the charter explicitly rather than rubber-stamping: the charter correctly identifies that the roadmap is checked through M33 (V1.14 SHIPPED in run_4506c41d74e23e8e and re-verified in run_cd98cdad0fb83285) and that the candidate pool from VISION.md still contains unbound material. The charter is honored by binding exactly one new bounded testable milestone (M34), not by re-running M33, not by leaving the roadmap idle, and not by binding multiple competing candidates in one turn. VISION sources cited: **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 40–46 (`### Code And API Surface`)** — explicitly named in the charter's candidate pool; item 1 enumerates "REST routes, GraphQL operations, queue handlers, and similar entry points" — the **HTTP method (verb)** is the first-class attribute of every REST route and the most operator-recognizable axis of capability surface area; reviewers ask "how many DELETE / POST / PUT / PATCH endpoints am I exposing" daily, but no shipped milestone aggregates capabilities by their `method` field. Plus **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 689–697 (`### Capability Overexposure` under § Risks)** — "An AI surface that exposes too many capabilities or destructive workflows ... governance must show what is exposed at any moment and require approval for changes"; surfacing the count of destructive verbs (DELETE/POST/PUT/PATCH) per HTTP-method bucket — and the per-bucket cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_unknown_auth` — is the smallest reviewer-aid that lets an operator see, at a glance, "are my POST endpoints behind unknown auth, and are my DELETE endpoints in the destructive side-effect bucket." Shipped reality M1–M33 covers the compiler/governance half plus the M27 redaction-review reviewer-aid, the M30 surface-plan planner, the M31 per-domain index, the M32 per-side-effect-class index, and the M33 per-sensitivity-class index. The per-HTTP-method index — the parallel reviewer-aid that buckets capabilities by their `method` value (the canonical HTTP verb attached to every capability since M1) — has not shipped. M34 is the smallest first increment that addresses the HTTP-method-visibility dimension of VISION § Code And API Surface **without** committing the project to runtime HTTP-method routing, REST-convention validation, idempotency certification, automatic destructive-verb classification, or alteration of the M32 `side_effect_class` derivation rules (M32 derives `side_effect_class` from `method` plus other signals; M34 buckets capabilities by the manifest's verbatim `method` value and MUST NOT touch M32 rules). Scope decision resolved this turn: M34 introduces a new top-level noun `method` with a single subcommand `index` (mirroring the M27 `redaction review`, M30 `surface plan`, M31 `domain index`, M32 `effect index`, and M33 `sensitivity index` precedents). The CLI surface grows from 17 → 18 commands with `method` inserted alphabetically between `effect` and `policy` (`method` vs `policy`: `m` < `p`; `method` vs `effect`: `e` < `m`). The mutually exclusive alternative forms (`tusq verb index` with `verb` as the noun, `tusq http index` with a protocol-prefixed noun, `tusq capability index --by method` with an axis-flag) are explicitly rejected for V1.15: `verb` is ambiguous against grammatical use elsewhere in tusq help text; `http` would suggest broader HTTP-protocol scope (headers, status codes, content types) that this milestone explicitly does not address; an `--by` axis flag would couple multiple aggregation modes into one command and violate the M27/M30/M31/M32/M33 single-aggregation-axis precedent. The closed five-value `http_method` bucket-key enum (`GET | POST | PUT | PATCH | DELETE | unknown`) covers the canonical REST verbs that tusq's scanner already emits in the manifest's `method` field; HEAD, OPTIONS, TRACE, CONNECT, and any non-standard verb aggregate into `unknown` (mirroring M28 / M29 / M31 / M32 / M33 zero-evidence guards). The closed two-value `aggregation_key` enum is `method | unknown` (parallel to M31 `domain | unknown`, M32 `class | unknown`, M33 `class | unknown`). The frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape (`http_method`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_unknown_auth`) is intentionally name-and-counters-only and intentionally **omits** cross-axis roll-up arrays (`domains_represented[]`, `auth_schemes_represented[]`, `side_effect_classes_represented[]`, `sensitivity_classes_represented[]`) and **omits** any derived `idempotency_class` or `risk_tier` — both reserved for the deferred M-Risk-1 / M-Idempotency-1 milestones with their own enums, their own acceptance contracts, and their own re-approval expectations. Bucket iteration order is the **closed-enum order** `GET → POST → PUT → PATCH → DELETE → unknown` (NOT manifest first-appearance — distinct from M31's first-appearance rule because M34 buckets on a closed enum, not an open string; NOT risk-precedence — the closed-enum order matches the conventional REST CRUD reading order (GET=read, POST=create, PUT=replace, PATCH=update, DELETE=delete) but carries no risk semantic and MUST NOT be described as "low-to-high risk," "destructive-ascending," "safety-ordered," or any phrase implying risk semantics; the `unknown` bucket is appended last, mirroring M32/M33). Within each bucket, capability names follow manifest declared order (mirroring M31 / M32 / M33 within-bucket ordering). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear (a `DELETE` bucket appears only if at least one capability has `method === "DELETE"` — distinct from emitting an empty array for missing buckets). Read-only invariants mirror M27/M28/M29/M30/M31/M32/M33: zero manifest mutation, zero `capability_digest` flips, zero new dependency in `package.json`, byte-identical `tusq compile`/`tusq serve`/`tusq policy verify`/`tusq redaction review`/`tusq surface plan`/`tusq domain index`/`tusq effect index`/`tusq sensitivity index` output pre and post-M34. Empty-capabilities exits 0 with `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) and `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, methods: [] }` (`--json`); every error path empties stdout and writes exclusively to stderr. The `--out <path>` flag rejects any path resolving inside `.tusq/` (mirroring M30/M31/M32/M33). The `--method <value>` filter limits output to a single bucket (string match against one of the five enum values: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`, plus the literal `unknown`); an unrecognized filter value exits 1 with `Unknown method:` followed by the value, empty stdout. The `--method` flag value matching is **case-sensitive** (uppercase only) — mirroring the manifest's verbatim uppercase `method` field convention; lowercase values like `get` exit 1 (rather than being upper-cased silently) so reviewer scripts cannot accidentally drift from the canonical uppercase form. Eval regression scenario `method-index-determinism` brings the harness from 24 → **≥25** scenarios. The `--out <path>` flag rejects any path resolving inside `.tusq/` (mirroring M30/M31). The `--effect <value>` filter limits output to a single bucket (string match against one of the four enum values); an unrecognized value exits 1 with `Unknown effect:` followed by the value, empty stdout. ## Phases

| Phase | Goal | Status |
|-------|------|--------|
| Planning | Define Docusaurus platform scope, content architecture, and acceptance criteria | Complete (parent run) |
| Implementation | Build Docusaurus site, migrate content, create docs and blog | Complete (parent run) |
| QA | Validate build, links, content accuracy, and product truth alignment | Complete (parent run) |

## Implementation Milestones

### M1: Docusaurus Project Setup (~0.5 day)
- [x] Initialize Docusaurus 3.x project in `website/` directory
- [x] Configure `docusaurus.config.ts` with site metadata, navbar, footer
- [x] Set up `package.json` with required dependencies
- [x] Configure TypeScript support
- [x] Verify `npm run build` and `npm start` work with default content

### M2: Brand & Theme (~0.5 day)
- [x] Migrate brand colors and fonts from `websites/styles.css` to `website/src/css/custom.css`
- [x] Configure Fraunces + Space Grotesk font loading
- [x] Set up custom color palette in Docusaurus theme config
- [x] Create or migrate any logo/favicon assets to `website/static/img/`

### M3: Homepage Component (~0.5 day)
- [x] Create custom homepage component at `website/src/pages/index.tsx`
- [x] Migrate hero section (eyebrow, headline, lede, CTA)
- [x] Migrate feature cards (What it does / ships / matters)
- [x] Migrate workflow steps section
- [x] Migrate V1 surface grid
- [x] Update CTAs to point to docs (Getting Started) instead of README.md

### M4: Documentation Pages (~1 day)
- [x] Create docs sidebar structure in `sidebars.ts`
- [x] Write Getting Started page (install + 6-command workflow)
- [x] Write CLI Reference page (all 8 commands, flags, exit codes)
- [x] Write Manifest Format page (capability fields, schema, examples)
- [x] Write Configuration page (tusq.config.json reference)
- [x] Write Supported Frameworks page (Express, Fastify, NestJS)
- [x] Write MCP Server page (describe-only behavior, tools/list, tools/call)
- [x] Write FAQ page (from MESSAGING.md claims/non-claims)

### M5: Blog Setup (~0.5 day)
- [x] Configure Docusaurus blog plugin (already built-in)
- [x] Adapt ANNOUNCEMENT.md into a blog post with proper frontmatter
- [x] Adapt RELEASE_NOTES.md into a blog post with proper frontmatter
- [x] Verify blog index and RSS feed generation

### M6: Navigation, 404, and Polish (~0.5 day)
- [x] Configure top navbar: Docs, Blog, GitHub
- [x] Verify docs sidebar ordering and grouping
- [x] Create custom 404 page (adapt from `websites/404.html`)
- [x] Add SEO metadata (Open Graph, descriptions) to all pages

### M13: Version History and Diffs Specification (~0.5 day)

- [x] Add `capability_digest` (SHA-256 hex of content fields) to manifest Capability object shape in SYSTEM_SPEC.md

### M15: First-Pass Manifest Usability (~1 day)
- [x] Implement path parameter extraction in `finalizeRouteInference()` — extract `:param` and `{param}` tokens from route paths into `input_schema.properties` with `required` array
- [x] Implement smart domain inference in `inferDomain()` — skip common API prefixes (`api`, `v1`–`v5`, `rest`, `graphql`, `internal`, `public`, `external`) before selecting first meaningful segment
- [x] Implement rich capability descriptions in `describeCapability()` — use verb/noun/qualifier/side_effect/auth_context template instead of static "METHOD /path capability in domain domain"
- [x] Implement honest confidence penalty in `scoreConfidence()` — subtract 0.10 when no schema hint is detected, pushing schema-less routes below `review_needed` threshold of 0.8
- [x] Update smoke tests to assert: path params appear in `input_schema.properties`, domain inference skips prefixes, descriptions contain handler name and auth context, confidence is below 0.8 for schema-less routes with named handler + auth
- [x] Update `website/docs/manifest-format.md` to document path parameter extraction, rich descriptions, and updated confidence scoring

### M16: Manifest Diff and Review Queue (~1 day)
- [x] Add `tusq diff` command to compare two manifest files with explicit `--from <path>` and `--to <path>` inputs
- [x] Default `tusq diff` to comparing the current `tusq.manifest.json` against the predecessor implied by `previous_manifest_hash` only when a predecessor file is available locally
- [x] Emit a human-readable diff summary with added, removed, changed, and unchanged capability counts
- [x] Emit `--json` output using the planned structured diff shape from SYSTEM_SPEC.md
- [x] Detect changed capabilities by `capability_digest` and report field-level changes for changed records
- [x] Generate a review queue for added, changed, unapproved, and `review_needed` capabilities
- [x] Add `--fail-on-unapproved-changes` to exit non-zero when added or changed capabilities are not approved
- [x] Update `tusq help`, command reference docs, manifest docs, README examples, and smoke tests for the new diff workflow

- [x] Add an executable Node eval runner in `tests/eval-regression.mjs`

- [x] Validate compiled tool-call metadata including auth hints, side-effect class, schema sources, examples, constraints, redaction, and manifest-only approval metadata boundaries
