(b) `--tier low`; (c) `--tier medium`; (d) `--tier high` (synthetic fixture with ≥15-token description); (e) `--tier unknown` (synthetic with missing/null/empty description); (f) `--tier LOW` (exit 1, case-sensitive); (g) `--tier xyz` (exit 1, unknown tier); (h) `--manifest <missing>` (exit 1, file not found); (i) `--manifest <malformed.json>` (exit 1, parse error); (j) `--manifest <no-capabilities>` (exit 1, missing capabilities); (k) `--unknown-flag` (exit 1, unknown flag); (l) `--tier` with no value (exit 1); (m) `--out <valid path>` writes correctly and stdout is empty; (n) `--out .tusq/foo.json` (exit 1, must not be inside .tusq/); (o) `--out <unwritable parent>` (exit 1, parent directory does not exist or is not writable); (p) `--json` outputs valid JSON with `tiers[]` and `warnings: []` (or with warnings populated when malformed description is present); (q) determinism: three consecutive runs produce byte-identical stdout; (r) manifest mtime + SHA-256 + per-capability `capability_digest` invariant pre/post AND `description_word_count_tier` MUST NOT appear in `tusq.manifest.json` after run (non-persistence) AND `tusq compile` byte-identical pre/post; (s) `tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index` all byte-identical pre/post; (t) empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, tiers: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (u) malformed-`description` capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed description (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all three frozen reason codes (`description_field_missing`, `description_field_not_string`, `description_field_empty_after_trim` — exercised with `description: undefined` (key absent), `description: 42`, `description: '   '` (whitespace-only) respectively); (v) tier boundary values: `description: "a b c d e f g"` (7 tokens) → low, `description: "a b c d e f g h"` (8 tokens) → medium, `description: "a b c d e f g h i j k l m n"` (14 tokens) → medium, `description: "a b c d e f g h i j k l m n o"` (15 tokens) → high; (w) Unicode whitespace tokenization: `description: "a\tb\nc"` (3 tokens via `\s` `u` flag) → low; (x) markdown-not-stripped: `description: "**bold** _italic_ \`code\`"` (3 tokens, markdown literal) → low. Update existing M35/M36/M37/M38/M39/M40/M41/M42/M43 help-count assertions from 27 to 28.
- [x] Add `description-word-count-tier-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval count 34 → 35). Add `runDescriptionWordCountTierIndexDeterminismScenario` handler to `tests/eval-regression.mjs` verifying byte-identical output across three runs, closed four-value bucket-key enum, closed two-value aggregation_key enum, tier order determinism, `warnings[]` always present, manifest non-mutation, `description_word_count_tier` non-persistence.
- [x] Update `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with this turn's M44 entry (challenge to PM turn, implementation details, verification evidence: `npm test` exit 0 with 35 scenarios, zero new dependencies, zero package drift, 28-command CLI surface confirmed, all M44 invariants verified).
- [x] Add `### M44` detail block to `SYSTEM_SPEC.md` with: purpose statement, command shape, frozen four-value `description_word_count_tier` bucket-key enum, frozen two-value `aggregation_key` enum, frozen tier-function rules (whitespace-token-count thresholds 7/14 with `u`-flag Unicode whitespace splitting, no markdown/HTML/punctuation stripping, empty-after-trim → unknown, non-string → unknown, missing → unknown), frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape, top-level `warnings[]` rule with three frozen reason codes, closed-enum bucket iteration order rule (`low → medium → high → unknown`), within-bucket manifest-declared-order rule, case-sensitive `--tier` filter rule, empty-capabilities and stdout-discipline rules, read-only invariants including the explicit non-persistence-of-`description_word_count_tier` rule, the no-sub-schema-walking rule, the no-markdown-stripping rule, the no-per-language-handling rule, and the explicit non-runtime-doc-validator / non-doc-conformance-detector / non-description-generator / non-claim-completeness-certifier boundary statement.
- [x] Add `### M44` Product CLI Surface block to `command-surface.md` with the two-row command table, four-flag table, bucket-key enum table, aggregation_key enum table, tier-function rules table, per-bucket entry shape table, bucket iteration order table, default-preservation table for the 27 unchanged commands, failure UX table, and local-only invariants table.
- [x] Add Constraint 37 to SYSTEM_SPEC.md Constraints section: "`tusq description index` is a planning aid that surfaces the natural-language word-count tier of capability descriptions and the cross-axis side-effect/sensitivity exposure of each tier bucket. It does NOT execute capability invocations at runtime, does NOT validate published documentation against the declared `description`, does NOT cross-reference manifest descriptions vs published help-center articles or API docs (that is a deferred runtime-conformance milestone), does NOT generate descriptions, does NOT measure documentation-quality variance or readability scores, does NOT certify customer-facing-claim completeness, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical description extraction rules, does NOT walk `input_schema.description` / `output_schema.description` / `examples[].description` (only the literal top-level `description` field is read), does NOT strip markdown / HTML tags / punctuation / numbers (whitespace-token splitting is naïve and Unicode-aware via the `u` flag), does NOT apply per-language word-count handling (CJK descriptions with no whitespace tokenize as a single token regardless of grapheme count; the per-language split is reserved for `M-Description-Per-Language-Word-Count-Tier-Index-1`), does NOT cross-reference the description against `examples[]` or `auth_hints[]` claim-content (cross-axis claim-richness rolls reserved for `M-Description-Cross-Axis-Claim-Richness-Index-1`), does NOT promote `unknown` to per-cause sub-buckets (single `unknown` bucket; per-cause split reserved for `M-Description-Per-Cause-Sub-Bucket-Index-1`), does NOT persist `description_word_count_tier` into `tusq.manifest.json`, and does NOT compute statistical aggregates (`min`, `max`, `mean`, percentiles, distributions). Subsequent milestones (`M-Description-Char-Length-Tier-Index-1`, `M-Description-Sentence-Count-Tier-Index-1`, `M-Description-Sub-Schema-Word-Count-Tier-Index-1`, `M-Description-Per-Language-Word-Count-Tier-Index-1`, `M-Description-Cross-Axis-Claim-Richness-Index-1`, `M-Description-Per-Cause-Sub-Bucket-Index-1`, `M-Description-Word-Count-Tier-Threshold-Adjustment-1` if thresholds are later authorized for change, `M-Description-Persistence-1` if persistence is later authorized, `M-Description-Doc-Contradiction-1`, `M-Description-Markdown-Strip-1`, `M-Description-Runtime-Conformance-1`) ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts and fresh re-approval expectations. The four-value `description_word_count_tier` bucket-key enum (`low | medium | high | unknown`), the two-value `aggregation_key` enum (`tier | unknown`), the three-value warning reason-code enum (`description_field_missing | description_field_not_string | description_field_empty_after_trim`), and the threshold pair (`7`, `14`) are frozen; any addition is a material governance event. The `--tier` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case filter values exit 1 with `Unknown description word count tier:`. Malformed `description` values are bucketed as `unknown` and emit a `Warning: ...` to stderr (human) or `warnings[]` (`--json`); they MUST NOT be silently coerced to `'low'` or any other default. The `unknown` bucket MUST NOT be silently split into per-malformation-cause sub-buckets; the threshold pair MUST NOT be silently re-tuned at runtime."
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq description index` command, the four-value bucket-key enum, the two-value aggregation_key enum, the frozen tier function (whitespace-token-count thresholds 7/14, `u`-flag Unicode whitespace), the four flags, exit codes, the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--tier` rule, the empty-capabilities behavior, the `warnings[]` rule with three frozen reason codes, the no-markdown-stripping rule, the no-sub-schema-walking rule, and the explicit "planning aid, not a runtime doc validator, doc-conformance detector, description generator, or customer-facing-claim certifier" callout.
- [x] Update `website/docs/manifest-format.md` with a "Description Word Count Tier Index" subsection explaining how the manifest's per-capability `description` field (M11/M14-derived) drives per-bucket aggregation through the frozen tier function (1-7 tokens → low; 8-14 → medium; 15+ → high; missing/null/non-string/empty-after-trim → unknown), how the index is orthogonal to M37/M38/M39/M40/M41 — M44 measures the unstructured natural-language description string's whitespace-token cardinality rather than a typed structural collection's element count, how malformed values produce a `Warning: ...` rather than silent coercion, that bucket iteration follows the closed-enum order (NOT doc-quality-ranked, NOT doc-completeness-ranked — explicitly a stable-output convention only matching ascending-tier-numeric-span order), that within each bucket capability names follow manifest declared order, that the `--tier` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only, that `description_word_count_tier` is reviewer-aid metadata only and is NOT persisted into the manifest, and that the description word count tier index is read-only and never mutates the manifest.
- [x] `npm test` continues to exit 0; no new dependency added to `package.json` (no markdown parser, no HTML stripper, no readability-score library, no language-detector, no tokenizer beyond Node.js built-in `String.prototype.split`); 28-command CLI surface preserved exactly (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, auth, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, method, output, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, surface, version, help); only the new `tusq description index` command is added; every existing command's stdout, stderr, and exit code is byte-identical pre and post-M44.

#### M44 Key Risks

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| `description_word_count_tier` accidentally persisted into manifest | Silent revocation of approval across capabilities (digest flip cascade), duplicates M30–M43 manifest-mutation risk | Read-only by design: `cmdDescriptionIndex` never calls back into manifest builders or `computeCapabilityDigest`. Smoke test (r) captures manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` pre/post and asserts byte-identity AND asserts no capability has a `description_word_count_tier` field. |
| Markdown / HTML / punctuation stripped before tokenization | Operators expecting raw whitespace-token semantics would see `**bold** _italic_` (3 tokens raw) collapse to `bold italic` (2 tokens stripped), masking the markdown-literal distinction that `M-Description-Markdown-Strip-1` is reserved to surface; downstream tools that hard-code the closed four-value enum tier thresholds would see undocumented tier shifts on markdown-formatted descriptions | Tier function uses naïve `description.trim().split(/\s+/u)` only — markdown, HTML tags, punctuation, and numbers are NOT stripped. SYSTEM_SPEC § M44 and Constraint 37 codify the no-stripping rule. Markdown-aware tokenization is reserved for `M-Description-Markdown-Strip-1`. Smoke case (x) explicitly asserts ``**bold** _italic_ `code` `` tokenizes to 3 tokens (low). |
| Per-language word-count handling (CJK / RTL / no-whitespace scripts) silently grapheme-clustered | A Japanese description (no whitespace) would tokenize to 1 token regardless of grapheme count, and operators expecting per-language word-count parity would see CJK descriptions falsely bucketed as `low`; downstream reviewers reading the Japanese `low` bucket would mis-conclude that the description is sparse | Tier function uses naïve whitespace-only tokenization with the `u` Unicode flag for whitespace recognition only — NO per-language handling, NO grapheme-cluster splitting, NO CJK/RTL adjustments. SYSTEM_SPEC § M44 and Constraint 37 codify the no-per-language rule. Per-language handling is reserved for `M-Description-Per-Language-Word-Count-Tier-Index-1`. The `tusq description index` callout reinforces "naïve whitespace tokenization." |
| Threshold values (`7`, `14`) silently re-tuned at runtime | A capability with 8 tokens (currently `medium`) could be silently re-bucketed to `low` if thresholds drift; reviewer dashboards built against the frozen `low ≤7 / medium 8-14 / high ≥15` rule would silently shift | Threshold pair is hard-coded as integer literals in `classifyDescriptionWordCountTier`; module-init guard `_guardDescriptionWordCountTierBucketKey` does NOT include threshold validation (thresholds are inline literals); SYSTEM_SPEC § M44 and Constraint 37 codify the threshold immutability post-ship. Smoke case (v) explicitly tests boundary values (7→low, 8→medium, 14→medium, 15→high). Threshold change is reserved for `M-Description-Word-Count-Tier-Threshold-Adjustment-1`. |
| Bucket order described as "doc-quality-ranked" or "doc-staleness-blast-radius-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a documentation-quality gauge, leading to false confidence in `low`-bucket capabilities (which may have terse-but-stale descriptions) and false-pessimism on `high`-bucket capabilities (which may have rich-and-current descriptions) | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only matching ascending-tier-numeric-span order (NOT doc-quality-ranked, NOT doc-completeness-ranked, NOT doc-richness-ranked, NOT doc-staleness-blast-radius-ranked, NOT customer-facing-truth-surface ranked); SYSTEM_SPEC § M44 and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatDescriptionWordCountTierIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only." |
| `--tier` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--tier LOW` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about the case-sensitivity contract; `--tier ''` could accidentally match the `unknown` bucket if input validation is sloppy | `--tier` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M43 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown description word count tier:`. Empty-string `--tier ''` exits 1 with `Unknown description word count tier:` (empty string is not in the closed four-value set). Smoke case (f) asserts `--tier LOW` exits 1; smoke case (g) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `description` inserted in the wrong position between commands would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 27-command surface and produce noise in user-facing help output | Alphabetical insertion verified: `confidence` < `description` (`c` (99) < `d` (100) at position 0); `description` < `diff` (`d` (100) = `d`, `e` (101) < `i` (105) at position 1); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., confidence, description, diff, domain, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^description$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 28. |

### M45: Static Capability Output Schema Items Type Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.26 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-27 in `run_79db9c1f34791188` (`turn_99cd4b15e01e46ad`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `9187fa7` from intake charter `intent_1777332389381_27e8` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M44 Key Risks. Read .planning/VISION.md and .planning/ROADMAP.md to select one next testable milestone."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 66–72 (`### Frontend, Design System, And Product UX` under § Input Sources)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Action Execution Policy; M34 cites § Code And API Surface for method extraction; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence and § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth). The section reads: "frontend routes, page components, dashboard layouts, **forms, action menus, modals, tables, and command surfaces**; UI-side permission checks and feature gates; design tokens, CSS variables, Tailwind config, theme files, component libraries, icons, logos, typography, radius, spacing, and motion conventions; empty states, error states, onboarding flows, tooltips, product terminology, and microcopy; user journeys that reveal how backend capabilities are actually exposed in the product." For an array-typed capability response, the JSON-Schema `output_schema.items.type` is the most fundamental structural claim about how the response will be RENDERED in the frontend: an array-typed capability whose `items.type === 'object'` renders in product as a multi-column **table** (each row = one object with named columns); whose `items.type` is a primitive (`string` / `number` / `integer` / `boolean` / `null`) renders as a single-column **list** with text/numeric/boolean cells; whose `items.type` is itself `array` renders as a nested-array tree (the most exotic UI affordance for command surfaces). The per-`output_schema_items_type` capability roll-up answers the operator question: "for the array-typed responses my capabilities return, which item-shape will the frontend dashboard render — a table of objects, a list of strings, a list of numbers, or a nested-array tree?" Cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `object` items-type bucket where action menus on object-rows (each row supports edit/delete/approve actions per the section's "forms, action menus, modals, tables, and command surfaces" framing) most often expose destructive-side-effect or restricted-sensitivity capabilities. Distinct from M42 (which buckets on `output_schema.type` TOP-LEVEL primitive — object vs array vs string vs number vs boolean vs null) and orthogonal to all prior axes: M42 measures the top-level shape of the response; M45 measures, for array-typed responses only, the SHAPE OF EACH ARRAY ELEMENT. M45 is the natural successor of M42 on the response side: M42 says "is this a table, a card, or a primitive?"; M45 says "if it's a table, what's in each row?" A capability with `output_schema.type === 'object'` (M42 `object`) buckets as `not_applicable` under M45 — there is no `items.type` for non-arrays. A capability with `output_schema.type === 'array'` AND `output_schema.items.type === 'object'` (M42 `array` + M45 `object`) is the canonical product-table-of-records render-target. Distinct from M40 (`output_schema.properties` Object.keys length tier — object-shape property cardinality) and M41 (`path` segment count tier — URL-path structural cardinality): M40/M41 measure structural cardinalities of the object/path side; M45 measures the per-array-element JSON-Schema primitive type — a fundamentally different axis class.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 19–32 (`### The Promise`)** — re-cited (cited previously by no shipped milestone as primary; cited here as a structural-anchor for the "tools, skills, and agents that match what's actually true in this product" framing). M45 cites it for the frontend-rendering-paraphrase framing only — the `items.type` is the shape contract that every frontend rendering surface ("brand-matched chat and voice surfaces with a friendly UI tone," "embeddable widgets and command surfaces") presupposes when paraphrasing what an array response looks like to the end user.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed nine-value): `object | array | string | number | integer | boolean | null | not_applicable | unknown`. The seven JSON-Schema 2020-12 core spec primitive type values are the bucket-key values when `output_schema.type === 'array'` AND `items.type` matches the closed primitive set; `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose `output_schema.type !== 'array'` (no `items.type` to evaluate); `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `output_schema` or `items` field. No additional bucket-key values may be introduced (e.g., `tuple`, `union`, `oneOf`, `anyOf`, `mixed`, `compound`) without a separate ROADMAP milestone. Note: unlike M42 (which buckets `integer` as `unknown` because the JSON-Schema 2020-12 spec treats integer as a number subset), M45 does NOT collapse `integer` into `number` — a typed array of integers (`[1, 2, 3]`) is a distinct rendering target from a typed array of arbitrary numbers (`[1.5, 2.7, 3.9]`) because frontend tables format integers (no decimal point) differently from floats; both buckets are first-class.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `items_type | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in any of the seven primitive items-type buckets (object/array/string/number/integer/boolean/null) carry aggregation_key `items_type`; capabilities in the `not_applicable` bucket carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in the `unknown` bucket carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values rather than two so that downstream consumers can distinguish "no items.type to evaluate" (legitimate non-array response) from "could not evaluate" (manifest malformation).
> - **Tier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `output_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `output_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array.
>   - `unknown` if `output_schema.type` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string.
>   - `not_applicable` if `output_schema.type` is a string but not the literal `'array'` (i.e., the response is object / string / number / integer / boolean / null / any other top-level type).
>   - For `output_schema.type === 'array'`:
>     - `unknown` if `output_schema.items` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array.
>     - `unknown` if `output_schema.items.type` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string.
>     - `unknown` if `output_schema.items.type` is a string but not in the closed seven-value JSON-Schema primitive set (`object`, `array`, `string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean`, `null`).
>     - Else: bucket as the literal `output_schema.items.type` value.
>   - The closed nine-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship. Multi-type items (`items: { type: ['object', 'null'] }`) bucket as `unknown` (the literal `items.type` is an array, not a string). Compositional items (`items: { oneOf: [...] }`) without a top-level `type` bucket as `unknown` (no `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf` walking; reserved for `M-Items-Type-OneOf-AnyOf-AllOf-Index-1`). Tuple-style items (`items: [schema1, schema2]`) bucket as `unknown` (the literal `items` is an array, not a plain object; tuple-style is reserved for `M-Items-Type-Tuple-Index-1`). Nested array elements (`items.items.type`) are NOT examined — only the immediate `items.type` is read.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `output_schema_items_type`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M44 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`output_schema`/`output_schema.items` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `output_schema_field_missing`, `output_schema_field_not_object`, `output_schema_items_field_missing_when_type_is_array`, `output_schema_items_field_not_object_when_type_is_array`, `output_schema_items_type_field_missing_or_invalid_when_type_is_array`. The five reasons split the malformation cases at the natural manifest-shape boundary. Importantly: the `not_applicable` bucket (`output_schema.type !== 'array'`) does NOT emit a warning — it is a valid named bucket signaling "this response is non-array, items.type is correctly absent." Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings. No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a UI-rendering-precedence ranking, NOT a frontend-blast-radius ranking, NOT a table-vs-list-priority ranking, NOT a render-complexity ranking, NOT a JSON-Schema-spec-precedence ranking, NOT a customer-facing-render-surface ranking): `object → array → string → number → integer → boolean → null → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional reading order of JSON-Schema 2020-12 spec primitive enumeration with `object` first (canonical record-row case), followed by structural arrays, followed by primitives in JSON-Schema-spec enumeration order, with `not_applicable` and `unknown` appended last. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--items-type` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `OBJECT`, `Array`, or any value not in the closed nine-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown output schema items type: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M44 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for output schema items type: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M44): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; `tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index` all byte-identical pre/post.
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `output_schema_items_type` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 28 → 29 commands. New top-level noun `items` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `input` and `method` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, method, output, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, surface, version`; `input` < `items` because at position 0 `i` = `i`, at position 1 `n` (110) < `t` (116); `items` < `method` because at position 0 `i` (105) < `m` (109)). Insertion sequence: ..., `examples`, `input`, `items`, `method`, `output`, `path`, ..., `version`, `help`. The fresh noun `items` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `response`) preserves the M31–M44 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `items` (plural noun matching the JSON-Schema `output_schema.items.type` field path) is intentional and consistent with M42's `response` (matching `output_schema` semantics) and M43's `request` (matching `input_schema` semantics) — the new noun matches the manifest sub-field whose values it aggregates over.
> - **Result-array field name** `items_types` (plural noun, distinct from M42's `types`, M43's `sources`, M44's `tiers`).
> - **Eval scenario**: `output-schema-items-type-index-determinism` added; eval harness 35 → ≥36 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 38** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M45 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `output_schema.items.type` for array-typed responses; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime response payloads against the declared `items.type`, does NOT generate frontend table/list components, does NOT certify rendering completeness, does NOT collapse `integer` into `number` (both are first-class buckets in M45 even though M42 collapses integer to unknown), does NOT walk `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf` items compositions (reserved for `M-Items-Type-OneOf-AnyOf-AllOf-Index-1`), does NOT walk tuple-style items arrays (reserved for `M-Items-Type-Tuple-Index-1`), does NOT walk multi-type items (`items.type: ['object', 'null']` buckets as `unknown`; reserved for `M-Items-Type-Multi-Type-Index-1`), does NOT walk nested-array element typing (`items.items.type` is NOT examined; reserved for `M-Items-Type-Nested-Array-Index-1`), does NOT walk `items.properties` for object-typed items (object-property aggregation reserved for `M-Items-Object-Property-Count-Tier-Index-1`).
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Items-Type-OneOf-AnyOf-AllOf-Index-1` (compositional items schemas), `M-Items-Type-Tuple-Index-1` (tuple-style items arrays), `M-Items-Type-Multi-Type-Index-1` (multi-type items), `M-Items-Type-Nested-Array-Index-1` (nested-array element typing via `items.items.type`), `M-Items-Object-Property-Count-Tier-Index-1` (per-element object property cardinality), `M-Items-Type-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Items-Type-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest items.type and published API docs), `M-Items-Type-Runtime-Conformance-1` (runtime response-array element-type conformance detection), `M-Items-Type-Frontend-Render-Mapping-1` (mapping items.type → frontend component recommendation).

Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardOutputSchemaItemsTypeBucketKey` and `_guardOutputSchemaItemsTypeAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M44 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.

Returns `'not_applicable'` if `outputSchema.type` is a string but not the literal `'array'`. For `outputSchema.type === 'array'`: returns `'unknown'` if `outputSchema.items` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'unknown'` if `outputSchema.items.type` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string; returns `'unknown'` if `outputSchema.items.type` is a string but not in the closed seven-value JSON-Schema primitive set; else returns the literal `outputSchema.items.type` value. The closed nine-value bucket-key set is immutable; integer is NOT collapsed into number (distinct from M42).

Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`object → array → string → number → integer → boolean → null → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Field name is `items_types` (plural categorical, distinct from M42's `types`, M43's `sources`, M44's `tiers`).

- [x] Add Constraint 38 to SYSTEM_SPEC.md Constraints section: "`tusq items index` is a planning aid that surfaces the JSON-Schema `output_schema.items.type` for array-typed capability responses and the cross-axis side-effect/sensitivity exposure of each items-type bucket. It does NOT execute capability invocations at runtime, does NOT validate runtime response payloads against the declared `items.type`, does NOT generate frontend table/list components or component-library recommendations (mapping reserved for `M-Items-Type-Frontend-Render-Mapping-1`), does NOT certify rendering completeness, does NOT alter M42's top-level `output_schema.type` bucketing rules, does NOT walk compositional items schemas (`oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf`; reserved for `M-Items-Type-OneOf-AnyOf-AllOf-Index-1`), does NOT walk tuple-style items arrays (reserved for `M-Items-Type-Tuple-Index-1`), does NOT walk multi-type items (`items.type: ['object', 'null']` buckets as `unknown`; reserved for `M-Items-Type-Multi-Type-Index-1`), does NOT walk nested-array element typing (`items.items.type`; reserved for `M-Items-Type-Nested-Array-Index-1`), does NOT walk per-element object property cardinality (reserved for `M-Items-Object-Property-Count-Tier-Index-1`), does NOT collapse `integer` into `number` (distinct from M42 — both are first-class buckets in M45), does NOT persist `output_schema_items_type` into `tusq.manifest.json`, and does NOT compute statistical aggregates. Subsequent milestones ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts and fresh re-approval expectations. The nine-value `output_schema_items_type` bucket-key enum (`object | array | string | number | integer | boolean | null | not_applicable | unknown`), the three-value `aggregation_key` enum (`items_type | not_applicable | unknown`), and the five-value warning reason-code enum are frozen; any addition is a material governance event. The `--items-type` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case filter values exit 1 with `Unknown output schema items type:`. Malformed `output_schema`/`items` values are bucketed as `unknown` and emit a `Warning: ...` to stderr (human) or `warnings[]` (`--json`); the `not_applicable` bucket (`output_schema.type !== 'array'`) is a valid named bucket and emits NO warning."
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq items index` command, the nine-value bucket-key enum, the three-value aggregation_key enum, the frozen tier function, the four flags, exit codes, the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--items-type` rule, the empty-capabilities behavior, the `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, the integer-not-collapsed-to-number rule, the no-oneOf-anyOf-allOf rule, and the explicit "planning aid, not a runtime response validator, frontend component generator, or rendering completeness certifier" callout.
- [x] Update `website/docs/manifest-format.md` with an "Output Schema Items Type Index" subsection explaining how the manifest's per-capability `output_schema.items.type` field drives per-bucket aggregation through the frozen tier function, how the index is orthogonal to M42 — M45 measures the per-element shape of array-typed responses while M42 measures the top-level shape, how malformed values produce a `Warning: ...` rather than silent coercion, that `not_applicable` is a valid named bucket for non-array responses (NOT an error), that `integer` is NOT collapsed into `number` (distinct from M42's collapse-rule), that bucket iteration follows the closed-enum order (NOT UI-rendering-precedence-ranked — explicitly a stable-output convention only), that within each bucket capability names follow manifest declared order, that the `--items-type` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only, that `output_schema_items_type` is reviewer-aid metadata only and is NOT persisted into the manifest, and that the items type index is read-only and never mutates the manifest.
- [x] `npm test` continues to exit 0; no new dependency added to `package.json` (no JSON Schema validator, no frontend renderer, no component library detector); 29-command CLI surface preserved exactly (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, auth, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, items, method, output, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, surface, version, help); only the new `tusq items index` command is added; every existing command's stdout, stderr, and exit code is byte-identical pre and post-M45.

#### M45 Key Risks

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| `output_schema_items_type` accidentally persisted into manifest | Silent revocation of approval (digest flip cascade), duplicates M30–M44 manifest-mutation risk | Read-only by design: `cmdItemsIndex` never calls back into manifest builders or `computeCapabilityDigest`. Smoke test (u) captures manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` pre/post and asserts byte-identity AND asserts no capability has an `output_schema_items_type` field. |
| Integer silently collapsed into number bucket (mirroring M42) | M42 collapses `integer` to `unknown` (since JSON-Schema spec treats integer as a number subset); a naive M45 implementer reading the M42 precedent could similarly collapse `integer` items.type into `number` or `unknown`, masking the integer-formatted-table-cell distinction (a column of integers renders without a decimal point; a column of arbitrary numbers renders with one) | M45 explicitly does NOT collapse integer into number — both are first-class buckets in the closed nine-value enum. SYSTEM_SPEC § M45 and Constraint 38 codify the no-collapse rule. Smoke case (e) explicitly tests an array-of-integers fixture and asserts the bucket is `integer` not `number` and not `unknown`. The eval scenario asserts the integer-not-collapsed invariant. |
| `not_applicable` bucket framed as a malformation rather than a valid named bucket | Reviewers reading the index could mistake `not_applicable` capabilities (legitimately non-array responses) for malformed manifests, leading to false-positive review escalations on object-typed / primitive-typed responses that are correctly described | `not_applicable` is a valid named bucket with its own aggregation_key value (`not_applicable`), distinct from `unknown` (which carries aggregation_key `unknown`). SYSTEM_SPEC § M45 codifies the three-value aggregation_key enum. The `not_applicable` bucket emits NO warning (only `unknown` triggers warnings). The CLI callout text and docs both clarify "non-array responses bucket as not_applicable; this is not an error." Smoke case (a) and (c) explicitly test the not_applicable bucket on the canonical fixture (cap2 and cap3 both bucket as not_applicable with no warning). |
| Compositional items schemas (`oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf`) silently walked | A naive implementer could read JSON Schema 2020-12 spec and decide that `items: { oneOf: [{type: 'object'}, {type: 'string'}] }` should bucket as `mixed` or `oneOf`, silently expanding the closed enum without authorization | Compositional items bucket as `unknown` with reason `output_schema_items_type_field_missing_or_invalid_when_type_is_array` (the literal `items.type` field is missing). SYSTEM_SPEC § M45 and Constraint 38 codify the no-walking rule. Compositional aggregation is reserved for the deferred `M-Items-Type-OneOf-AnyOf-AllOf-Index-1` successor. Smoke case (h) tests the compositional-items → unknown path. |
| Bucket order described as "UI-render-precedence-ranked" or "frontend-blast-radius-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a UI-rendering-priority gauge, leading to false confidence that `object`-bucket capabilities are "rendered first" or "render the most important UI" | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only matching JSON-Schema 2020-12 spec primitive enumeration order with `object` first (canonical record-row case); SYSTEM_SPEC § M45 and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatOutputSchemaItemsTypeIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only, NOT UI-render-precedence-ranked." |
| `--items-type` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--items-type OBJECT` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about case-sensitivity contract | `--items-type` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M44 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown output schema items type:`. Empty-string `--items-type ''` exits 1 with `Unknown output schema items type:`. Smoke case (i) asserts `--items-type OBJECT` exits 1; smoke case (j) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `items` inserted in the wrong position would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 28-command surface | Alphabetical insertion verified: `input` < `items` (`i` = `i` at position 0; `n` (110) < `t` (116) at position 1); `items` < `method` (`i` (105) < `m` (109) at position 0); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., examples, input, items, method, output, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^items$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 29. |

### M46: Static Capability Output Schema additionalProperties Strictness Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.27 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-27 in `run_7c4036f0eba4cde3` (`turn_c1bdc2ccb3a73e68`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `2df5438` from intake charter `intent_1777339528822_74c3` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M45 Key Risks. Read .planning/VISION.md and .planning/ROADMAP.md to select one next testable milestone."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 164–173 (`### Tools` under § What We Output)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Action Execution Policy; M34 cites § Code And API Surface for method extraction; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence and § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX). The section reads: "Tools are the lowest-level AI-callable units. They are generated from approved capabilities and include: **strict schemas**, normalized errors, dry-run and confirmation support, IAM context requirements, audit metadata, provenance back to source." The "strict schemas" framing maps directly onto the per-capability `output_schema.additionalProperties` boolean — for an object-typed capability response, `additionalProperties === false` declares a STRICT schema (the LLM/caller may NOT introduce response fields not enumerated in `properties[]`); `additionalProperties === true` declares a PERMISSIVE schema (unspecified response fields are tolerated). This is the most fundamental structural claim a capability makes about whether its tool-generated schema is enforceable as written or implicitly open-ended. The per-`output_schema_strictness` capability roll-up answers the operator question: "for the object-typed responses my capabilities return, which of my generated tool schemas are STRICT (enforceable contract), which are PERMISSIVE (unspecified fields tolerated), and which capabilities respond with non-object top-level shapes (no `additionalProperties` to evaluate)?" Cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `permissive` strictness bucket where destructive or restricted-sensitivity capabilities expose unspecified-field tolerance — a permissive schema on a destructive action means the generated tool may accept caller-introduced fields the manifest never enumerated, blunting the manifest's reviewability contract. Distinct from M45 (which buckets on `output_schema.items.type` for array-typed responses) and orthogonal to all prior axes: M40 measures `output_schema.properties` cardinality on object responses (how many top-level keys?); M42 measures `output_schema.type` top-level primitive (what shape at all?); M45 measures `output_schema.items.type` per-element shape on array-typed responses (what's in each row?); M46 measures, for object-typed responses only, whether the response schema is closed (`additionalProperties === false`) or open (`additionalProperties === true`) — a fundamentally different axis class (boolean strictness gate, not numeric tier, not categorical primitive type, not categorical locus class).
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 19–32 (`### The Promise`)** — re-cited (cited previously by M44/M45 as a structural-anchor only; cited here for the "AI tools with strict schemas" framing only — the strict-schemas claim in § The Promise is the public-facing form of the strictness contract that § Tools encodes structurally as `additionalProperties === false`).
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `strict | permissive | not_applicable | unknown`. `strict` denotes `output_schema.type === 'object'` AND `output_schema.additionalProperties === false` (the JSON-Schema closed-schema declaration). `permissive` denotes `output_schema.type === 'object'` AND `output_schema.additionalProperties === true` (the JSON-Schema open-schema declaration). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose `output_schema.type !== 'object'` (no top-level `additionalProperties` to evaluate — the response is array / string / number / integer / boolean / null / any other top-level type). `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `output_schema` or whose `output_schema.additionalProperties` is missing/null/non-boolean (e.g., `additionalProperties` as a schema object — schema-as-additionalProperties walking is reserved for the deferred `M-Strictness-Schema-As-AdditionalProperties-1` successor). No additional bucket-key values may be introduced (e.g., `closed`, `open`, `mixed`, `partial`, `schema`, `false_strict`, `true_open`) without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `strictness | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in either of the two strictness buckets (strict/permissive) carry aggregation_key `strictness`; capabilities in the `not_applicable` bucket carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in the `unknown` bucket carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values rather than two so that downstream consumers can distinguish "no additionalProperties to evaluate" (legitimate non-object response) from "could not evaluate" (manifest malformation).
> - **Tier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `output_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `output_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array.
>   - `unknown` if `output_schema.type` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string.
>   - `not_applicable` if `output_schema.type` is a string but not the literal `'object'` (i.e., the response is array / string / number / integer / boolean / null / any other top-level type).
>   - For `output_schema.type === 'object'`:
>     - `unknown` if `output_schema.additionalProperties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`.
>     - `unknown` if `output_schema.additionalProperties` is not a boolean (including the schema-object case `additionalProperties: { type: 'string' }`; reserved for `M-Strictness-Schema-As-AdditionalProperties-1`).
>     - `'strict'` if `output_schema.additionalProperties === false`.
>     - `'permissive'` if `output_schema.additionalProperties === true`.
>   - The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship. The boolean-only contract is intentional: JSON-Schema 2020-12 allows `additionalProperties` as either a boolean or a schema; M46 reads only the boolean form. Schema-as-additionalProperties (e.g., `{ "type": "string" }` constraining all unspecified fields to strings) buckets as `unknown` (reserved for `M-Strictness-Schema-As-AdditionalProperties-1`). The `strict`/`permissive` distinction is NOT a security/blast-radius/risk ranking — it is a structural claim about whether the schema enumerates a closed key set.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `output_schema_strictness`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M45 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`output_schema`/`output_schema.additionalProperties` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `output_schema_field_missing`, `output_schema_field_not_object`, `output_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `output_schema_additional_properties_missing_when_type_is_object`, `output_schema_additional_properties_not_boolean_when_type_is_object`. The `not_applicable` bucket (`output_schema.type !== 'object'`) does NOT emit a warning — it is a valid named bucket signaling "non-object response, additionalProperties is correctly absent." Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings. No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a security-blast-radius ranking, NOT a strictness-precedence ranking, NOT a contract-quality ranking, NOT a tool-generation-safety ranking, NOT a JSON-Schema-spec-precedence ranking, NOT a customer-facing-strictness-claim ranking): `strict → permissive → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional reading order of "closed-then-open" boolean enumeration (false before true in JavaScript falsy-first convention) with `not_applicable` and `unknown` appended last. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--strictness` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `STRICT`, `Permissive`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown output schema strictness: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M45 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for output schema strictness: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M45): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; `tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index` all byte-identical pre/post.
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `output_schema_strictness` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 29 → 30 commands. New top-level noun `strictness` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `sensitivity` and `surface` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, items, method, output, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, surface, version`; `sensitivity` < `strictness` because at position 0 `s` = `s`, at position 1 `e` (101) < `t` (116); `strictness` < `surface` because at position 0 `s` = `s`, at position 1 `t` (116) < `u` (117)). Insertion sequence: ..., `response`, `sensitivity`, `strictness`, `surface`, `version`, `help`. The fresh noun `strictness` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `response` or `output`) preserves the M31–M45 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `strictness` (abstract noun matching the JSON-Schema "strict-vs-permissive" contract distinction) is intentional and consistent with M44's `description` (matching the manifest description-field semantics) and M45's `items` (matching the JSON-Schema items-field semantics) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over.
> - **Result-array field name** `strictnesses` (plural noun, distinct from M42's `types`, M43's `sources`, M44's `tiers`, M45's `items_types`).
> - **Eval scenario**: `output-schema-strictness-index-determinism` added; eval harness 36 → ≥37 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 39** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M46 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `output_schema.additionalProperties` strictness for object-typed responses; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime response payloads against the declared strictness, does NOT generate strict-schema enforcement middleware, does NOT certify schema enforceability, does NOT walk schema-as-additionalProperties (reserved for `M-Strictness-Schema-As-AdditionalProperties-1`), does NOT walk per-property additionalProperties under nested object properties (reserved for `M-Strictness-Per-Property-1`), does NOT walk items.additionalProperties for array-of-object items (reserved for `M-Strictness-Items-Object-1`), does NOT bucket on `input_schema.additionalProperties` strictness (reserved for `M-Strictness-Input-Schema-1`).
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Strictness-Input-Schema-1` (`input_schema.additionalProperties` strictness as a distinct request-side axis), `M-Strictness-Items-Object-1` (`output_schema.items.additionalProperties` for array-of-object items), `M-Strictness-Schema-As-AdditionalProperties-1` (when `additionalProperties` is itself a schema object rather than a boolean), `M-Strictness-Per-Property-1` (per-nested-property strictness traversal), `M-Strictness-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Strictness-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest strictness and published API docs), `M-Strictness-Runtime-Conformance-1` (runtime response-payload-vs-strictness conformance detection).

Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardOutputSchemaStrictnessBucketKey` and `_guardOutputSchemaStrictnessAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M45 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyOutputSchemaStrictness(outputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `outputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array, AND for `outputSchema.type` that is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` if `outputSchema.type` is a string but not the literal `'object'`. For `outputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `outputSchema.additionalProperties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`; returns `'unknown'` if `outputSchema.additionalProperties` is not a boolean (the schema-object case buckets as `unknown`); returns `'strict'` if `outputSchema.additionalProperties === false`; returns `'permissive'` if `outputSchema.additionalProperties === true`. The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable; the boolean-only contract is intentional.
- [x] Add `cmdStrictness` dispatcher and `cmdStrictnessIndex(args)` command handler in `src/cli.js`. The handler MUST: (a) call `parseStrictnessIndexArgs` first; (b) detect `--out` path validity (rejected: paths under `.tusq/`, paths in non-existent or non-writable parent directories) BEFORE reading the manifest, with stderr `--out path must not be inside .tusq/` or `--out parent directory does not exist or is not writable` and exit 1, empty stdout; (c) read and parse the manifest from `--manifest <path>` (default `tusq.manifest.json` in cwd) with stderr `Manifest file not found: <path>` for missing, `Failed to parse manifest JSON: <message>` for malformed, `Manifest is missing capabilities array` for missing/non-array `capabilities` field, each exit 1 with empty stdout; (d) apply case-sensitive `--strictness <value>` filter against the closed four-value enum, with stderr `Unknown output schema strictness: <value>` and exit 1 for any value not in the enum; (e) build the index via `buildOutputSchemaStrictnessIndex(manifest)`; (f) emit per-capability malformed warnings to stderr in human mode (one `Warning: capability '<name>' has malformed output_schema strictness (<reason>)` per malformed capability) and to `warnings[]` in `--json` mode; (g) format via `formatOutputSchemaStrictnessIndex` (human) or `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` (`--json`).
- [x] Add `parseStrictnessIndexArgs(args)` accepting exactly four flags: `--strictness <value>`, `--manifest <path>`, `--out <path>`, `--json`. Reject any unknown flag with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>` and exit 1, empty stdout. Reject `--strictness` with no value (stderr `--strictness requires a value`), `--manifest` with no value, `--out` with no value, each exit 1.
- [x] Add `buildOutputSchemaStrictnessIndex(manifest, options = {})` returning `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, strictnesses, warnings }`. Iteration: for each capability in `manifest.capabilities[]` (preserving manifest declared order), compute `bucket = classifyOutputSchemaStrictness(capability.output_schema)`; if bucket is `'unknown'`, append a warning entry of the form `{ capability: <name>, reason: <one of five frozen reason codes> }`. Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`strict → permissive → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Each per-bucket entry contains the frozen 8 fields (`output_schema_strictness`, `aggregation_key` ('strictness' for the strict/permissive buckets, 'not_applicable' for not_applicable, 'unknown' for unknown), `capability_count`, `capabilities[]` of capability names in manifest order, `approved_count` (count of `approved === true`), `gated_count` (count of `approved !== true`), `has_destructive_side_effect` (true iff any capability in bucket has `side_effect_class === 'destructive'`), `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` (true iff any capability in bucket has `sensitivity_class === 'restricted'` or `sensitivity_class === 'confidential'`)). `warnings[]` is always present (empty `[]` when no malformed capabilities) for `--json` shape stability. Field name is `strictnesses` (plural, distinct from M42's `types`, M43's `sources`, M44's `tiers`, M45's `items_types`).

Bucket order is deterministic stable-output ordering only (NOT security-blast-radius-ranked, NOT strictness-precedence-ranked, NOT contract-quality-ranked, NOT tool-generation-safety-ranked).` callout at the bottom; `Bucket rule: strict (additionalProperties === false) | permissive (additionalProperties === true) | not_applicable (top-level type !== 'object') | unknown (malformed output_schema or non-boolean additionalProperties).` line; `Bucket order: strict → permissive → not_applicable → unknown` line.

(v) empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, strictnesses: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (w) malformed-`output_schema`/non-boolean-additionalProperties capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed output_schema strictness (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all five frozen reason codes (`output_schema_field_missing`, `output_schema_field_not_object`, `output_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `output_schema_additional_properties_missing_when_type_is_object`, `output_schema_additional_properties_not_boolean_when_type_is_object`);
