CLI help text for `tusq obligation index` explicitly states "this index reports firstKey ∈ input_schema.required[] membership, distinct from M49's primitive-type classification (`tusq signature index`)." Smoke case (a) verifies both axes on the canonical fixture — three capabilities discriminate cleanly across both. |
| `optional` bucket misread as "no required parameters" rather than "first property is not required" | Operator sees a capability in the `optional` bucket and assumes the entire input schema has zero required parameters; in fact only the FIRST property is checked | M50 measures ONLY the FIRST property's required-status. A capability with `properties = {a: {...}, b: {...}}, required = ['b']` buckets as `optional` (firstKey `a` ∉ required[]) even though the schema has a required parameter. CLI help, SYSTEM_SPEC § M50, and the format callout all explicitly say "first property's required-status, not the capability's overall required-count." The all-property required-status distribution is reserved for `M-Obligation-All-Properties-Required-Status-Index-1`. |
| Missing `input_schema.required` bucketed as `unknown` instead of `optional` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid capabilities that simply don't declare any required fields, masking real malformations | Classifier rule explicitly treats `input_schema.required` missing/`null`/`undefined` as `[]` for the membership check, returning `'optional'` (no warning) when the firstKey is not present. Only `required` PRESENT but not-an-array (or array with non-string element) returns `'unknown'`. Smoke case (t) asserts a synthetic fixture with `required` field absent buckets as `optional`. |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | If the implementer sorts keys or uses Map iteration with re-ordered keys, the FIRST property selected may differ across runs, breaking byte-identity | The classifier MUST use `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` directly; MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence; MUST rely on JSON.parse's insertion-order-preserving behavior. Determinism eval scenario explicitly verifies byte-identical output across three runs on a fixture with `properties` keys ordered `{c, a, b}` and `required: ['a']` to confirm the selected first-key is `c` (bucketed as `optional`), not the sorted `a` (which would have been `required`). |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid array-typed input capabilities, masking real malformations | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) when `input_schema.type` is a string other than `'object'`; only missing-or-non-string `type` returns `'unknown'`. Smoke case (matching M49 precedent) includes a synthetic fixture with `input_schema={type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}` asserting `not_applicable` with no warning. |
| Bucket order described as "runtime-execution-safety-ranked" or "action-policy-precedence-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a runtime-policy gauge, leading to false confidence that `required`-bucket capabilities are "safer to direct-execute" or "lower-risk" than `optional`-bucket capabilities | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only; SYSTEM_SPEC § M50, Constraint 43, and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyRequiredStatusIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only, NOT runtime-execution-safety-ranked, NOT call-site-strictness-ranked, NOT destructive-action-priority-ranked, NOT action-policy-precedence-ranked." Action-policy crossref is reserved for `M-Obligation-Action-Policy-Crossref-1`. |
| `--status` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--status REQUIRED` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about case-sensitivity contract | `--status` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M49 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property required status:`. Empty-string `--status ''` exits 1. Smoke case (f) asserts `--status REQUIRED` exits 1; smoke case (g) asserts `--status Optional` exits 1; smoke case (h) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `obligation` inserted in the wrong position would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 33-command surface | Alphabetical insertion verified: `method` < `obligation` (`m` (109) < `o` (111) at position 0); `obligation` < `output` (`o` = `o` at position 0; `b` (98) < `u` (117) at position 1); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., items, method, obligation, output, parameter, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^obligation$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 34. |
| Action-execution-risk score derived in scope creep | An implementer could read the `optional` + destructive cross-axis pair as a natural place to compute a derived risk score and emit it inline | M50 emits ONLY the per-bucket `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` boolean flags in the existing 8-field entry shape; it does NOT emit a derived risk score. The action-policy crossref is explicitly deferred to `M-Obligation-Action-Policy-Crossref-1`. SYSTEM_SPEC § M50 and Constraint 43 codify this scope boundary. |

### M51: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Source Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.32 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_c39bd102a520411b` (`turn_400dc74e4496c4df`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `14d8f85` from intake charter `intent_1777367619111_9934` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M50: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Required Status Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.31 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 175–185 (`### Skills`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy). The section reads: "Skills group tools into domain-level packs such as: Billing operations, Customer support, Admin operations, User and organization management, Project or workflow management. Skills are V1 outputs. They should be useful to humans, MCP clients, generated widgets, and later domain agents." A skill is the orchestration layer that composes individual capability calls into domain-level workflows; the per-`input_schema_first_property_source` capability roll-up answers the skill-author-recognizable question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, where in the HTTP request anatomy does the FIRST input parameter come from — URL path (entity-anchored: the skill must already hold an entity identifier), request body (state-mutation payload: the skill must construct a structured object), URL query string (filter/list shape: the skill is querying a collection), HTTP header (transport-context: tenant/auth/idempotency-key conveyance), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — the capability has no first property to classify a source for), or unknown (malformed)?" A skill that primarily composes `path`-bucket capabilities is doing entity-scoped operations (refund THIS invoice, suspend THIS account); a skill that primarily composes `request_body`-bucket capabilities is doing payload-mutation operations (create A NEW user with this data); a skill that primarily composes `query`-bucket capabilities is doing list-shaped operations (list invoices matching THIS filter). The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `path` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose first input slot is path-bound exposes a "URL-anchored irreversible action" risk that skill-composition reviewers MUST surface — the URL itself names the entity that will be irreversibly mutated. Distinct from M43 on the SAME `input_schema.properties[*].source` field but on a different aggregation axis: M43 reads ALL properties' source values and computes a UNION (capability-wide, six categorical buckets `path | request_body | query | header | mixed | none | unknown` where `mixed` denotes multi-locus capabilities and `none` denotes zero-property capabilities); M51 reads ONLY `input_schema.properties[firstKey].source` (the source of the FIRST property at Object.keys insertion-order index 0). M51 cannot produce a `mixed` value because index 0 is a single property; M51 emits `not_applicable` (rather than M43's `none`) when there is no first property to classify, paralleling the M48/M49/M50 first-property-axis convention. Distinct from M49 on the SAME `input_schema.properties[<firstKey>]` object (M49 reads `.type`; M51 reads `.source` — different fields entirely). Distinct from M50 on the SAME firstKey (M50 measures `firstKey ∈ input_schema.required[]` membership; M51 measures `properties[firstKey].source` value — different value space entirely). Distinct from M48 (output-side first-property type, no `source` field on output properties). The first milestone to use § Skills as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 218–227 (`### Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the embeddable-widget framing item — M51's first-property-source bucketing aligns with how widget generators decide what kind of input control to render for a tool's PRIMARY input slot: a `path`-bound first property typically renders as an entity-picker; a `request_body`-bound first property typically renders as a structured form; a `query`-bound first property typically renders as a filter input; a `header`-bound first property typically renders as a hidden context-injector. Re-cited only — M51's primary source is § Skills.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed six-value): `path | request_body | query | header | not_applicable | unknown`. The four locus values are the canonical HTTP request anatomy locations (URL path, request body, URL query string, HTTP headers) that M11/M14 emit as `source` strings on each `input_schema.properties[*]` entry — same closed four-value source set as M43. `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — semantically valid cases where there is no first input property to classify a source for, paralleling M49/M50's `not_applicable` semantics for the same shape. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), or `properties[firstKey]` lacking a valid `.source` field (missing/null/non-string OR string-but-not-in-the-closed-four-value-set). The closed six-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship. Note: M51 has NO `mixed` bucket (Object.keys index 0 returns a single property, so the union-of-loci concept does not apply) and NO `none` bucket (M43's `none` corresponds semantically to M51's `not_applicable` — empty `properties` means no first property to classify).
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `source | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the four locus buckets (`path`, `request_body`, `query`, `header`) carry aggregation_key `source`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48/M49/M50 first-property-axis pattern: a single semantic-aggregation key for the "real" buckets, and two reserved keys for the absent-target and malformed cases.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` (e.g., `'array'`, `'string'`, `'number'`, etc.) — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid (zero-property object input has no first property to classify); no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, OR `firstVal.source` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-string, OR `firstVal.source` is a string but not in the closed four-value set `{'path', 'request_body', 'query', 'header'}`, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid`). Otherwise return `firstVal.source` verbatim.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order via `JSON.parse`. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_source`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M50 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-`source` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid`. The `not_applicable` bucket (non-object input or zero-property object) does NOT emit a warning — it is a valid named bucket signaling "no first input property to classify a source for." The `path`/`request_body`/`query`/`header` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings. No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a security-blast-radius ranking, NOT a workflow-criticality ranking, NOT a permission-sensitivity ranking, NOT an HTTP-spec-precedence ranking, NOT a skill-composition-priority ranking, NOT a widget-render-precedence ranking): `path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional HTTP request anatomy reading order (URL path before request body before query string before headers; not_applicable and unknown last) and is identical to M43's first-four locus ordering. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--source` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `PATH`, `Request_Body`, or any value not in the closed six-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property source: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M50 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property source: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M50): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 21 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_source` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 34 → 35 commands. New top-level noun `binding` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `auth` and `confidence` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version`; `auth` < `binding` because at position 0 `a` (97) < `b` (98); `binding` < `confidence` because at position 0 `b` (98) < `c` (99)). Insertion sequence: ..., `approve`, `auth`, `binding`, `confidence`, `description`, ..., `version`, `help`. The fresh noun `binding` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `request`, `signature`, or `obligation`) preserves the M31–M50 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `binding` (singular abstract noun matching the conceptual "where does the first input property bind in the HTTP request anatomy?" axis) is intentional and is distinct from `request` (M43, capability-wide locus union with `mixed` value), `signature` (M49, input_schema.properties[firstKey].type), `obligation` (M50, input_schema.properties[firstKey] required-status), `input` (M39, input_schema.required count tier — cardinality), and `parameter` (M47, input_schema.properties count tier) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter HTTP-request-anatomy binding location.
> - **Result-array field name** `first_property_sources` (plural categorical with closed bucket-key family; the input-side scope is carried by the per-bucket field name `input_schema_first_property_source` rather than the result-array field name; matches M48's `first_property_types` plural categorical naming convention verbatim).
> - **Eval scenario**: `input-schema-first-property-source-index-determinism` added; eval harness 41 → ≥42 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 44** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M51 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].source` HTTP-request-anatomy locus classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime request payloads against the declared first-property source, does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property locus vs runtime request shape, does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M39's `input_schema.required` count bucketing rules, does NOT alter M43's `input_schema` capability-wide primary-parameter-source bucketing rules (M43 reads ALL properties' sources and computes a union; M51 reads ONLY the first property's source — the two milestones are orthogonal axes on the related field), does NOT alter M47's `input_schema.properties` cardinality bucketing rules, does NOT alter M48's output-side first-property type bucketing rules, does NOT alter M49's input-side first-property primitive type bucketing rules, does NOT alter M50's input-side first-property required-status bucketing rules, does NOT walk per-property source beyond the FIRST property (the all-property source distribution is M43's capability-wide axis and the per-property fine-grained distribution is reserved for `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property source under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].source` (reserved for `M-Binding-Nested-Property-Source-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property source (reserved for `M-Binding-Output-First-Property-Source-Index-1`), does NOT extend the closed four-value source set to include `cookie` (reserved for `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1`) or `file`/`multipart`/`form-data` (reserved for `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1`), does NOT generate downstream SDK call-site signature definitions (reserved for `M-Binding-SDK-Generator-1`), does NOT detect runtime payload conformance against the declared first-property source (reserved for `M-Binding-Runtime-Conformance-1`), does NOT compute a derived widget-render-control type by cross-referencing source with type (reserved for `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1`), does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_source` into `tusq.manifest.json`, does NOT compute statistical aggregates.
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1` (per-property fine-grained source distribution rather than capability-level first-property locus or capability-wide union), `M-Binding-Nested-Property-Source-Index-1` (recursive walk of nested-object property source under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].source`), `M-Binding-Output-First-Property-Source-Index-1` (output-side analog when output_schema property entries declare a `source` field — currently rare in scanner output), `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1` (extend the closed four-value source set to include `cookie`), `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1` (extend the closed four-value source set to include `file`/`multipart`/`form-data`), `M-Binding-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Binding-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest first-property source and published API/OpenAPI/SDK signature docs), `M-Binding-Runtime-Conformance-1` (runtime request-payload-vs-first-property-source conformance detection), `M-Binding-SDK-Generator-1` (downstream SDK call-site signature-generator that consumes M51's index as input — explicitly out of M51's scope, which is read-only reviewer aid only), `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1` (cross-reference first-property source against first-property type to compute a derived widget-render-control type — explicitly out of M51's scope).

- [x] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `path | request_body | query | header | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_VALUE_SET` (frozen `Set` of the closed four-value `{path, request_body, query, header}` accepted property-`source` values), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `source | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M50 input-schema-first-property-required-status constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M50 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertySource(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object OR `firstVal.source` is not a string OR `firstVal.source` is a string not in `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_VALUE_SET`; otherwise returns `firstVal.source` verbatim.
- [x] Add `cmdBinding` dispatcher and `cmdBindingIndex(args)` command handler in `src/cli.js`. The handler MUST: (a) call `parseBindingIndexArgs` first; (b) detect `--out` path validity (rejected: paths under `.tusq/`, paths in non-existent or non-writable parent directories) BEFORE reading the manifest, with stderr `--out path must not be inside .tusq/` or `--out parent directory does not exist or is not writable` and exit 1, empty stdout; (c) read and parse the manifest from `--manifest <path>` (default `tusq.manifest.json` in cwd) with stderr `Manifest file not found: <path>` for missing, `Failed to parse manifest JSON: <message>` for malformed, `Manifest is missing capabilities array` for missing/non-array `capabilities` field, each exit 1 with empty stdout; (d) apply case-sensitive `--source <value>` filter against the closed six-value enum, with stderr `Unknown input schema first property source: <value>` and exit 1 for any value not in the enum; (e) build the index via `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndex(manifest)`; (f) emit per-capability malformed warnings to stderr in human mode (one `Warning: capability '<name>' has malformed input_schema first property source (<reason>)` per malformed capability) and to `warnings[]` in `--json` mode; (g) format via `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndex` (human) or `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` (`--json`).
- [x] Add `parseBindingIndexArgs(args)` accepting exactly four flags: `--source <value>`, `--manifest <path>`, `--out <path>`, `--json`. Reject any unknown flag with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>` and exit 1, empty stdout. Reject `--source` with no value (stderr `--source requires a value`), `--manifest` with no value, `--out` with no value, each exit 1.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndex(manifest, options = {})` returning `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_sources, warnings }`. Iteration: for each capability in `manifest.capabilities[]` (preserving manifest declared order), compute `bucket = classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertySource(capability.input_schema)`; if bucket is `'unknown'`, append a warning entry of the form `{ capability: <name>, reason: <one of five frozen reason codes> }`. Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Each per-bucket entry contains the frozen 8 fields (`input_schema_first_property_source`, `aggregation_key` (`source` for the four locus buckets, `not_applicable` for not_applicable, `unknown` for unknown), `capability_count`, `capabilities[]` of capability names in manifest order, `approved_count` (count of `approved === true`), `gated_count` (count of `approved !== true`), `has_destructive_side_effect` (true iff any capability in bucket has `side_effect_class === 'destructive'`), `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` (true iff any capability in bucket has `sensitivity_class === 'restricted'` or `sensitivity_class === 'confidential'`)). `warnings[]` is always present (empty `[]` when no malformed capabilities) for `--json` shape stability. Field name is `first_property_sources` (plural categorical, matching M48's `first_property_types` precedent verbatim).
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndex(index)` returning a human-readable multi-line string with: `Input Schema First Property Source Index — <manifest_path>` header; per-bucket section showing `<SOURCE>: <capability_count> capabilit{y|ies}` followed by indented capability names and `(approved: <n>, gated: <n>, destructive: <yes|no>, restricted_or_confidential: <yes|no>)` summary line; `Planning aid: this index reports per-capability input_schema.properties[firstKey].source HTTP-request-anatomy locus classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, validate runtime request payloads against the declared first-property source, cross-reference manifest first-property locus vs runtime request shape, generate request payloads, or auto-flip tusq serve filtering. Bucket order is deterministic stable-output ordering only (NOT security-blast-radius-ranked, NOT workflow-criticality-ranked, NOT permission-sensitivity-ranked, NOT HTTP-spec-precedence-ranked, NOT skill-composition-priority-ranked, NOT widget-render-precedence-ranked).` callout at the bottom; `Bucket rule: path (firstKey.source === 'path') | request_body (firstKey.source === 'request_body') | query (firstKey.source === 'query') | header (firstKey.source === 'header') | not_applicable (input_schema.type !== 'object' or zero-property object) | unknown (malformed input_schema or firstKey.source missing/non-string/outside closed four-value set).` line; `Bucket order: path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable → unknown` line.
- [x] Wire `binding` into the `dispatch` table in `src/cli.js` between `auth` and `confidence` (alphabetical in dispatch ordering). The `binding` enumerator (no subcommand) prints a help block listing `tusq binding index` and exits 0.
- [x] Update `printHelp()` to emit the 35-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, auth, **binding**, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version, help) with `binding` inserted between `auth` and `confidence` in the post-`docs` alphabetical block.
- [x] Update `printCommandHelp()` to add `binding` and `binding index` entries with: command shape `tusq binding index [--source <value>] [--manifest <path>] [--out <path>] [--json]`; the four-flag table; the closed six-value bucket-key enum; the closed three-value aggregation_key enum; the frozen classifier function; the bucket iteration order; the planning-aid framing callout; the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--source` rule; the explicit notes that the input-side first-property source axis is distinct from M43 (capability-wide source UNION across all properties with `mixed`/`none` values via `tusq request index`), M47 (input_schema.properties count cardinality via `tusq parameter index`), M49 (input-side first-property primitive type via `tusq signature index`), M50 (input-side first-property required-status via `tusq obligation index`), and M48 (output-side first-property type via `tusq shape index`); that the all-property source distribution is M43's capability-wide axis (for capability-level locus claims) or is reserved for the deferred `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1` successor (for per-property fine-grained distribution); that nested-property-source recursion is reserved for `M-Binding-Nested-Property-Source-Index-1`; that the output-side analog is reserved for `M-Binding-Output-First-Property-Source-Index-1`; that the cookie/file/multipart-locus extensions are reserved for `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1` and `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1`; that downstream SDK call-site signature generation is reserved for `M-Binding-SDK-Generator-1`; that runtime conformance detection is reserved for `M-Binding-Runtime-Conformance-1`; that widget-render-control derivation is reserved for `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1`.
- [x] Add 24-case smoke matrix to `tests/smoke.mjs` (cases a–x): (a) default closed-enum run on canonical fixture (asserts `path` bucket holds one capability — `get_users_api_v1_users_id` (input_schema.properties.id.source='path') — and `request_body` bucket holds one capability — `post_users_users` (input_schema.properties.body.source='request_body') — and `not_applicable` bucket holds one capability — `get_users_users` (input_schema.properties={}, no warning), no warnings); (b) `--source path`; (c) `--source request_body`; (d) `--source query` (synthetic fixture); (e) `--source header` (synthetic fixture); (f) `--source not_applicable`; (g) `--source unknown` (synthetic fixture with malformed source); (h) `--source PATH` (exit 1, case-sensitive); (i) `--source Request_Body` (exit 1); (j) `--source cookie` (exit 1, not in closed six-value bucket-key enum); (k) `--source xyz` (exit 1, unknown); (l) `--manifest <missing>` (exit 1); (m) `--manifest <malformed.json>` (exit 1); (n) `--manifest <no-capabilities>` (exit 1); (o) `--unknown-flag` (exit 1); (p) `--source` with no value (exit 1); (q) `--out <valid path>` writes correctly and stdout is empty; (r) `--out .tusq/foo.json` (exit 1); (s) `--out <unwritable parent>` (exit 1); (t) `--json` outputs valid JSON with `first_property_sources[]` and `warnings: []` (or with warnings populated when malformed); (u) determinism: three consecutive runs produce byte-identical stdout AND manifest mtime + SHA-256 + per-capability `capability_digest` invariant pre/post AND `input_schema_first_property_source` MUST NOT appear in `tusq.manifest.json` after run (non-persistence) AND `tusq compile` byte-identical pre/post AND all 21 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post; (v) malformed capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed input_schema first property source (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all five frozen reason codes (`input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid`) AND empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_sources: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (w) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.source = 'cookie'` → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid` (cookie is NOT in the closed four-value source set; cookie-locus extension is reserved for `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1`); (x) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.source = ['path', 'query']` (array source) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid` (per-property `source` MUST be a single string; array-source is NOT supported and is reserved for a deferred milestone). Update existing M35–M50 help-count assertions from 34 to 35.
- [x] Add `input-schema-first-property-source-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval count 41 → 42). Add `runInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndexDeterminismScenario` handler to `tests/eval-regression.mjs` verifying byte-identical output across three runs, closed six-value bucket-key enum, closed three-value aggregation_key enum, bucket order determinism (`path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable → unknown`), `warnings[]` always present, manifest non-mutation, `input_schema_first_property_source` non-persistence, and the closed-four-value source-set membership contract.
- [x] Update `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with the dev-turn M51 entry (challenge to PM turn, implementation details, verification evidence: `npm test` exit 0 with 42 scenarios, zero new dependencies, zero package drift, 35-command CLI surface confirmed, all M51 invariants verified).
- [x] Add `### M51` detail block to `SYSTEM_SPEC.md` with: purpose statement, command shape, frozen six-value `input_schema_first_property_source` bucket-key enum, frozen three-value `aggregation_key` enum, frozen classifier-function rules, frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape, top-level `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, closed-enum bucket iteration order rule (`path → request_body → query → header → not_applicable → unknown`), within-bucket manifest-declared-order rule, case-sensitive `--source` filter rule, empty-capabilities and stdout-discipline rules, read-only invariants including the explicit non-persistence-of-`input_schema_first_property_source` rule, the no-all-properties-source-walking rule (M43 covers capability-wide union; per-property granularity reserved for `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1`), the no-nested-source-recursion rule, the no-output-side-source rule, the no-cookie/file/multipart-locus rule, and the explicit non-runtime-validator / non-request-conformance-detector / non-SDK-generator / non-widget-renderer-crossref boundary statement.
- [x] Add `### M51` Product CLI Surface block to `command-surface.md` with the two-row command table, four-flag table, bucket-key enum table, aggregation_key enum table, classifier-function rules table, per-bucket entry shape table, bucket iteration order table, default-preservation table for the 34 unchanged commands, failure UX table, and local-only invariants table.
- [x] Add Constraint 44 to SYSTEM_SPEC.md Constraints section: "`tusq binding index` is a planning aid that surfaces the per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].source` HTTP-request-anatomy locus classification (whether the FIRST input property is path-bound, request-body-bound, query-bound, header-bound, not-applicable, or unknown) and the cross-axis side-effect/sensitivity exposure of each locus bucket. It does NOT execute capability invocations at runtime, does NOT validate runtime request payloads against the declared first-property source, does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property locus vs runtime request shape (that is a deferred runtime-conformance milestone), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M39's `input_schema.required` count bucketing rules, does NOT alter M43's capability-wide primary-parameter-source bucketing rules (M43 reads ALL properties' sources and computes a union; M51 reads ONLY the first property's source — the two milestones are orthogonal axes on the related field), does NOT alter M47's `input_schema.properties` cardinality bucketing rules, does NOT alter M48's `output_schema.properties[<firstKey>].type` bucketing rules, does NOT alter M49's `input_schema.properties[<firstKey>].type` bucketing rules, does NOT alter M50's `input_schema.required[]`-membership-of-firstKey bucketing rules, does NOT walk per-property source beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property source under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].source` (reserved for `M-Binding-Nested-Property-Source-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property source (reserved for `M-Binding-Output-First-Property-Source-Index-1`), does NOT extend the closed four-value source set to include `cookie` (reserved for `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1`) or `file`/`multipart`/`form-data` (reserved for `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1`), does NOT generate downstream SDK call-site signature definitions (reserved for `M-Binding-SDK-Generator-1`), does NOT detect runtime payload conformance against the declared first-property source (reserved for `M-Binding-Runtime-Conformance-1`), does NOT compute a derived widget-render-control type by cross-referencing source with type (reserved for `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1`), does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_source` into `tusq.manifest.json`, does NOT compute statistical aggregates. Subsequent milestones ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts and fresh re-approval expectations. The six-value `input_schema_first_property_source` bucket-key enum (`path | request_body | query | header | not_applicable | unknown`), the three-value `aggregation_key` enum (`source | not_applicable | unknown`), the closed four-value property-`source` value set (`path`, `request_body`, `query`, `header`), and the five-value warning reason-code enum are frozen; any addition is a material governance event. The `--source` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case filter values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property source:`. Malformed `input_schema`/malformed-first-property-`source` values are bucketed as `unknown` and emit a `Warning: ...` to stderr (human) or `warnings[]` (`--json`); the `not_applicable` bucket (non-object input or zero-property object) is a valid named bucket and emits NO warning."
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq binding index` command, the six-value bucket-key enum, the three-value aggregation_key enum, the frozen classifier function, the four flags, exit codes, the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--source` rule, the empty-capabilities behavior, the `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, the explicit "planning aid, not a runtime request validator, request-conformance detector, SDK call-site generator, or widget-render-control deriver" callout, and the explicit M43-vs-M51 distinction (M43 capability-wide union via `tusq request index`, M51 first-property-only via `tusq binding index`).

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Reviewers conflate M51 first-property-source with M43 capability-wide source union | Operator could assume `tusq binding index` is a re-bucketing of `tusq request index` (which buckets on the union of ALL properties' sources with `mixed`/`none` values) rather than a per-FIRST-property axis | M51 buckets on `input_schema.properties[firstKey].source` ONLY (single-property categorical: `path | request_body | query | header | not_applicable | unknown` — no `mixed` value because index 0 returns a single property; `not_applicable` instead of M43's `none` to parallel M48/M49/M50 first-property-axis convention); M43 buckets on the UNION of ALL properties' source values (capability-wide categorical: `path | request_body | query | header | mixed | none | unknown`). The two axes share the source value space but operate on different scopes. Smoke case (a) cross-verifies on the canonical fixture: `get_users_api_v1_users_id` is `path` in both M43 and M51 (single-property capability with path source); `post_users_users` is `request_body` in both M43 and M51 (single-property capability with request_body source); `get_users_users` is `none` in M43 (zero-property capability) and `not_applicable` in M51 (the equivalent zero-property semantic at the first-property-axis level). Multi-property synthetic fixtures with two distinct sources differentiate cleanly: M43 → `mixed`, M51 → first-property's source only. CLI help text for `tusq binding index` explicitly states "this index reports per-FIRST-property source classification (Object.keys index 0); for the capability-wide union of ALL properties' sources, see `tusq request index` (M43)." |
| Reviewers conflate M51 first-property-source with M49 first-property-type or M50 first-property-required-status | Operator could assume `tusq binding index` is a re-bucketing of `tusq signature index` (M49, primitive type) or `tusq obligation index` (M50, required-status) rather than a separate cross-axis classification | M49 measures the first property's JSON-Schema primitive `type` (categorical: `string | number | integer | boolean | null | object | array | not_applicable | unknown`); M50 measures whether the SAME firstKey is in `input_schema.required[]` (membership: `required | optional | not_applicable | unknown`); M51 measures the same firstKey's `source` value (HTTP-locus categorical: `path | request_body | query | header | not_applicable | unknown`). The three axes share the firstKey selection but operate on different value spaces (type vs required-membership vs HTTP-locus). CLI help text for `tusq binding index` explicitly states "this index reports firstKey's HTTP-request-anatomy source locus, distinct from M49's primitive-type classification (`tusq signature index`) and M50's required-status membership (`tusq obligation index`)." Smoke case (a) verifies all three axes on the canonical fixture — the same three capabilities discriminate cleanly across all three first-property axes. |
| `path` bucket misread as "this capability has only path-bound parameters" rather than "the first property is path-bound" | Operator sees a capability in the `path` bucket and assumes the entire input schema is path-bound; in fact only the FIRST property is checked | M51 measures ONLY the FIRST property's source. A capability with `properties = {a: {source:'path'}, b: {source:'request_body'}}` buckets as `path` (firstKey `a` source is path) even though the schema also has a request-body-bound parameter. CLI help, SYSTEM_SPEC § M51, and the format callout all explicitly say "first property's source, not the capability's overall locus union." The capability-wide locus union is M43's axis (`tusq request index` with `mixed`/`none` values); the per-property fine-grained source distribution is reserved for `M-Binding-All-Properties-Source-Index-1`. |
| `cookie`/`file`/`multipart`/`form-data` first-property sources silently bucketed as `request_body` | Operators expecting strict-spec four-locus enforcement would see cookie-bound and multipart-bound first properties disappear into `request_body`, masking the cookie-locus distinction that `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1` is reserved to surface and the file/multipart-locus distinction that `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1` is reserved to surface | Classifier checks `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_SOURCE_VALUE_SET.has(firstVal.source)` against the frozen closed four-value set (`path`, `request_body`, `query`, `header`); any first-property source outside this set buckets the capability as `unknown` with reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_source_invalid`. Smoke case (j) explicitly asserts `--source cookie` exits 1; smoke case (w) asserts a synthetic fixture with `source: 'cookie'` buckets as `unknown`. SYSTEM_SPEC § M51 and Constraint 44 codify the no-coercion rule. The cookie/file/multipart distinctions are reserved for `M-Binding-Cookie-Bucket-1` and `M-Binding-File-Bucket-1`. |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | If the implementer sorts keys or uses Map iteration with re-ordered keys, the FIRST property selected may differ across runs, breaking byte-identity | The classifier MUST use `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` directly; MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence; MUST rely on JSON.parse's insertion-order-preserving behavior. Determinism eval scenario explicitly verifies byte-identical output across three runs on a fixture with `properties` keys ordered `{c, a, b}` where each has a different source to confirm the selected first-key's source matches insertion-order index 0, not the sorted-key index 0. |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid array-typed input capabilities, masking real malformations | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) when `input_schema.type` is a string other than `'object'`; only missing-or-non-string `type` returns `'unknown'`. Smoke case (matching M49/M50 precedent) includes a synthetic fixture with `input_schema={type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}` asserting `not_applicable` with no warning. |
| Bucket order described as "security-blast-radius-ranked" or "skill-composition-priority-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a privilege-sensitivity gauge or skill-orchestration-priority hint, leading to false confidence that `path`-bucket capabilities are "safer to compose" or "lower-risk" than `request_body`-bucket capabilities | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only matching HTTP request anatomy reading order; SYSTEM_SPEC § M51, Constraint 44, and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertySourceIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only, NOT security-blast-radius-ranked, NOT workflow-criticality-ranked, NOT permission-sensitivity-ranked, NOT HTTP-spec-precedence-ranked, NOT skill-composition-priority-ranked, NOT widget-render-precedence-ranked." Cross-axis derivations are reserved for `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1`. |
| `--source` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--source PATH` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about case-sensitivity contract | `--source` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M50 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property source:`. Empty-string `--source ''` exits 1. Smoke case (h) asserts `--source PATH` exits 1; smoke case (i) asserts `--source Request_Body` exits 1; smoke case (k) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `binding` inserted in the wrong position would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 34-command surface | Alphabetical insertion verified: `auth` < `binding` (`a` (97) < `b` (98) at position 0); `binding` < `confidence` (`b` (98) < `c` (99) at position 0); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., approve, auth, binding, confidence, description, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^binding$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 35. |
| Widget-render-control derivation in scope creep | An implementer could read the path/request_body/query/header source as a natural place to compute a derived widget-render-control type (entity-picker / structured-form / filter-input / hidden-context) and emit it inline | M51 emits ONLY the per-bucket 8-field entry shape with the source string and cross-axis flags; it does NOT emit a derived widget-render-control type. The widget-renderer crossref is explicitly deferred to `M-Binding-Widget-Renderer-Crossref-1`. SYSTEM_SPEC § M51 and Constraint 44 codify this scope boundary. |

### M52: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Description Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.33 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_3f128359168988b4` (`turn_09cba7c7ad415bd0`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `15424bc` from intake charter `intent_1777370751746_c43c` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M51: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Source Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.32 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 99–106 (`### Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy; M51 cites § Skills). The section reads: "Internal docs make employee copilots, support copilots, implementation copilots, and governance artifacts substantially more useful than code alone" and enumerates support macros, runbooks, security procedures, SOC2 controls, audit processes, sales engineering notes, CSM playbooks, implementation guides, product specs, PRDs, compliance guidance, data-handling rules, and operational constraints. The per-`input_schema_first_property_description_presence` capability roll-up answers the documentation-completeness question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, does the FIRST input parameter carry a non-empty docstring (`description` field) that a Support Copilot, Sales Engineer Copilot, CSM Copilot, Implementation Copilot, Admin Copilot, Security Review Copilot, or governance reviewer can rely on as the operating-knowledge gloss for that parameter — or is the FIRST input parameter undescribed (forcing the copilot to fall back to inferred/best-guess prose), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — there is no first property to describe), or unknown (malformed)?" A Support Copilot composing a refund tool whose first input parameter (`invoice_id`) is `described` ("the canonical invoice identifier as printed on the customer's receipt") can surface that gloss to the agent at tool-call time as call-site help; a Security Review Copilot reviewing the same capability whose first parameter is `undescribed` MUST flag the capability as a documentation-completeness gap that blocks safe agent composition. The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `undescribed` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose primary input slot lacks a docstring exposes a "documentation-completeness gap on a high-stakes capability" review obligation that copilot-composition reviewers MUST surface. Distinct from M44 on the SAME `description`-noun-family: M44 reads `capability.description` (the capability-level top-level description string) and bucketizes its word count; M52 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` (the FIRST input property's docstring) and bucketizes its presence (described|undescribed). M44 is a capability-level word-count cardinality axis; M52 is a per-FIRST-property presence-of-docstring axis. Distinct from M49 on the SAME `input_schema.properties[<firstKey>]` object (M49 reads `.type`; M50 reads `firstKey ∈ required[]` membership; M51 reads `.source`; M52 reads `.description` — different fields entirely). Distinct from M48 (output-side first-property type, no `description`-presence aggregation). The first milestone to use § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 268–283 (`### Knowledge Artifacts`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the documentation-provenance framing item — Knowledge Artifacts must "cite source provenance and flag contradictions between docs, code, and observed runtime behavior." M52's per-capability first-input-property docstring presence is a static-code-derived signal that knowledge-artifact compilers can join against external doc-corpora to detect missing-or-mismatched parameter docstrings. Re-cited only — M52's primary source is § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `described | undescribed | not_applicable | unknown`. `described` denotes capabilities whose `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` is a non-empty trimmed string (`typeof === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0`). `undescribed` denotes capabilities whose `firstKey.description` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR is a string with `value.trim().length === 0` (semantically a present-but-empty docstring counts as absent). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — semantically valid cases where there is no first input property to evaluate description-presence on, paralleling M49/M50/M51's `not_applicable` semantics for the same shape. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), `properties[firstKey]` not a plain non-null object, OR `properties[firstKey].description` PRESENT but not a string (e.g., array, plain object, number, boolean — strict-typing rule: a present-but-non-string description is malformed, NOT silently coerced into `undescribed`). The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `description_presence | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the two presence buckets (`described`, `undescribed`) carry aggregation_key `description_presence`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48/M49/M50/M51 first-property-axis pattern: a single semantic-aggregation key for the "real" buckets, and two reserved keys for the absent-target and malformed cases.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` (e.g., `'array'`, `'string'`, `'number'`, etc.) — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid (zero-property object input has no first property to evaluate); no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid`). If `firstVal.description` is PRESENT (i.e., `firstVal` has the property `description` AND its value is not `undefined`) but `typeof firstVal.description !== 'string'`, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present`). If `firstVal.description` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR `typeof === 'string'` AND `firstVal.description.trim().length === 0`, return `'undescribed'` (no warning — empty/missing docstring is a documentation-completeness signal, not a malformation). Otherwise return `'described'`.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order via `JSON.parse`. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_description_presence`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M51 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-descriptor / malformed-first-property-`description` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present`. The `not_applicable` bucket (non-object input or zero-property object) does NOT emit a warning. The `described` and `undescribed` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes; a missing-or-empty docstring is a documentation-completeness signal, NOT a malformation. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings, and a present-but-non-string `description` value (the only malformation specific to this axis) is the fifth code. Note: malformed-`firstVal-not-an-object` falls under `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` which is the `unknown`-bucket reason already covered by the existing four codes — the fifth code is reserved exclusively for the `description`-field-typing failure. No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a documentation-completeness ranking, NOT a reviewer-priority ranking, NOT a maintenance-debt ranking, NOT a public-docs-gap ranking, NOT a launch-readiness ranking, NOT a copilot-composition-readiness ranking, NOT an onboarding-clarity ranking): `described → undescribed → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional "documented-first, undocumented-second, irrelevant-third, malformed-last" reading order for documentation-completeness review aids. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--presence` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `DESCRIBED`, `Undescribed`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property description presence: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M51 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property description presence: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M51): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 22 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`, `tusq binding index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 35 → 36 commands. New top-level noun `gloss` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `examples` and `input` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, binding, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version`; `examples` < `gloss` because at position 0 `e` (101) < `g` (103); `gloss` < `input` because at position 0 `g` (103) < `i` (105)). Insertion sequence: ..., `examples`, `gloss`, `input`, `items`, `method`, ..., `version`, `help`. The fresh noun `gloss` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `description`, `signature`, or `binding`) preserves the M31–M51 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `gloss` (a brief annotation/explanation of a term, the textual sense of "the docstring on the first input parameter") is intentional and is distinct from `description` (M44, capability-level top-level description word count), `signature` (M49, input_schema.properties[firstKey].type), `obligation` (M50, input_schema.properties[firstKey] required-status), `binding` (M51, input_schema.properties[firstKey].source), and `parameter` (M47, input_schema.properties count tier — cardinality) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter docstring/annotation presence.
> - **Result-array field name** `first_property_description_presences` (plural categorical with closed bucket-key family; the input-side scope is carried by the per-bucket field name `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` rather than the result-array field name; matches M48/M51's `first_property_<axis>` plural categorical naming convention verbatim).
> - **Eval scenario**: `input-schema-first-property-description-presence-index-determinism` added; eval harness 42 → ≥43 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 45** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M52 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` docstring-presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime documentation completeness, does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property docstring vs published API/OpenAPI/SDK signature docs (that is a deferred doc-contradiction milestone), does NOT auto-generate missing docstrings from the property name (reserved for an LLM-synthesizer milestone), does NOT score docstring quality (reserved for a quality-scorer milestone), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M44's `capability.description` word-count tier rules (M44 reads the capability-level top-level `description` string; M52 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` — entirely different fields), does NOT walk per-property description beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Gloss-All-Properties-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property description under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].description` (reserved for `M-Gloss-Nested-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property description (reserved for `M-Gloss-Output-First-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT count description word/character/sentence length (reserved for `M-Gloss-Description-Word-Count-Tier-1`), does NOT detect description language (reserved for `M-Gloss-Description-Language-Detector-1`), does NOT generate downstream SDK call-site help-text definitions (reserved for `M-Gloss-SDK-Help-Generator-1`), does NOT detect runtime payload conformance against the declared description (no runtime semantics on description), does NOT compute statistical aggregates, does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` into `tusq.manifest.json`.
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Gloss-All-Properties-Description-Presence-Index-1` (per-property fine-grained description-presence distribution rather than capability-level first-property axis), `M-Gloss-Nested-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1` (recursive walk of nested-object property description under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].description`), `M-Gloss-Output-First-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1` (output-side analog), `M-Gloss-Description-Word-Count-Tier-1` (per-FIRST-property description word-count tier — distinct from M44's capability-level word-count tier), `M-Gloss-Description-Language-Detector-1` (language detection on the firstKey description), `M-Gloss-Description-Quality-Score-1` (heuristic quality score: starts-with-verb, mentions-units, mentions-format, mentions-constraint, etc.), `M-Gloss-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Gloss-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest first-property description and published API/OpenAPI/SDK docs), `M-Gloss-LLM-Description-Synthesizer-1` (LLM-based docstring synthesizer for `undescribed`-bucket capabilities — explicitly out of M52's scope, which is read-only reviewer aid only), `M-Gloss-SDK-Help-Generator-1` (downstream SDK call-site help-text generator that consumes M52's index as input).

- [x] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTION_PRESENCE_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `described | undescribed | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTION_PRESENCE_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `description_presence | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTION_PRESENCE_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `described → undescribed → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M51 input-schema-first-property-source constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M51 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresence(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.description` is present (key exists with non-`undefined` value) AND `typeof firstVal.description !== 'string'`; returns `'undescribed'` if `firstVal.description` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR is a string with `.trim().length === 0`; otherwise returns `'described'`.
- [x] Add `cmdGloss` dispatcher and `cmdGlossIndex(args)` command handler in `src/cli.js`. The handler MUST: (a) call `parseGlossIndexArgs` first; (b) detect `--out` path validity (rejected: paths under `.tusq/`, paths in non-existent or non-writable parent directories) BEFORE reading the manifest, with stderr `--out path must not be inside .tusq/` or `--out parent directory does not exist or is not writable` and exit 1, empty stdout; (c) read and parse the manifest from `--manifest <path>` (default `tusq.manifest.json` in cwd) with stderr `Manifest file not found: <path>` for missing, `Failed to parse manifest JSON: <message>` for malformed, `Manifest is missing capabilities array` for missing/non-array `capabilities` field, each exit 1 with empty stdout; (d) apply case-sensitive `--presence <value>` filter against the closed four-value enum, with stderr `Unknown input schema first property description presence: <value>` and exit 1 for any value not in the enum; (e) build the index via `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndex(manifest)`; (f) emit per-capability malformed warnings to stderr in human mode (one `Warning: capability '<name>' has malformed input_schema first property description presence (<reason>)` per malformed capability) and to `warnings[]` in `--json` mode; (g) format via `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndex` (human) or `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` (`--json`).
- [x] Add `parseGlossIndexArgs(args)` accepting exactly four flags: `--presence <value>`, `--manifest <path>`, `--out <path>`, `--json`. Reject any unknown flag with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>` and exit 1, empty stdout. Reject `--presence` with no value (stderr `--presence requires a value`), `--manifest` with no value, `--out` with no value, each exit 1.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndex(manifest, options = {})` returning `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_description_presences, warnings }`. Iteration: for each capability in `manifest.capabilities[]` (preserving manifest declared order), compute `bucket = classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresence(capability.input_schema)`; if bucket is `'unknown'`, append a warning entry of the form `{ capability: <name>, reason: <one of five frozen reason codes> }`. Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`described → undescribed → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Each per-bucket entry contains the frozen 8 fields (`input_schema_first_property_description_presence`, `aggregation_key` (`description_presence` for the two presence buckets, `not_applicable` for not_applicable, `unknown` for unknown), `capability_count`, `capabilities[]` of capability names in manifest order, `approved_count` (count of `approved === true`), `gated_count` (count of `approved !== true`), `has_destructive_side_effect` (true iff any capability in bucket has `side_effect_class === 'destructive'`), `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` (true iff any capability in bucket has `sensitivity_class === 'restricted'` or `sensitivity_class === 'confidential'`)). `warnings[]` is always present (empty `[]` when no malformed capabilities) for `--json` shape stability. Field name is `first_property_description_presences` (plural categorical, matching M48/M51's `first_property_<axis>` precedent verbatim).
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndex(index)` returning a human-readable multi-line string with: `Input Schema First Property Description Presence Index — <manifest_path>` header; per-bucket section showing `<PRESENCE>: <capability_count> capabilit{y|ies}` followed by indented capability names and `(approved: <n>, gated: <n>, destructive: <yes|no>, restricted_or_confidential: <yes|no>)` summary line; `Planning aid: this index reports per-capability input_schema.properties[firstKey].description docstring-presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, validate runtime documentation completeness, cross-reference manifest first-property docstring vs published API/OpenAPI/SDK docs, generate missing docstrings, score docstring quality, or auto-flip tusq serve filtering. Bucket order is deterministic stable-output ordering only (NOT documentation-completeness-ranked, NOT reviewer-priority-ranked, NOT maintenance-debt-ranked, NOT public-docs-gap-ranked, NOT launch-readiness-ranked, NOT copilot-composition-readiness-ranked, NOT onboarding-clarity-ranked).` callout at the bottom; `Bucket rule: described (firstKey.description is a non-empty trimmed string) | undescribed (firstKey.description missing/null/undefined or empty trimmed string) | not_applicable (input_schema.type !== 'object' or zero-property object) | unknown (malformed input_schema or firstKey not a plain object or firstKey.description present but non-string).` line; `Bucket order: described → undescribed → not_applicable → unknown` line.
- [x] Wire `gloss` into the `dispatch` table in `src/cli.js` between `examples` and `input` (alphabetical in dispatch ordering). The `gloss` enumerator (no subcommand) prints a help block listing `tusq gloss index` and exits 0.

- [x] Update `printCommandHelp()` to add `gloss` and `gloss index` entries with: command shape `tusq gloss index [--presence <value>] [--manifest <path>] [--out <path>] [--json]`; the four-flag table; the closed four-value bucket-key enum; the closed three-value aggregation_key enum; the frozen classifier function; the bucket iteration order; the planning-aid framing callout; the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--presence` rule; the explicit notes that the input-side first-property description-presence axis is distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level description word count via `tusq description index`), M47 (input_schema.properties count cardinality via `tusq parameter index`), M49 (input-side first-property primitive type via `tusq signature index`), M50 (input-side first-property required-status via `tusq obligation index`), M51 (input-side first-property HTTP-source locus via `tusq binding index`), and M48 (output-side first-property type via `tusq shape index`); that the all-property description-presence distribution is reserved for the deferred `M-Gloss-All-Properties-Description-Presence-Index-1` successor; that nested-property description recursion is reserved for `M-Gloss-Nested-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`; that the output-side analog is reserved for `M-Gloss-Output-First-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`; that the per-FIRST-property word-count tier is reserved for `M-Gloss-Description-Word-Count-Tier-1`; that quality scoring is reserved for `M-Gloss-Description-Quality-Score-1`; that LLM-synthesis is reserved for `M-Gloss-LLM-Description-Synthesizer-1`; that doc-contradiction detection is reserved for `M-Gloss-Doc-Contradiction-1`.
