(b) `--presence described`; (c) `--presence undescribed`; (d) `--presence not_applicable`; (e) `--presence unknown` (synthetic fixture with malformed input_schema.properties.first.description=42); (f) `--presence DESCRIBED` (exit 1, case-sensitive); (g) `--presence Undescribed` (exit 1); (h) `--presence xyz` (exit 1, unknown); (i) `--manifest <missing>` (exit 1); (j) `--manifest <malformed.json>` (exit 1); (k) `--manifest <no-capabilities>` (exit 1); (l) `--unknown-flag` (exit 1); (m) `--presence` with no value (exit 1); (n) `--out <valid path>` writes correctly and stdout is empty; (o) `--out .tusq/foo.json` (exit 1); (p) `--out <unwritable parent>` (exit 1); (q) `--json` outputs valid JSON with `first_property_description_presences[]` and `warnings: []` (or with warnings populated when malformed); (r) determinism: three consecutive runs produce byte-identical stdout AND manifest mtime + SHA-256 + per-capability `capability_digest` invariant pre/post AND `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` MUST NOT appear in `tusq.manifest.json` after run (non-persistence) AND `tusq compile` byte-identical pre/post AND all 22 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post; (s) malformed capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed input_schema first property description presence (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all five frozen reason codes (`input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present`) AND empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_description_presences: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (t) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.description = ''` (empty string) → bucket `undescribed` with NO warning; (u) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.description = '   '` (whitespace-only string) → bucket `undescribed` with NO warning (trim semantics); (v) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.description = ['a','b']` (array) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present`; (w) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.description = {text:'foo'}` (object) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present`; (x) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.description = null` → bucket `undescribed` with NO warning (null is treated identically to missing). Update existing M35–M51 help-count assertions from 35 to 36.
- [x] Add `input-schema-first-property-description-presence-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval count 42 → 43). Add `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndexDeterminismScenario` handler to `tests/eval-regression.mjs` verifying byte-identical output across three runs, closed four-value bucket-key enum, closed three-value aggregation_key enum, bucket order determinism (`described → undescribed → not_applicable → unknown`), `warnings[]` always present, manifest non-mutation, `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` non-persistence, and the trim-empty-counts-as-undescribed contract.
- [x] Update `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with the dev-turn M52 entry (challenge to PM turn, implementation details, verification evidence: `npm test` exit 0 with 43 scenarios, zero new dependencies, zero package drift, 36-command CLI surface confirmed, all M52 invariants verified).
- [x] Add `### M52` detail block to `SYSTEM_SPEC.md` with: purpose statement, command shape, frozen four-value `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` bucket-key enum, frozen three-value `aggregation_key` enum, frozen classifier-function rules, frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape, top-level `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, closed-enum bucket iteration order rule (`described → undescribed → not_applicable → unknown`), within-bucket manifest-declared-order rule, case-sensitive `--presence` filter rule, empty-capabilities and stdout-discipline rules, read-only invariants including the explicit non-persistence-of-`input_schema_first_property_description_presence` rule, the no-all-properties-description-walking rule, the no-nested-description-recursion rule, the no-output-side-description rule, the no-quality-scoring rule, the no-LLM-synthesis rule, the no-language-detection rule, and the explicit non-runtime-validator / non-doc-contradiction-detector / non-quality-scorer / non-LLM-synthesizer / non-SDK-help-generator boundary statement.
- [x] Add `### M52` Product CLI Surface block to `command-surface.md` with the two-row command table, four-flag table, bucket-key enum table, aggregation_key enum table, classifier-function rules table, per-bucket entry shape table, bucket iteration order table, default-preservation table for the 35 unchanged commands, failure UX table, and local-only invariants table.
- [x] Add Constraint 45 to SYSTEM_SPEC.md Constraints section: "`tusq gloss index` is a planning aid that surfaces the per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` docstring-presence classification (whether the FIRST input property carries a non-empty trimmed docstring, is undescribed, is not-applicable, or is unknown) and the cross-axis side-effect/sensitivity exposure of each presence bucket. It does NOT execute capability invocations at runtime, does NOT validate runtime documentation completeness, does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property docstring vs published API/OpenAPI/SDK docs (deferred to `M-Gloss-Doc-Contradiction-1`), does NOT auto-generate missing docstrings (deferred to `M-Gloss-LLM-Description-Synthesizer-1`), does NOT score docstring quality (deferred to `M-Gloss-Description-Quality-Score-1`), does NOT count description word/character/sentence length per FIRST property (deferred to `M-Gloss-Description-Word-Count-Tier-1`), does NOT detect description language (deferred to `M-Gloss-Description-Language-Detector-1`), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M44's `capability.description` word-count tier rules (M44 reads the capability-level top-level `description` string; M52 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` — entirely different fields), does NOT walk per-property description beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Gloss-All-Properties-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property description under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].description` (reserved for `M-Gloss-Nested-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property description (reserved for `M-Gloss-Output-First-Property-Description-Presence-Index-1`), does NOT generate downstream SDK call-site help-text definitions (reserved for `M-Gloss-SDK-Help-Generator-1`), does NOT compute statistical aggregates, does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` into `tusq.manifest.json`. Subsequent milestones ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts and fresh re-approval expectations. The four-value `input_schema_first_property_description_presence` bucket-key enum (`described | undescribed | not_applicable | unknown`), the three-value `aggregation_key` enum (`description_presence | not_applicable | unknown`), and the five-value warning reason-code enum are frozen; any addition is a material governance event. The `--presence` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case filter values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property description presence:`. Malformed `input_schema`/malformed-firstVal-descriptor/present-but-non-string-`description` values are bucketed as `unknown` and emit a `Warning: ...` to stderr (human) or `warnings[]` (`--json`); the `not_applicable` bucket and the `described`/`undescribed` buckets are valid named outcomes and emit NO warning. A present-but-empty (or whitespace-only) `description` string is `undescribed`, NOT a malformation: an empty docstring is a documentation-completeness signal, not a typing failure."
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq gloss index` command, the four-value bucket-key enum, the three-value aggregation_key enum, the frozen classifier function, the four flags, exit codes, the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--presence` rule, the empty-capabilities behavior, the `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, the explicit "planning aid, not a runtime documentation validator, doc-contradiction detector, quality scorer, LLM synthesizer, or SDK help-text generator" callout, and the explicit M44-vs-M52 distinction (M44 capability-level description word-count tier via `tusq description index`, M52 first-property docstring-presence via `tusq gloss index`).

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Reviewers conflate M52 first-property description-presence with M44 capability-level description word-count tier | Operator could assume `tusq gloss index` is a re-bucketing of `tusq description index` (which buckets on the count of words in the capability-level top-level `description` string) rather than a per-FIRST-property docstring-presence axis | M52 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` (the FIRST input property's docstring, presence-only); M44 reads `capability.description` (the capability-level top-level description, word-count tier). The two axes share the `description`-noun-family but operate on different fields entirely. CLI help text for `tusq gloss index` explicitly states "this index reports per-FIRST-input-property description presence (Object.keys index 0); for the capability-level top-level description word-count tier, see `tusq description index` (M44)." Smoke case (a) cross-verifies on the canonical fixture that the two axes do not overlap. |
| Empty-string and whitespace-only `description` silently bucket as `unknown` instead of `undescribed` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid-but-empty docstring capabilities, masking real documentation-malformation cases AND inflating the malformed-warning count | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'undescribed'` (no warning) when `firstVal.description` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR a string with `.trim().length === 0`. The `unknown` bucket is reserved for present-but-non-string `description` values (array, plain object, number, boolean) — those ARE malformations of the JSON-Schema `description` field. Smoke cases (t), (u), (x) verify empty-string, whitespace-only, and null all bucket as `undescribed` with NO warning; smoke cases (v), (w) verify array and object descriptions bucket as `unknown` with the fifth warning code. |
| Present-but-non-string `description` (e.g., array of strings, object with text) silently coerces into `described` or `undescribed` | A reviewer evaluating a capability with a mistakenly-typed description (e.g., array form) would see it bucket as `described` (because the array is truthy) or `undescribed` (because the array is not a string), masking a real schema malformation that downstream SDK help-generators would choke on | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'unknown'` with reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_description_invalid_when_present` when `firstVal.description` is PRESENT but `typeof !== 'string'`. The strict-typing rule mirrors M49/M50/M51's strict-typing precedent: a present-but-malformed value is `unknown`, not silently coerced. Smoke cases (v), (w) explicitly assert array and object descriptions bucket as `unknown` with the fifth warning code. |
| `undescribed` bucket misread as "this capability has no documentation at all" rather than "the first property is undescribed" | Reviewer sees a capability in the `undescribed` bucket and assumes the entire input schema is undocumented; in fact only the FIRST property is checked | M52 measures ONLY the FIRST property's `description` field. A capability with `properties = {a: {description: ''}, b: {description: 'detailed docstring on b'}}` buckets as `undescribed` (firstKey `a` description is empty) even though the schema has a fully-documented second property. CLI help, SYSTEM_SPEC § M52, and the format callout all explicitly say "first property's description, not the capability's overall documentation completeness." The all-property description-presence distribution is reserved for `M-Gloss-All-Properties-Description-Presence-Index-1`. |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | If the implementer sorts keys or uses Map iteration with re-ordered keys, the FIRST property selected may differ across runs, breaking byte-identity | The classifier MUST use `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` directly; MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence; MUST rely on JSON.parse's insertion-order-preserving behavior. Determinism eval scenario explicitly verifies byte-identical output across three runs on a fixture with `properties` keys ordered `{c, a, b}` where `c` is undescribed and `a` is described to confirm the selected first-key's description-presence matches insertion-order index 0, not the sorted-key index 0 (which would flip the bucket from `undescribed` to `described`). |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid array-typed input capabilities, masking real malformations | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) when `input_schema.type` is a string other than `'object'`; only missing-or-non-string `type` returns `'unknown'`. Smoke case (matching M49/M50/M51 precedent) includes a synthetic fixture with `input_schema={type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}` asserting `not_applicable` with no warning. |
| Bucket order described as "documentation-completeness-ranked" or "launch-readiness-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a launch-readiness gauge or copilot-composition-priority hint, leading to false confidence that `described`-bucket capabilities are "launch-ready" while `undescribed`-bucket capabilities are blockers | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only; SYSTEM_SPEC § M52, Constraint 45, and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyDescriptionPresenceIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only, NOT documentation-completeness-ranked, NOT reviewer-priority-ranked, NOT maintenance-debt-ranked, NOT public-docs-gap-ranked, NOT launch-readiness-ranked, NOT copilot-composition-readiness-ranked, NOT onboarding-clarity-ranked." Cross-axis derivations are reserved for `M-Gloss-Description-Quality-Score-1`. |
| `--presence` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--presence DESCRIBED` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about case-sensitivity contract | `--presence` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M51 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property description presence:`. Empty-string `--presence ''` exits 1. Smoke case (f) asserts `--presence DESCRIBED` exits 1; smoke case (g) asserts `--presence Undescribed` exits 1; smoke case (h) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `gloss` inserted in the wrong position would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 35-command surface | Alphabetical insertion verified: `examples` < `gloss` (`e` (101) < `g` (103) at position 0); `gloss` < `input` (`g` (103) < `i` (105) at position 0); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., examples, gloss, input, items, method, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^gloss$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 36. |
| LLM-synthesis or quality-scoring scope creep | An implementer could read the `undescribed` bucket as a natural place to auto-synthesize missing docstrings via LLM, or could derive a quality score from the `described` bucket's docstring contents and emit it inline | M52 emits ONLY the per-bucket 8-field entry shape with the presence label and cross-axis flags; it does NOT call any LLM, does NOT synthesize text, does NOT score quality. LLM synthesis is explicitly deferred to `M-Gloss-LLM-Description-Synthesizer-1`; quality scoring is deferred to `M-Gloss-Description-Quality-Score-1`. SYSTEM_SPEC § M52 and Constraint 45 codify these scope boundaries. |

### M53: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Format Hint Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.34 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_e05bf49856cd2880` (`turn_6ad1bae70240d47c`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `78a5bd4` from intake charter `intent_1777373633777_96d2` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M52: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Description Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.33 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 268–283 (`### Knowledge Artifacts`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy; M51 cites § Skills; M52 cites § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge). The section reads: "tusq.dev should generate knowledge artifacts from public docs, internal docs, support systems, and code-derived product truth. Outputs include canonical product Q&A, RAG indexes and retrieval manifests, support macros, troubleshooting trees, escalation playbooks, onboarding guides, customer-facing help flows, internal operator guides. Knowledge artifacts must cite source provenance and flag contradictions between docs, code, and observed runtime behavior." The per-`input_schema_first_property_format_hint` capability roll-up answers the knowledge-artifact-rendering question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, does the FIRST input parameter declare a JSON-Schema `format` hint (`email`, `date`, `uri`, `uuid`, etc.) that customer-facing help flows, troubleshooting trees, RAG retrieval manifests, and SDK-call-site widgets can use to render a typed-input affordance (email validator, date picker, URI normalizer, etc.) at composition time — or is the FIRST input parameter unhinted (forcing the knowledge-artifact compiler to fall back to free-form text input), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — there is no first property to hint), or unknown (malformed)?" A customer-facing help flow rendering a `password reset` capability whose first input parameter is `hinted` (`format: 'email'`) can render an email-validator widget with inline format help; a troubleshooting tree composing the same capability whose first parameter is `unhinted` MUST flag the capability as a knowledge-artifact-rendering gap that blocks safe automated affordance generation. The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `unhinted` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose primary input slot lacks a format hint exposes a "knowledge-artifact-rendering gap on a high-stakes capability" review obligation that knowledge-artifact-compiler reviewers MUST surface. Distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level `description` word count); distinct from M49 (firstKey `.type` JSON-Schema primitive type); distinct from M50 (firstKey ∈ required[] membership); distinct from M51 (firstKey `.source` HTTP-locus); distinct from M52 (firstKey `.description` docstring presence). M53 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].format` (the FIRST input property's JSON-Schema format-hint string) — a different field entirely from M49/M50/M51/M52. The first milestone to use § Knowledge Artifacts as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 99–106 (`### Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the operator-guide-rendering framing item — Internal Docs include implementation guides, support macros, and CSM playbooks that benefit from typed-input rendering when the first input parameter declares its formatting convention. Re-cited only — M53's primary source is § Knowledge Artifacts. M52 already used § Internal Docs as primary.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `hinted | unhinted | not_applicable | unknown`. `hinted` denotes capabilities whose `input_schema.properties[firstKey].format` is a non-empty trimmed string (`typeof === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0`). `unhinted` denotes capabilities whose `firstKey.format` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR is a string with `value.trim().length === 0` (semantically a present-but-empty format hint counts as absent — mirrors M52's empty-string-counts-as-absent precedent). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — paralleling M49/M50/M51/M52's `not_applicable` semantics for the same shape. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), `properties[firstKey]` not a plain non-null object, OR `properties[firstKey].format` PRESENT but not a string (e.g., array, plain object, number, boolean — strict-typing rule: a present-but-non-string format is malformed, NOT silently coerced into `unhinted`). The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `format_hint | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the two presence buckets (`hinted`, `unhinted`) carry aggregation_key `format_hint`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48/M49/M50/M51/M52 first-property-axis pattern: a single semantic-aggregation key for the "real" buckets, and two reserved keys for the absent-target and malformed cases.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` (e.g., `'array'`, `'string'`, `'number'`, etc.) — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid (zero-property object input has no first property to evaluate); no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid`, mirroring M52's 6th-code-via-shared-descriptor-malformation pattern). If `firstVal.format` is PRESENT (i.e., `firstVal` has the property `format` AND its value is not `undefined`) but `typeof firstVal.format !== 'string'`, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present`). If `firstVal.format` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR `typeof === 'string'` AND `firstVal.format.trim().length === 0`, return `'unhinted'` (no warning — empty/missing format hint is a knowledge-artifact-rendering signal, not a malformation). Otherwise return `'hinted'`.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order via `JSON.parse`. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_format_hint`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M52 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-descriptor / malformed-first-property-`format` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present`. The `not_applicable` bucket (non-object input or zero-property object) does NOT emit a warning. The `hinted` and `unhinted` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes; a missing-or-empty format hint is a knowledge-artifact-rendering signal, NOT a malformation. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings, and a present-but-non-string `format` value (the only malformation specific to this axis) is the fifth code. Note: malformed-`firstVal-not-an-object` falls under `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` which is the `unknown`-bucket reason already covered as a 6th-code-via-shared-descriptor-malformation pattern (mirrors M52); the fifth code in the PM-frozen list is reserved exclusively for the `format`-field-typing failure. No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a knowledge-artifact-readiness ranking, NOT a reviewer-priority ranking, NOT a UX-affordance-completeness ranking, NOT a help-flow-readiness ranking, NOT an SDK-validator-readiness ranking, NOT an onboarding-clarity ranking, NOT an OpenAPI-conformance ranking): `hinted → unhinted → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional "format-hinted-first, format-unhinted-second, irrelevant-third, malformed-last" reading order for knowledge-artifact-rendering review aids. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--hint` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `HINTED`, `Unhinted`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property format hint: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M52 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property format hint: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M52): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 23 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`, `tusq binding index`, `tusq gloss index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 36 → 37 commands. New top-level noun `hint` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `gloss` and `input` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, binding, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, gloss, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version`; `gloss` < `hint` because at position 0 `g` (103) < `h` (104); `hint` < `input` because at position 0 `h` (104) < `i` (105)). Insertion sequence: ..., `gloss`, `hint`, `input`, `items`, `method`, ..., `version`, `help`. The fresh noun `hint` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `signature`, `gloss`, or `binding`) preserves the M31–M52 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `hint` (a JSON-Schema format-hint string declaring the formatting convention of a typed primitive — e.g., `email`, `date`, `uri`, `uuid`) is intentional and is distinct from `description` (M44, capability-level top-level description word count), `signature` (M49, input_schema.properties[firstKey].type primitive type), `obligation` (M50, input_schema.properties[firstKey] required-status), `binding` (M51, input_schema.properties[firstKey].source HTTP-locus), `gloss` (M52, input_schema.properties[firstKey].description docstring presence), and `parameter` (M47, input_schema.properties count tier — cardinality) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter format-hint presence.
> - **Result-array field name** `first_property_format_hints` (plural categorical with closed bucket-key family; the input-side scope is carried by the per-bucket field name `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` rather than the result-array field name; matches M48/M51/M52's `first_property_<axis>` plural categorical naming convention verbatim).
> - **Eval scenario**: `input-schema-first-property-format-hint-index-determinism` added; eval harness 43 → ≥44 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 46** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M53 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].format` JSON-Schema-format-hint presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime payload conformance against the declared format (e.g., does NOT verify that an `email`-hinted parameter actually receives a valid email at runtime), does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property format vs published OpenAPI/SDK signature format hints (deferred to a doc-contradiction milestone), does NOT auto-infer missing format hints from the property name or type (reserved for an LLM-inferrer milestone), does NOT generate downstream SDK input-validator stubs (reserved for an SDK-validator-generator milestone), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M44's `capability.description` word-count tier rules, does NOT walk per-property format-hint beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Hint-All-Properties-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property format-hint under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].format` (reserved for `M-Hint-Nested-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property format-hint (reserved for `M-Hint-Output-First-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT bucket on the specific format-hint string value (`email` vs `date` vs `uri` vs `uuid` — reserved for `M-Hint-Format-Value-Distribution-Index-1`), does NOT compute statistical aggregates, does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` into `tusq.manifest.json`.
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Hint-All-Properties-Format-Hint-Index-1` (per-property fine-grained format-hint distribution rather than capability-level first-property axis), `M-Hint-Nested-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1` (recursive walk of nested-object property format-hint under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].format`), `M-Hint-Output-First-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1` (output-side analog), `M-Hint-Format-Value-Distribution-Index-1` (per-FIRST-property format-string-value distribution: `email` vs `date` vs `uri` vs `uuid` vs `date-time` vs ...; reports the specific value rather than presence), `M-Hint-Format-Validator-Crossref-1` (cross-reference declared format hint vs runtime validator presence in the source code), `M-Hint-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Hint-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest first-property format hint and published OpenAPI/SDK docs), `M-Hint-LLM-Format-Inferrer-1` (LLM-based format-hint inferrer for `unhinted`-bucket capabilities — explicitly out of M53's scope, which is read-only reviewer aid only), `M-Hint-SDK-Validator-Generator-1` (downstream SDK input-validator-stub generator that consumes M53's index as input).

- [x] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_FORMAT_HINT_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `hinted | unhinted | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_FORMAT_HINT_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `format_hint | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_FORMAT_HINT_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `hinted → unhinted → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M52 input-schema-first-property-description-presence constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M52 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHint(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.format` is present (key exists with non-`undefined` value) AND `typeof firstVal.format !== 'string'`; returns `'unhinted'` if `firstVal.format` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR is a string with `.trim().length === 0`; otherwise returns `'hinted'`.
- [x] Add `cmdHint` dispatcher and `cmdHintIndex(args)` command handler in `src/cli.js`. The handler MUST: (a) call `parseHintIndexArgs` first; (b) detect `--out` path validity (rejected: paths under `.tusq/`, paths in non-existent or non-writable parent directories) BEFORE reading the manifest, with stderr `--out path must not be inside .tusq/` or `--out parent directory does not exist or is not writable` and exit 1, empty stdout; (c) read and parse the manifest from `--manifest <path>` (default `tusq.manifest.json` in cwd) with stderr `Manifest file not found: <path>` for missing, `Failed to parse manifest JSON: <message>` for malformed, `Manifest is missing capabilities array` for missing/non-array `capabilities` field, each exit 1 with empty stdout; (d) apply case-sensitive `--hint <value>` filter against the closed four-value enum, with stderr `Unknown input schema first property format hint: <value>` and exit 1 for any value not in the enum; (e) build the index via `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndex(manifest)`; (f) emit per-capability malformed warnings to stderr in human mode (one `Warning: capability '<name>' has malformed input_schema first property format hint (<reason>)` per malformed capability) and to `warnings[]` in `--json` mode; (g) format via `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndex` (human) or `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` (`--json`).
- [x] Add `parseHintIndexArgs(args)` accepting exactly four flags: `--hint <value>`, `--manifest <path>`, `--out <path>`, `--json`. Reject any unknown flag with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>` and exit 1, empty stdout. Reject `--hint` with no value (stderr `--hint requires a value`), `--manifest` with no value, `--out` with no value, each exit 1.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndex(manifest, options = {})` returning `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_format_hints, warnings }`. Iteration: for each capability in `manifest.capabilities[]` (preserving manifest declared order), compute `bucket = classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHint(capability.input_schema)`; if bucket is `'unknown'`, append a warning entry of the form `{ capability: <name>, reason: <one of five frozen reason codes> }`. Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`hinted → unhinted → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Each per-bucket entry contains the frozen 8 fields (`input_schema_first_property_format_hint`, `aggregation_key` (`format_hint` for the two presence buckets, `not_applicable` for not_applicable, `unknown` for unknown), `capability_count`, `capabilities[]` of capability names in manifest order, `approved_count` (count of `approved === true`), `gated_count` (count of `approved !== true`), `has_destructive_side_effect` (true iff any capability in bucket has `side_effect_class === 'destructive'`), `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` (true iff any capability in bucket has `sensitivity_class === 'restricted'` or `sensitivity_class === 'confidential'`)). `warnings[]` is always present (empty `[]` when no malformed capabilities) for `--json` shape stability. Field name is `first_property_format_hints` (plural categorical, matching M48/M51/M52's `first_property_<axis>` precedent verbatim).
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndex(index)` returning a human-readable multi-line string with: `Input Schema First Property Format Hint Index — <manifest_path>` header; per-bucket section showing `<HINT>: <capability_count> capabilit{y|ies}` followed by indented capability names and `(approved: <n>, gated: <n>, destructive: <yes|no>, restricted_or_confidential: <yes|no>)` summary line; `Planning aid: this index reports per-capability input_schema.properties[firstKey].format JSON-Schema-format-hint presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, validate runtime payload conformance against the declared format, cross-reference manifest first-property format vs published OpenAPI/SDK docs, infer missing format hints, or auto-flip tusq serve filtering. Bucket order is deterministic stable-output ordering only (NOT knowledge-artifact-readiness-ranked, NOT reviewer-priority-ranked, NOT UX-affordance-completeness-ranked, NOT help-flow-readiness-ranked, NOT SDK-validator-readiness-ranked, NOT onboarding-clarity-ranked, NOT OpenAPI-conformance-ranked).` callout at the bottom; `Bucket rule: hinted (firstKey.format is a non-empty trimmed string) | unhinted (firstKey.format missing/null/undefined or empty trimmed string) | not_applicable (input_schema.type !== 'object' or zero-property object) | unknown (malformed input_schema or firstKey not a plain object or firstKey.format present but non-string).` line; `Bucket order: hinted → unhinted → not_applicable → unknown` line.
- [x] Wire `hint` into the `dispatch` table in `src/cli.js` between `gloss` and `input` (alphabetical in dispatch ordering). The `hint` enumerator (no subcommand) prints a help block listing `tusq hint index` and exits 0.
- [x] Update `printHelp()` to emit the 37-command CLI surface (init, scan, manifest, compile, serve, review, docs, approve, auth, binding, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, gloss, **hint**, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version, help) with `hint` inserted between `gloss` and `input` in the post-`docs` alphabetical block.
- [x] Update `printCommandHelp()` to add `hint` and `hint index` entries with: command shape `tusq hint index [--hint <value>] [--manifest <path>] [--out <path>] [--json]`; the four-flag table; the closed four-value bucket-key enum; the closed three-value aggregation_key enum; the frozen classifier function; the bucket iteration order; the planning-aid framing callout; the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--hint` rule; the explicit notes that the input-side first-property format-hint axis is distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level description word count via `tusq description index`), M47 (input_schema.properties count cardinality via `tusq parameter index`), M49 (input-side first-property primitive type via `tusq signature index`), M50 (input-side first-property required-status via `tusq obligation index`), M51 (input-side first-property HTTP-source locus via `tusq binding index`), M52 (input-side first-property description-presence via `tusq gloss index`), and M48 (output-side first-property type via `tusq shape index`); that the all-property format-hint distribution is reserved for the deferred `M-Hint-All-Properties-Format-Hint-Index-1` successor; that nested-property format-hint recursion is reserved for `M-Hint-Nested-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`; that the output-side analog is reserved for `M-Hint-Output-First-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`; that the per-FIRST-property format-string-value distribution (email vs date vs uri etc.) is reserved for `M-Hint-Format-Value-Distribution-Index-1`; that runtime payload conformance crosscheck is reserved for `M-Hint-Format-Validator-Crossref-1`; that LLM-inference is reserved for `M-Hint-LLM-Format-Inferrer-1`; that doc-contradiction detection is reserved for `M-Hint-Doc-Contradiction-1`.
