(b) `--hint hinted` (exit 1, absent bucket); (c) `--hint unhinted`; (d) `--hint not_applicable`; (e) `--hint unknown` (synthetic fixture with malformed input_schema.properties.first.format=42); (f) `--hint HINTED` (exit 1, case-sensitive); (g) `--hint Unhinted` (exit 1); (h) `--hint xyz` (exit 1, unknown); (i) `--manifest <missing>` (exit 1); (j) `--manifest <malformed.json>` (exit 1); (k) `--manifest <no-capabilities>` (exit 1); (l) `--unknown-flag` (exit 1); (m) `--hint` with no value (exit 1); (n) `--out <valid path>` writes correctly and stdout is empty; (o) `--out .tusq/foo.json` (exit 1); (p) `--out <unwritable parent>` (exit 1); (q) `--json` outputs valid JSON with `first_property_format_hints[]` and `warnings: []` (or with warnings populated when malformed); (r) determinism: three consecutive runs produce byte-identical stdout AND manifest mtime + SHA-256 + per-capability `capability_digest` invariant pre/post AND `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` MUST NOT appear in `tusq.manifest.json` after run (non-persistence) AND `tusq compile` byte-identical pre/post AND all 23 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post; (s) malformed capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed input_schema first property format hint (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all five frozen reason codes (`input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present`) AND empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_format_hints: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (t) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.format = ''` (empty string) → bucket `unhinted` with NO warning; (u) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.format = '   '` (whitespace-only string) → bucket `unhinted` with NO warning (trim semantics); (v) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.format = ['email','date']` (array) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present`; (w) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.format = {kind:'email'}` (object) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present`; (x) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.format = null` → bucket `unhinted` with NO warning (null is treated identically to missing). Update existing M35–M52 help-count assertions from 36 to 37.
- [x] Add `input-schema-first-property-format-hint-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval count 43 → 44). Add `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndexDeterminismScenario` handler to `tests/eval-regression.mjs` verifying byte-identical output across three runs, closed four-value bucket-key enum, closed three-value aggregation_key enum, bucket order determinism (`hinted → unhinted → not_applicable → unknown`), `warnings[]` always present, manifest non-mutation, `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` non-persistence, and the trim-empty-counts-as-unhinted contract.
- [x] Update `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with the dev-turn M53 entry (challenge to PM turn, implementation details, verification evidence: `npm test` exit 0 with 44 scenarios, zero new dependencies, zero package drift, 37-command CLI surface confirmed, all M53 invariants verified).
- [x] Add `### M53` detail block to `SYSTEM_SPEC.md` with: purpose statement, command shape, frozen four-value `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` bucket-key enum, frozen three-value `aggregation_key` enum, frozen classifier-function rules, frozen 8-field per-bucket entry shape, top-level `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, closed-enum bucket iteration order rule (`hinted → unhinted → not_applicable → unknown`), within-bucket manifest-declared-order rule, case-sensitive `--hint` filter rule, empty-capabilities and stdout-discipline rules, read-only invariants including the explicit non-persistence-of-`input_schema_first_property_format_hint` rule, the no-all-properties-format-walking rule, the no-nested-format-recursion rule, the no-output-side-format rule, the no-format-value-distribution rule, the no-runtime-payload-validation rule, the no-LLM-inference rule, and the explicit non-runtime-validator / non-doc-contradiction-detector / non-format-value-distributor / non-LLM-inferrer / non-SDK-validator-generator boundary statement.
- [x] Add `### M53` Product CLI Surface block to `command-surface.md` with the two-row command table, four-flag table, bucket-key enum table, aggregation_key enum table, classifier-function rules table, per-bucket entry shape table, bucket iteration order table, default-preservation table for the 36 unchanged commands, failure UX table, and local-only invariants table.
- [x] Add Constraint 46 to SYSTEM_SPEC.md Constraints section: "`tusq hint index` is a planning aid that surfaces the per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].format` JSON-Schema-format-hint presence classification (whether the FIRST input property carries a non-empty trimmed format-hint string, is unhinted, is not-applicable, or is unknown) and the cross-axis side-effect/sensitivity exposure of each presence bucket. It does NOT execute capability invocations at runtime, does NOT validate runtime payload conformance against the declared format (deferred to `M-Hint-Format-Validator-Crossref-1`), does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property format vs published OpenAPI/SDK docs (deferred to `M-Hint-Doc-Contradiction-1`), does NOT auto-infer missing format hints (deferred to `M-Hint-LLM-Format-Inferrer-1`), does NOT bucket on the specific format-string value (`email` vs `date` vs `uri` vs `uuid` — deferred to `M-Hint-Format-Value-Distribution-Index-1`), does NOT generate downstream SDK input-validator stubs (deferred to `M-Hint-SDK-Validator-Generator-1`), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M44's `capability.description` word-count tier rules, does NOT walk per-property format-hint beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Hint-All-Properties-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property format-hint under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].format` (reserved for `M-Hint-Nested-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property format-hint (reserved for `M-Hint-Output-First-Property-Format-Hint-Index-1`), does NOT compute statistical aggregates, does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` into `tusq.manifest.json`. Subsequent milestones ship under their own ROADMAP entries with fresh acceptance contracts and fresh re-approval expectations. The four-value `input_schema_first_property_format_hint` bucket-key enum (`hinted | unhinted | not_applicable | unknown`), the three-value `aggregation_key` enum (`format_hint | not_applicable | unknown`), and the five-value warning reason-code enum are frozen; any addition is a material governance event. The `--hint` filter is case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case filter values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property format hint:`. Malformed `input_schema`/malformed-firstVal-descriptor/present-but-non-string-`format` values are bucketed as `unknown` and emit a `Warning: ...` to stderr (human) or `warnings[]` (`--json`); the `not_applicable` bucket and the `hinted`/`unhinted` buckets are valid named outcomes and emit NO warning. A present-but-empty (or whitespace-only) `format` string is `unhinted`, NOT a malformation: an empty format hint is a knowledge-artifact-rendering signal, not a typing failure."
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq hint index` command, the four-value bucket-key enum, the three-value aggregation_key enum, the frozen classifier function, the four flags, exit codes, the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--hint` rule, the empty-capabilities behavior, the `warnings[]` rule with five frozen reason codes, the explicit "planning aid, not a runtime payload validator, doc-contradiction detector, format-value distributor, LLM inferrer, or SDK validator generator" callout, and the explicit M52-vs-M53 distinction (M52 first-property docstring-presence via `tusq gloss index`, M53 first-property format-hint presence via `tusq hint index` — different fields).

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Reviewers conflate M53 first-property format-hint presence with M49 first-property primitive type | Operator could assume `tusq hint index` is a re-bucketing of `tusq signature index` (M49, primitive type like `string | number | boolean`) rather than a separate JSON-Schema `format`-hint axis (which is an annotation on top of `string`-typed properties to declare a formatting convention like `email`, `date`, `uri`) | M49 reads `firstKey.type` (primitive-type categorical: `string | number | integer | boolean | null | object | array | not_applicable | unknown`); M53 reads `firstKey.format` (a JSON-Schema string annotation declaring a formatting convention on top of the primitive type — a `string`-typed property may carry `format: 'email'` to indicate it expects an email-formatted string, but the `type` is still `'string'`). The two axes share the firstKey selection but operate on different fields entirely. CLI help text for `tusq hint index` explicitly states "this index reports firstKey's JSON-Schema format-hint presence (the `.format` annotation on the FIRST input property), distinct from M49's primitive-type classification (`tusq signature index`) which reads `.type`." Smoke case (a) cross-verifies on the canonical fixture that the two axes do not overlap. |
| Empty-string and whitespace-only `format` silently bucket as `unknown` instead of `unhinted` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid-but-empty format-hint capabilities, masking real format-malformation cases AND inflating the malformed-warning count | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'unhinted'` (no warning) when `firstVal.format` is missing/`null`/`undefined` OR a string with `.trim().length === 0` (mirrors M52's empty-string-counts-as-absent precedent verbatim). The `unknown` bucket is reserved for present-but-non-string `format` values (array, plain object, number, boolean) — those ARE malformations of the JSON-Schema `format` field. Smoke cases (t), (u), (x) verify empty-string, whitespace-only, and null all bucket as `unhinted` with NO warning; smoke cases (v), (w) verify array and object format hints bucket as `unknown` with the fifth warning code. |
| Present-but-non-string `format` (e.g., array of strings, object with kind) silently coerces into `hinted` or `unhinted` | A reviewer evaluating a capability with a mistakenly-typed format (e.g., array form) would see it bucket as `hinted` (because the array is truthy) or `unhinted` (because the array is not a string), masking a real schema malformation that downstream SDK validator-generators would choke on | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'unknown'` with reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_format_invalid_when_present` when `firstVal.format` is PRESENT but `typeof !== 'string'`. The strict-typing rule mirrors M49/M50/M51/M52's strict-typing precedent: a present-but-malformed value is `unknown`, not silently coerced. Smoke cases (v), (w) explicitly assert array and object format hints bucket as `unknown` with the fifth warning code. |
| `unhinted` bucket misread as "this capability has no format hints at all" rather than "the first property is unhinted" | Reviewer sees a capability in the `unhinted` bucket and assumes the entire input schema lacks format hints; in fact only the FIRST property is checked | M53 measures ONLY the FIRST property's `format` field. A capability with `properties = {a: {type:'string'}, b: {type:'string', format:'email'}}` buckets as `unhinted` (firstKey `a` has no format) even though the schema has a fully-format-hinted second property. CLI help, SYSTEM_SPEC § M53, and the format callout all explicitly say "first property's format hint, not the capability's overall format-hint coverage." The all-property format-hint distribution is reserved for `M-Hint-All-Properties-Format-Hint-Index-1`. |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | If the implementer sorts keys or uses Map iteration with re-ordered keys, the FIRST property selected may differ across runs, breaking byte-identity | The classifier MUST use `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` directly; MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence; MUST rely on JSON.parse's insertion-order-preserving behavior. Determinism eval scenario explicitly verifies byte-identical output across three runs on a fixture with `properties` keys ordered `{c, a, b}` where `c` is unhinted and `a` is `format:'email'`-hinted to confirm the selected first-key's format-hint presence matches insertion-order index 0, not the sorted-key index 0 (which would flip the bucket from `unhinted` to `hinted`). |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Reviewer would see false "unknown" warnings for valid array-typed input capabilities, masking real malformations | Classifier rule explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) when `input_schema.type` is a string other than `'object'`; only missing-or-non-string `type` returns `'unknown'`. Smoke case (matching M49/M50/M51/M52 precedent) includes a synthetic fixture with `input_schema={type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}` asserting `not_applicable` with no warning. |
| Bucket order described as "knowledge-artifact-readiness-ranked" or "OpenAPI-conformance-ranked" | Reviewers misread the index as a knowledge-artifact-readiness gauge or SDK-validator-priority hint, leading to false confidence that `hinted`-bucket capabilities are "knowledge-artifact-ready" while `unhinted`-bucket capabilities are blockers | Bucket iteration order is explicitly framed as a deterministic stable-output convention only; SYSTEM_SPEC § M53, Constraint 46, and `manifest-format.md` carry this disclaimer; the `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyFormatHintIndex` callout reinforces "deterministic stable-output ordering only, NOT knowledge-artifact-readiness-ranked, NOT reviewer-priority-ranked, NOT UX-affordance-completeness-ranked, NOT help-flow-readiness-ranked, NOT SDK-validator-readiness-ranked, NOT onboarding-clarity-ranked, NOT OpenAPI-conformance-ranked." Cross-axis derivations are reserved for `M-Hint-Format-Validator-Crossref-1`. |
| `--hint` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operator typing `--hint HINTED` would unexpectedly succeed and the index output would mislead the operator about case-sensitivity contract | `--hint` is case-sensitive lowercase-only (mirrors M35–M52 case-sensitivity precedent); uppercase or mixed-case values exit 1 with `Unknown input schema first property format hint:`. Empty-string `--hint ''` exits 1. Smoke case (f) asserts `--hint HINTED` exits 1; smoke case (g) asserts `--hint Unhinted` exits 1; smoke case (h) covers arbitrary-string fallback. |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `hint` inserted in the wrong position would break `printHelp()` byte-identity for the existing 36-command surface | Alphabetical insertion verified: `gloss` < `hint` (`g` (103) < `h` (104) at position 0); `hint` < `input` (`h` (104) < `i` (105) at position 0); insertion sequence in `printHelp()` is `..., gloss, hint, input, items, method, ...`; smoke test asserts grep `-c` of `^hint$` in help output is 1 and total command-line count is 37. |
| Format-value distribution or runtime-validator scope creep | An implementer could read the `hinted` bucket as a natural place to bucketize on the specific format string (`email` vs `date` vs `uri`) inline, or could call a runtime validator to assert that the declared format actually validates against a sample payload | M53 emits ONLY the per-bucket 8-field entry shape with the binary presence label and cross-axis flags; it does NOT bucketize on specific format-string values, does NOT call any validator, does NOT execute capability invocations. Format-value distribution is explicitly deferred to `M-Hint-Format-Value-Distribution-Index-1`; runtime-validator crosscheck is deferred to `M-Hint-Format-Validator-Crossref-1`. SYSTEM_SPEC § M53 and Constraint 46 codify these scope boundaries. |

### M54: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Enum Constraint Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.35 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_ca31318ae2693a36` (`turn_61989ef8c7d5bad7`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `530ce1a` from intake charter `intent_1777376262688_b03b` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M53: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Format Hint Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.34 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 218–227 (`### Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy; M51 cites § Skills; M52 cites § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge; M53 cites § Knowledge Artifacts). The section reads: "tusq.dev should generate framework-agnostic embeddable surfaces: action widgets for workflows such as refund, assign, create, update, upgrade, suspend, or invite; insight widgets for summaries, anomalies, recommendations, and next-best actions; command palette / Ctrl+K style surfaces; script-tag or web-component style embeds with config." The per-`input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` capability roll-up answers the embeddable-widget-rendering question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, does the FIRST input parameter declare a JSON-Schema `enum` (closed-vocabulary) constraint that an embeddable action widget, command-palette surface, or insight-widget composer can render as a dropdown / radio-set / chip-picker affordance — or is the FIRST input parameter unenumerated (forcing the widget composer to fall back to a free-form text input or numeric stepper), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — there is no first property to enumerate), or unknown (malformed)?" An action widget rendering a `suspend_user.reason` capability whose first input parameter is `enumerated` (`enum: ['policy_violation', 'fraud', 'duplicate_account']`) can render a three-option radio-set with explicit choice labels; a command-palette surface composing the same capability whose first parameter is `unenumerated` MUST flag the capability as a widget-composition gap that blocks safe automated drop-down generation. The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `unenumerated` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose primary input slot lacks an enum vocabulary exposes a "widget-composition gap on a high-stakes capability" review obligation that embeddable-widget-compiler reviewers MUST surface. Distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level `description` word count); distinct from M49 (firstKey `.type` JSON-Schema primitive type); distinct from M50 (firstKey ∈ required[] membership); distinct from M51 (firstKey `.source` HTTP-locus); distinct from M52 (firstKey `.description` docstring presence); distinct from M53 (firstKey `.format` JSON-Schema format-hint presence). M54 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].enum` (the FIRST input property's JSON-Schema enum-constraint array) — a different field entirely from M49/M50/M51/M52/M53. The first milestone to use § Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 193–204 (`### Brand-Matched Chat Interface`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the chat-affordance-rendering framing item — chat interfaces benefit from rendering enum-constrained parameters as inline pill / chip / quick-reply affordances rather than free-text prompts. Re-cited only — M54's primary source is § Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `enumerated | unenumerated | not_applicable | unknown`. `enumerated` denotes capabilities whose `input_schema.properties[firstKey].enum` is present and is a non-empty array (`Array.isArray(value) === true && value.length >= 1`). `unenumerated` denotes capabilities whose `firstKey.enum` is missing OR `null` OR `undefined` (semantically absent — there is no enum constraint declared). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — paralleling M49/M50/M51/M52/M53's `not_applicable` semantics for the same shape. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), `properties[firstKey]` not a plain non-null object, OR `properties[firstKey].enum` PRESENT but not an array (e.g., string, plain object, number, boolean — strict-typing rule: a present-but-non-array enum is malformed, NOT silently coerced into `unenumerated`), OR `properties[firstKey].enum` PRESENT and is an array of length 0 (an empty enum is invalid JSON-Schema — JSON-Schema requires `enum` to declare at least one allowed value; empty-array semantics deliberately diverges from M52/M53's empty-string-counts-as-absent precedent because an empty enum array is a typing failure rather than a meaningful absence signal). The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `enum_constraint | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the two presence buckets (`enumerated`, `unenumerated`) carry aggregation_key `enum_constraint`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48/M49/M50/M51/M52/M53 first-property-axis pattern: a single semantic-aggregation key for the "real" buckets, and two reserved keys for the absent-target and malformed cases.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` (e.g., `'array'`, `'string'`, `'number'`, etc.) — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid (zero-property object input has no first property to evaluate); no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid`, mirroring M52/M53's 6th-code-via-shared-descriptor-malformation pattern). If `firstVal.enum` is PRESENT (i.e., `firstVal` has the property `enum` AND its value is not `undefined` AND not `null`) but `Array.isArray(firstVal.enum) !== true`, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`). If `firstVal.enum` is PRESENT and is an array with `firstVal.enum.length === 0`, return `'unknown'` (same warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`). If `firstVal.enum` is missing/`null`/`undefined`, return `'unenumerated'` (no warning — absent enum declares "no closed vocabulary," which is a widget-composition signal, not a malformation). Otherwise (`firstVal.enum` is a non-empty array) return `'enumerated'`.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order via `JSON.parse`. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M53 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-descriptor / malformed-first-property-`enum` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Five frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`. The `not_applicable` bucket (non-object input or zero-property object) does NOT emit a warning. The `enumerated` and `unenumerated` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes; an absent enum is a widget-composition signal, NOT a malformation. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings, and BOTH the present-but-non-array `enum` value AND the present-but-empty-array `enum` value (the only two malformations specific to this axis) share the fifth code `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present` (one code spans both shapes — distinct from the M53 pattern where the format-hint axis had only one malformation shape on the `format` field). Note: malformed-`firstVal-not-an-object` falls under `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` which is the `unknown`-bucket reason already covered as a 6th-code-via-shared-descriptor-malformation pattern (mirrors M52/M53); the fifth code in the PM-frozen list is reserved exclusively for the `enum`-field malformations (non-array OR empty-array). No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a widget-composition-readiness ranking, NOT a reviewer-priority ranking, NOT a UX-affordance-completeness ranking, NOT a chat-affordance-readiness ranking, NOT a command-palette-readiness ranking, NOT an action-widget-readiness ranking, NOT a closed-vocabulary-coverage ranking): `enumerated → unenumerated → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional "enum-constrained-first, free-form-second, irrelevant-third, malformed-last" reading order for embeddable-widget-composition review aids. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--choice` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `ENUMERATED`, `Unenumerated`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property enum constraint: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M53 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property enum constraint: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M53): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 24 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`, `tusq binding index`, `tusq gloss index`, `tusq hint index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 37 → 38 commands. New top-level noun `choice` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `binding` and `confidence` in the `printHelp()` display block (the post-`docs` alphabetical block currently runs `approve, auth, binding, confidence, description, diff, domain, effect, examples, gloss, hint, input, items, method, obligation, output, parameter, path, pii, policy, redaction, request, response, sensitivity, shape, signature, strictness, surface, version`; `binding` < `choice` because at position 0 `b` (98) < `c` (99); `choice` < `confidence` because at position 0 both are `c` (99 = 99) AND at position 1 `h` (104) < `o` (111)). Insertion sequence: ..., `binding`, `choice`, `confidence`, `description`, ... The fresh noun `choice` (rather than a sibling subcommand under `signature`, `gloss`, `hint`, or `binding`) preserves the M31–M53 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `choice` (a closed vocabulary of allowed values declared via JSON-Schema `enum` — the choice-set the operator may pick from) is intentional and is distinct from `description` (M44, capability-level top-level description word count), `signature` (M49, input_schema.properties[firstKey].type primitive type), `obligation` (M50, input_schema.properties[firstKey] required-status), `binding` (M51, input_schema.properties[firstKey].source HTTP-locus), `gloss` (M52, input_schema.properties[firstKey].description docstring presence), `hint` (M53, input_schema.properties[firstKey].format JSON-Schema format-hint presence), and `parameter` (M47, input_schema.properties count tier — cardinality) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter enum-constraint (closed-vocabulary) presence.
> - **Result-array field name** `first_property_enum_constraints` (plural categorical with closed bucket-key family; the input-side scope is carried by the per-bucket field name `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` rather than the result-array field name; matches M48/M51/M52/M53's `first_property_<axis>` plural categorical naming convention verbatim).
> - **Eval scenario**: `input-schema-first-property-enum-constraint-index-determinism` added; eval harness 44 → ≥45 scenarios.
> - **Constraint 47** to be added in SYSTEM_SPEC § Constraints freezing: M54 is a planning aid that reports per-capability `input_schema.properties[firstKey].enum` JSON-Schema-enum-constraint presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, does NOT validate runtime payloads against the declared enum vocabulary (e.g., does NOT verify that an `enum: ['a','b','c']`-constrained parameter actually receives one of those values at runtime), does NOT cross-reference manifest first-property enum vs published OpenAPI/SDK signature enum (deferred to a doc-contradiction milestone), does NOT auto-infer missing enum vocabularies from observed runtime values (reserved for an LLM-inferrer milestone), does NOT generate downstream SDK input-validator stubs (reserved for an SDK-validator-generator milestone), does NOT auto-flip `tusq serve` filtering or approval gates, does NOT alter M11/M14's canonical input_schema extraction rules, does NOT alter M44's `capability.description` word-count tier rules, does NOT walk per-property enum beyond the FIRST property (per-property fine-grained granularity reserved for `M-Choice-All-Properties-Enum-Constraint-Index-1`), does NOT inspect nested-object property enum under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].enum` (reserved for `M-Choice-Nested-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1`), does NOT classify the OUTPUT-side first-property enum (reserved for `M-Choice-Output-First-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1`), does NOT bucket on the specific enum-vocabulary cardinality tier (1-value vs 2–4-value vs 5+-value — reserved for `M-Choice-Enum-Cardinality-Distribution-Index-1`), does NOT compute statistical aggregates, does NOT persist `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` into `tusq.manifest.json`.
> - **Deferred successor milestones**: `M-Choice-All-Properties-Enum-Constraint-Index-1` (per-property fine-grained enum-constraint distribution rather than capability-level first-property axis), `M-Choice-Nested-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1` (recursive walk of nested-object property enum under `input_schema.properties[<key>].properties[<nestedKey>].enum`), `M-Choice-Output-First-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1` (output-side analog), `M-Choice-Enum-Cardinality-Distribution-Index-1` (per-FIRST-property enum-array length tier: 1-value vs 2–4-value vs 5+-value), `M-Choice-Enum-Value-Type-Index-1` (per-FIRST-property enum-element type homogeneity: all-strings vs all-numbers vs mixed), `M-Choice-Enum-Validator-Crossref-1` (cross-reference declared enum vs runtime validator enforcement in the source code), `M-Choice-Persistence-1` (if persistence is later authorized), `M-Choice-Doc-Contradiction-1` (cross-source contradiction between manifest first-property enum and published OpenAPI/SDK docs), `M-Choice-LLM-Enum-Inferrer-1` (LLM-based enum-vocabulary inferrer for `unenumerated`-bucket capabilities — explicitly out of M54's scope, which is read-only reviewer aid only), `M-Choice-SDK-Picker-Generator-1` (downstream SDK / embeddable-widget dropdown-picker stub generator that consumes M54's index as input).

- [x] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_ENUM_CONSTRAINT_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `enumerated | unenumerated | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_ENUM_CONSTRAINT_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `enum_constraint | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_ENUM_CONSTRAINT_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `enumerated → unenumerated → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M53 input-schema-first-property-format-hint constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M53 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraint(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.enum` is present (key exists with non-`undefined` AND non-`null` value) AND `Array.isArray(firstVal.enum) !== true`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.enum` is present AND is an array AND `firstVal.enum.length === 0`; returns `'unenumerated'` if `firstVal.enum` is missing/`null`/`undefined`; otherwise returns `'enumerated'` (firstVal.enum is a non-empty array).
- [x] Add `cmdChoice` dispatcher and `cmdChoiceIndex(args)` command handler in `src/cli.js`. The handler MUST: (a) call `parseChoiceIndexArgs` first; (b) detect `--out` path validity (rejected: paths under `.tusq/`, paths in non-existent or non-writable parent directories) BEFORE reading the manifest, with stderr `--out path must not be inside .tusq/` or `--out parent directory does not exist or is not writable` and exit 1, empty stdout; (c) read and parse the manifest from `--manifest <path>` (default `tusq.manifest.json` in cwd) with stderr `Manifest file not found: <path>` for missing, `Failed to parse manifest JSON: <message>` for malformed, `Manifest is missing capabilities array` for missing/non-array `capabilities` field, each exit 1 with empty stdout; (d) apply case-sensitive `--choice <value>` filter against the closed four-value enum, with stderr `Unknown input schema first property enum constraint: <value>` and exit 1 for any value not in the enum; (e) build the index via `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintIndex(manifest)`; (f) emit per-capability malformed warnings to stderr in human mode (one `Warning: capability '<name>' has malformed input_schema first property enum constraint (<reason>)` per malformed capability) and to `warnings[]` in `--json` mode; (g) format via `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintIndex` (human) or `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` (`--json`).
- [x] Add `parseChoiceIndexArgs(args)` accepting exactly four flags: `--choice <value>`, `--manifest <path>`, `--out <path>`, `--json`. Reject any unknown flag with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>` and exit 1, empty stdout. Reject `--choice` with no value (stderr `--choice requires a value`), `--manifest` with no value, `--out` with no value, each exit 1.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintIndex(manifest, options = {})` returning `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_enum_constraints, warnings }`. Iteration: for each capability in `manifest.capabilities[]` (preserving manifest declared order), compute `bucket = classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraint(capability.input_schema)`; if bucket is `'unknown'`, append a warning entry of the form `{ capability: <name>, reason: <one of five frozen reason codes> }`. Group capabilities by bucket, then emit buckets in closed-enum order (`enumerated → unenumerated → not_applicable → unknown`). Empty buckets MUST NOT appear. Each per-bucket entry contains the frozen 8 fields (`input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint`, `aggregation_key` (`enum_constraint` for the two presence buckets, `not_applicable` for not_applicable, `unknown` for unknown), `capability_count`, `capabilities[]` of capability names in manifest order, `approved_count` (count of `approved === true`), `gated_count` (count of `approved !== true`), `has_destructive_side_effect` (true iff any capability in bucket has `side_effect_class === 'destructive'`), `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` (true iff any capability in bucket has `sensitivity_class === 'restricted'` or `sensitivity_class === 'confidential'`)). `warnings[]` is always present (empty `[]` when no malformed capabilities) for `--json` shape stability. Field name is `first_property_enum_constraints` (plural categorical, matching M48/M51/M52/M53's `first_property_<axis>` precedent verbatim).
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintIndex(index)` returning a human-readable multi-line string with: `Input Schema First Property Enum Constraint Index — <manifest_path>` header; per-bucket section showing `<CONSTRAINT>: <capability_count> capabilit{y|ies}` followed by indented capability names and `(approved: <n>, gated: <n>, destructive: <yes|no>, restricted_or_confidential: <yes|no>)` summary line; `Planning aid: this index reports per-capability input_schema.properties[firstKey].enum JSON-Schema-enum-constraint presence classification; it does NOT execute capability invocations, validate runtime payloads against the declared enum vocabulary, cross-reference manifest first-property enum vs published OpenAPI/SDK docs, infer missing enum vocabularies, or auto-flip tusq serve filtering. Bucket order is deterministic stable-output ordering only (NOT widget-composition-readiness-ranked, NOT reviewer-priority-ranked, NOT UX-affordance-completeness-ranked, NOT chat-affordance-readiness-ranked, NOT command-palette-readiness-ranked, NOT action-widget-readiness-ranked, NOT closed-vocabulary-coverage-ranked).` callout at the bottom; `Bucket rule: enumerated (firstKey.enum is a non-empty array) | unenumerated (firstKey.enum missing/null/undefined) | not_applicable (input_schema.type !== 'object' or zero-property object) | unknown (malformed input_schema or firstKey not a plain object or firstKey.enum present but not an array or present-but-empty array).` line; `Bucket order: enumerated → unenumerated → not_applicable → unknown` line.

- [x] Update `printCommandHelp()` to add `choice` and `choice index` entries with: command shape `tusq choice index [--choice <value>] [--manifest <path>] [--out <path>] [--json]`; the four-flag table; the closed four-value bucket-key enum; the closed three-value aggregation_key enum; the frozen classifier function; the bucket iteration order; the planning-aid framing callout; the case-sensitive lowercase-only `--choice` rule; the explicit notes that the input-side first-property enum-constraint axis is distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level description word count via `tusq description index`), M47 (input_schema.properties count cardinality via `tusq parameter index`), M49 (input-side first-property primitive type via `tusq signature index`), M50 (input-side first-property required-status via `tusq obligation index`), M51 (input-side first-property HTTP-source locus via `tusq binding index`), M52 (input-side first-property description-presence via `tusq gloss index`), M53 (input-side first-property format-hint presence via `tusq hint index`), and M48 (output-side first-property type via `tusq shape index`); that the all-property enum-constraint distribution is reserved for the deferred `M-Choice-All-Properties-Enum-Constraint-Index-1` successor; that nested-property enum recursion is reserved for `M-Choice-Nested-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1`; that the output-side analog is reserved for `M-Choice-Output-First-Property-Enum-Constraint-Index-1`; that the per-FIRST-property enum-cardinality tier (1 vs 2–4 vs 5+) is reserved for `M-Choice-Enum-Cardinality-Distribution-Index-1`; that runtime-payload-against-enum crosscheck is reserved for `M-Choice-Enum-Validator-Crossref-1`; that LLM-inference is reserved for `M-Choice-LLM-Enum-Inferrer-1`; that doc-contradiction detection is reserved for `M-Choice-Doc-Contradiction-1`.

(b) `--choice enumerated` (exit 1, absent bucket); (c) `--choice unenumerated`; (d) `--choice not_applicable`; (e) `--choice unknown` (synthetic fixture with malformed input_schema.properties.first.enum=42); (f) `--choice ENUMERATED` (exit 1, case-sensitive); (g) `--choice Unenumerated` (exit 1); (h) `--choice xyz` (exit 1, unknown); (i) `--manifest <missing>` (exit 1); (j) `--manifest <malformed.json>` (exit 1); (k) `--manifest <no-capabilities>` (exit 1); (l) `--unknown-flag` (exit 1); (m) `--choice` with no value (exit 1); (n) `--out <valid path>` writes correctly and stdout is empty; (o) `--out .tusq/foo.json` (exit 1); (p) `--out <unwritable parent>` (exit 1); (q) `--json` outputs valid JSON with `first_property_enum_constraints[]` and `warnings: []` (or with warnings populated when malformed); (r) determinism: three consecutive runs produce byte-identical stdout AND manifest mtime + SHA-256 + per-capability `capability_digest` invariant pre/post AND `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` MUST NOT appear in `tusq.manifest.json` after run (non-persistence) AND `tusq compile` byte-identical pre/post AND all 24 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post; (s) malformed capability produces stderr `Warning: capability '...' has malformed input_schema first property enum constraint (<reason>)` in human mode and matching `warnings[]` entry in `--json` mode covering all five frozen reason codes (`input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`) AND empty-capabilities exit 0 with stdout `No capabilities in manifest — nothing to index.` (human) or `{ manifest_path, manifest_version, generated_at, first_property_enum_constraints: [], warnings: [] }` (`--json`); (t) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.enum = []` (empty array) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present` (deliberate divergence from M52/M53's empty-counts-as-absent precedent — empty enum is malformed JSON-Schema); (u) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.enum = ['only_one']` (single-value array) → bucket `enumerated` with NO warning (a single-value enum is a valid closed vocabulary, even if degenerate); (v) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.enum = 'a,b,c'` (string) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`; (w) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.enum = {a:1,b:2}` (object) → bucket `unknown` with warning `input_schema_properties_first_property_enum_invalid_when_present`; (x) synthetic fixture with `input_schema.properties.first.enum = null` → bucket `unenumerated` with NO warning (null is treated identically to missing). Update existing M35–M53 help-count assertions from 37 to 38.

Add `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyEnumConstraintIndexDeterminismScenario` handler to `tests/eval-regression.mjs` verifying byte-identical output across three runs, closed four-value bucket-key enum, closed three-value aggregation_key enum, bucket order determinism (`enumerated → unenumerated → not_applicable → unknown`), `warnings[]` always present, manifest non-mutation, `input_schema_first_property_enum_constraint` non-persistence, and the empty-array-counts-as-malformed contract (deliberate deviation from M52/M53 trim-empty-counts-as-absent).
