Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamplesBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamplesAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M55 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamples(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` — the FIFTH FROZEN REASON CODE carried forward from M55); returns `'unexampled'` if `firstVal` does NOT have own property `'examples'` OR `firstVal.examples === undefined`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.examples` is present and NOT an `Array` (`Array.isArray(firstVal.examples) === false`) (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_examples_invalid_when_present` — the SIXTH FROZEN REASON CODE, axis-specific); returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.examples` is an `Array` with `length === 0` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_examples_invalid_when_present` — same axis-specific code); returns `'exampled'` if `firstVal.examples` is an `Array` with `length >= 1`. Has-own-property check MUST use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(firstVal, 'examples')`. The classifier MUST NOT classify, count, or distribute per-element types of the examples array.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamplesIndex(manifest)` pure function. Iterates `manifest.capabilities[]` in declared order. For each capability, classifies via `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamples(cap.input_schema)`, accumulates into the four buckets (`exampled`, `unexampled`, `not_applicable`, `unknown`). For each non-empty bucket, emits the 8-field entry with `aggregation_key` chosen per the closed three-value enum. Returns `{ first_property_examples: [...], warnings: [...] }`. Empty buckets MUST be omitted from the output array. Bucket iteration order: `exampled → unexampled → not_applicable → unknown`. Within-bucket: declared order. The function MUST NOT mutate the input manifest.
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamplesIndex(indexResult, options)` pure function returning either deterministic plain-text (default) or `JSON.stringify(indexResult, null, 2)` when `options.json === true`. Plain-text format mirrors M55: header line `Input Schema First Property Examples Index`, one section per non-empty bucket with capability list and counts. Warnings are appended after the buckets in plain-text mode; in JSON mode the `warnings[]` array is always present (even when empty `[]`) at the top-level alongside `first_property_examples[]`.
- [x] Add `cmdSample(argv)` CLI router dispatching to `cmdSampleIndex` for `argv[0] === 'index'`. Unknown subcommand exits 1 with stderr `Unknown sample subcommand: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M51–M55 single-subcommand-noun pattern).
- [x] Add `parseSampleIndexArgs(argv)` and `cmdSampleIndex(argv)`. Supports `--manifest <path>` (default `./tusq.manifest.json`), `--json`, `--out <path>` (rejects paths starting with `.tusq/` mirroring M55), `--sample <value>` (case-sensitive lowercase; closed four-value enum `exampled | unexampled | not_applicable | unknown`). Unknown flags exit 1 with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>`. `--sample` with no value exits 1 with stderr `--sample requires a value`. `--sample` with a value not in the closed four-value set exits 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property examples: <value>`. `--sample` with a value in the set but bucket is absent in the manifest exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property examples: <value>`.
- [x] Wire `'sample'` case in the dispatch table between `'response'` and `'sensitivity'` (alphabetical: r (114) < s (115) at position 0 between `response` and `sample`; s < s at position 0, then a (97) < e (101) at position 1 between `sample` and `sensitivity`).
- [x] Add `'sample'` line in `printHelp()` between `'response'` and `'sensitivity'` with help text `Aggregate capabilities by input_schema.properties[firstKey].examples presence`.
- [x] Add `'sample'` and `'sample index'` entries in `printCommandHelp()` mirroring M55's `'preset'` / `'preset index'` entries: brief synopsis + flag list (`--manifest`, `--json`, `--out`, `--sample`).
- [x] Update all `!== 39` help-count assertions in `tests/smoke.mjs` to `!== 40` (CLI surface growth 39 → 40).
- [x] Add a 24-case M56 smoke test matrix to `tests/smoke.mjs` using a synthetic in-memory manifest fixture covering: (a-d) empty/missing/null/null-prototype `input_schema`; (e-h) malformed `type`; (i) `type: 'array'` → `not_applicable`; (j) `type: 'object'` zero-property → `not_applicable`; (k) `type: 'object'` properties missing → `unknown`; (l) `type: 'object'` properties non-object → `unknown`; (m) `firstVal` not object → `unknown` (5th frozen code); (n) firstVal with `examples` absent → `unexampled`; (o) firstVal with `examples: undefined` → `unexampled`; (p) firstVal with `examples: ['USD']` (single-element) → `exampled`; (q) firstVal with `examples: ['USD', 'EUR', 'GBP']` (multi-element) → `exampled`; (r) firstVal with `examples: [null]` (single-null-element) → `exampled` (per-element type not classified); (s) firstVal with `examples: [42, 'mixed', true, {nested: 'object'}]` (heterogeneous) → `exampled`; (t) firstVal with `examples: []` (M56-SPECIFIC: empty array → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code); (u) firstVal with `examples: 'USD'` (string, not array) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (v) firstVal with `examples: {0: 'USD'}` (object, not array) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (w) firstVal with `examples: null` → `unexampled` (null treated as absent per JSON-Schema convention — `unexampled`, NOT `unknown`); (x) `--sample EXAMPLED` uppercase → exit 1 case-sensitive enforcement; plus the standard --json shape, --out, byte-identity assertions on sibling commands.
- [x] Add an `input-schema-first-property-examples-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval harness 46 → 47 scenarios). Scenario uses an in-memory synthetic manifest with all four bucket-key outcomes represented at least once, asserts byte-identical JSON output across two consecutive runs, and asserts the empty-bucket-MUST-NOT-appear invariant. Add the corresponding `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyExamplesIndexDeterminismScenario` handler in `tests/eval-regression.mjs`.
- [x] Run `node bin/tusq.js sample index --manifest tests/fixtures/express-sample/tusq.manifest.json --json` and assert exit 0 with `first_property_examples[]` containing the expected three-capability bucket distribution (the express-sample fixture's three capabilities all have empty `input_schema` → all three bucket as `unknown` with five-frozen-code warnings, OR — depending on fixture state — `not_applicable` if `type === 'object'` with zero properties). Fixture MUST NOT be mutated; if the fixture's classification yields a single bucket, the test asserts that bucket's content shape and that absent buckets do not appear.
- [x] Verify byte-identity invariants: `tusq compile` output, manifest SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest`, and all 26 prior peer index commands (`surface plan`, `domain index`, `effect index`, `sensitivity index`, `method index`, `auth index`, `confidence index`, `pii index`, `examples index`, `input index`, `output index`, `path index`, `response index`, `request index`, `description index`, `items index`, `strictness index`, `parameter index`, `shape index`, `signature index`, `obligation index`, `binding index`, `gloss index`, `hint index`, `choice index`, `preset index`) byte-identical pre/post.
- [x] Update `.planning/IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with M56 implementation summary mirroring M55's structure: classifier rules, frozen enums, frozen warning codes, axis-specific empty-array divergence (M55 had no axis-specific code; M56 reintroduces axis-specific code as the 6th frozen reason), CLI surface growth, eval scenarios.
- [x] Update `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md`: add Constraint 49 reserving the M56 boundary as "non-runtime-validator / non-doc-contradiction-detector / non-examples-element-type-distributor / non-examples-cardinality-tier-distributor / non-PII-leak-crossref / non-LLM-examples-inferrer / non-marketplace-listing-generator / non-statistical-aggregator." Replace the M55 charter sketch reservation block with a full M56 § "Input Schema First Property Examples Array Presence Index" section detailing: classifier rules; closed bucket enums; six frozen warning codes; bucket iteration order; per-bucket entry shape; CLI surface growth (39 → 40 with `sample` between `response` and `sensitivity`); read-only invariants (26 peer-index byte-identity); non-persistence rule; the M56-vs-M40 noun-distinctness clarification (`tusq sample index` aggregates per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].examples`; `tusq examples index` aggregates capability-level top-level `examples` field — orthogonal axes).
- [x] Update `.planning/command-surface.md`: replace the M56 charter sketch reservation with a full `### tusq sample index` entry mirroring M55's `### tusq preset index` entry — synopsis, flags table, output shape, exit codes, peer-index read-only invariant note.
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq sample index` command with synopsis, flags, output examples (JSON + plain text), and the M56-vs-M40 distinctness clarification.

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Reviewers conflate M56 first-property `examples` array presence with M40 capability-level top-level `examples` field presence | Marketplace-package-compiler reviewer treats `tusq sample index` and `tusq examples index` as duplicates; product manager mistakenly retires one of the two commands; downstream marketplace listing generators read the wrong field and produce mis-rendered example-prompt blocks | SYSTEM_SPEC § M56 + ROADMAP M56 charter explicitly call out the distinctness: M40 reads capability-level top-level `examples` field (worked-example invocations: `{name, request, response}`); M56 reads per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].examples` (JSON-Schema example values for the first input parameter). The two commands are read-only-invariant peers; both are required; CLI-help text on each command MUST cite the orthogonal field; website/docs MUST render both with their distinctness side-by-side |
| Empty `examples: []` array silently bucketed as `exampled` instead of `unknown` with warning | Operators who declared `examples: []` as a placeholder ("I'll fill these in later") see the capability classified as marketplace-ready when in reality there is no renderable sample value; downstream marketplace listings render with no example-prompt block and no warning | Classifier explicitly checks `Array.isArray(firstVal.examples) && firstVal.examples.length >= 1` for the `exampled` outcome; empty array buckets as `unknown` with 6th frozen warning code `input_schema_properties_first_property_examples_invalid_when_present`; smoke matrix case (t) covers this divergence; eval scenario asserts the empty-array-as-unknown invariant; SYSTEM_SPEC and ROADMAP both document this as deliberate alignment with M54's empty-`enum`-is-malformed precedent |
| Per-element type heterogeneity in `examples[]` (e.g., `[42, 'mixed', true]`) classified or warned about | Reviewers expect M56 to enforce per-element type uniformity (e.g., all-strings or all-numbers) and surface warnings when the array carries mixed types; classifier scope creep beyond presence-only counting | PM-frozen scope explicitly defers per-element type distribution to `M-Sample-Examples-Element-Type-Distribution-Index-1`; classifier MUST NOT iterate elements; smoke matrix case (s) asserts heterogeneous `[42, 'mixed', true, {nested: 'object'}]` → `exampled` with no warning; SYSTEM_SPEC Constraint 49 codifies the boundary |
| `firstVal.examples = null` ambiguously bucketed | JSON-Schema's `examples` keyword expects an array, but operators sometimes write `examples: null` to mean "explicitly cleared" or "not yet set"; classifier could plausibly bucket as `unexampled` (treating null as absent) OR `unknown` (treating null as malformed-not-array) | PM-frozen rule: `firstVal.examples === null` is treated as semantically absent — bucket as `unexampled` with no warning (mirrors M55's `firstVal.default === undefined` → `undefaulted` precedent for treating absence-equivalent values as the absent bucket, not the malformed bucket). Smoke matrix case (w) asserts this; SYSTEM_SPEC documents the null-as-absent rule explicitly |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | A capability with `properties: {b: {...}, a: {...}}` could classify on `a` (alphabetical) instead of `b` (insertion); manifest re-serialization could re-order keys | Classifier MUST use `Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` (insertion order); manifest persistence layer (M-Persistence-N) MUST NOT alphabetize property keys; eval scenario inserts a property whose insertion-order-first key differs from its alphabetical-first key and asserts insertion-order classification |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Operators with array-typed top-level input slots see their capabilities flagged as malformed when they are semantically valid (just not object-typed); downstream marketplace-package-compiler reviewers waste cycles triaging false-positive warnings | Classifier explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) for any `input_schema.type` that is a string but not `'object'`; smoke matrix case (i) asserts `type: 'array'` → `not_applicable`; SYSTEM_SPEC explicitly documents the type-other-than-object → not_applicable rule |
| `unexampled` bucket misread as "this capability has no documentation at all" | Marketplace-package-compiler reviewer over-reads `unexampled` as a quality red flag and downranks the capability listing when in reality the capability may have rich docstrings (`description` field), worked examples (capability-level `examples` field — M40), and/or `enum` constraints (M54) — the missing piece is only the per-first-property JSON-Schema `examples[]` array | M56 plain-text and JSON output explicitly label the bucket as "input_schema_first_property_examples = unexampled" (not "no examples" or "no documentation"); SYSTEM_SPEC § M56 includes a "what `unexampled` does and does not mean" clarification block; cross-axis flags (`has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`) are surfaced per-bucket so reviewers can prioritize triage by risk-weighted exposure rather than by bucket-identity-alone |
| Bucket order described as "marketplace-readiness-ranked" or "discoverability-ranked" | Operators / docs / RELEASE_NOTES infer that the bucket order `exampled → unexampled → not_applicable → unknown` implies a quality ranking and use it to drive marketplace-listing decisions; this is FALSE — the order is a deterministic stable-output convention only | PM-frozen scope explicitly enumerates the ranking interpretations the bucket order is NOT (marketplace-readiness, reviewer-priority, marketplace-discoverability, UX-affordance-completeness, OpenAI-ecosystem-readiness, Claude/Anthropic-ecosystem-readiness, MCP-registry-readiness, example-prompt-coverage); SYSTEM_SPEC § M56 reproduces this list verbatim; CLI-help and website/docs MUST NOT describe the order as a ranking |
| `--sample` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operators piping `--sample $BUCKET` with an unbound shell variable get the empty string; a tolerant filter would accept this and either return all buckets or return the first bucket; both behaviors are surprising | `--sample` is case-sensitive lowercase-only; empty string and any non-enum value exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property examples: <value>`; smoke matrix case (x) asserts `--sample EXAMPLED` exits 1 |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `sample` inserted in the wrong slot (e.g., between `request` and `response` instead of between `response` and `sensitivity`) drifts the help-output diff and breaks downstream parsers that depend on stable command order | PM-frozen insertion sequence `..., request, response, sample, sensitivity, shape, ...` is verified character-by-character: `response` (r=114, e=101) < `sample` (s=115, a=97) at position 0 (r (114) < s (115)); `sample` (s=115, a=97) < `sensitivity` (s=115, e=101) at position 0 same (s = s) AND at position 1 `a` (97) < `e` (101); `sample` < `shape` (s=115, h=104) at position 1 `a` (97) < `h` (104). Smoke test asserts the post-`docs` block ordering string-by-string |

### M57: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Title Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.38 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_27565cc0d89187ef` (`turn_7bb9f75f896a40a2`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `906dfca` from intake charter `intent_1777383136065_38f3` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M56: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Examples Array Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.37 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 193–204 (`### Brand-Matched Chat Interface`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy; M51 cites § Skills; M52 cites § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge; M53 cites § Knowledge Artifacts; M54 cites § Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces; M55 cites § Brand-Matched Voice Interface; M56 cites § Marketplace Packages). The section reads: "The first user-facing AI surface is an embedded chat assistant, generated as a self-hostable output. It should: use generated tools and skills; understand the logged-in user's IAM context; support safe action confirmation; match the SaaS brand through extracted and configured brand tokens; allow developers to override all CSS; be embeddable in the SaaS dashboard." The per-`input_schema_first_property_title` capability roll-up answers the chat-interface-readiness question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, does the FIRST input parameter declare a JSON-Schema `title` annotation (a SHORT human-readable label that the embedded chat surface can render in confirmation prompts, slot-filling forms, and brand-matched action widgets to label the parameter for the logged-in user) — or is the FIRST input parameter untitled (forcing the chat compiler to either fall back to the property KEY itself as a UI label, synthesize a placeholder label that may not match the SaaS brand voice, or omit the field label entirely from confirmation prompts), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — there is no first property to label), or unknown (malformed)?" A chat-interface confirmation prompt for a `cancel_subscription.subscription_id` capability whose first input parameter has `title: 'Subscription ID'` can render an at-a-glance brand-matched confirmation card ("Cancel Subscription ID: sub_abc123 — confirm?") that primes the operator on which entity is being acted on; a chat-interface confirmation prompt for the same capability whose first parameter is untitled MUST fall back to the raw property key (`subscription_id`) which leaks the snake_case API contract into the user-facing chat surface and breaks the brand-matched UX promise. The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `untitled` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose primary input slot lacks a UI-renderable label exposes a "chat-confirmation-prompt-readability vs. action-clarity" review obligation that chat-surface-compiler reviewers MUST surface — an `untitled` first-property on a `delete_user_account` capability would render the confirmation card with the raw `user_id` key visible to the operator and break the per-§-Brand-Matched-Chat-Interface-line-202 "match the SaaS brand through extracted and configured brand tokens" requirement. Distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level `description` word count); distinct from M48 (output-side first-property type); distinct from M49 (firstKey `.type`); distinct from M50 (firstKey ∈ required[] membership); distinct from M51 (firstKey `.source`); distinct from M52 (firstKey `.description`); distinct from M53 (firstKey `.format`); distinct from M54 (firstKey `.enum`); distinct from M55 (firstKey `.default`); distinct from M56 (firstKey `.examples`). M57 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].title` (the FIRST input property's JSON-Schema title annotation) — a different field entirely from M49/M50/M51/M52/M53/M54/M55/M56. The first milestone to use § Brand-Matched Chat Interface as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 164–173 (`### Tools`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the UI-label framing item: "tools" are the primary V1 output, and the per-property `title` annotation is what makes a tool's first parameter operator-readable in the chat surface's confirmation-prompt block. Re-cited only — M57's primary source is § Brand-Matched Chat Interface.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `titled | untitled | not_applicable | unknown`. `titled` denotes capabilities whose `input_schema.properties[firstKey]` has own property `'title'` whose value is a `string` of `length >= 1` (any non-empty string content is structurally valid; whitespace-only titles such as `"   "` count as `titled` because the JSON-Schema `title` keyword permits any non-empty string content — whitespace-only validation is deferred to a successor milestone). `untitled` denotes capabilities whose `firstKey` does NOT have own property `'title'` OR `firstKey.title === undefined` OR `firstKey.title === null` (semantically absent — there is no UI-renderable label declared; null-as-absent precedent from M55/M56). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — paralleling M49/M50/M51/M52/M53/M54/M55/M56's `not_applicable` semantics for the same shape. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), `properties[firstKey]` not a plain non-null object, OR `firstKey.title` present-but-not-a-string OR present-string-but-empty (`length === 0`). The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship. **Critically**, M57 DOES have an axis-specific malformation reason code (the JSON-Schema `title` keyword MUST be a non-empty string — present-but-non-string AND empty-string are BOTH malformations); this aligns with M54's empty-`enum`-is-malformed precedent and M56's empty-`examples`-is-malformed precedent.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `title_label | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the two presence buckets (`titled`, `untitled`) carry aggregation_key `title_label`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48–M56 first-property-axis pattern.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid; no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` — the FIFTH FROZEN REASON CODE, carried forward from M55's formal elevation of this code). If `firstVal` IS a plain non-null object: if `firstVal` does NOT have own property `'title'` OR `firstVal.title === undefined` OR `firstVal.title === null`, return `'untitled'` (no warning — absent title declares "no UI-renderable label," which is a chat-readiness signal, not a malformation; null-as-absent precedent from M55/M56). If `firstVal.title` is present and NOT a string (per `typeof firstVal.title !== 'string'`), return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present` — the SIXTH FROZEN REASON CODE, axis-specific, covers BOTH non-string AND empty-string malformations). If `firstVal.title` is a string with `length === 0`, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present` — same axis-specific code; deliberate alignment with M54's empty-`enum`-is-malformed precedent and M56's empty-`examples`-is-malformed precedent because the JSON-Schema `title` keyword requires the string to declare >=1 character to carry semantic intent — an empty title is incoherent on its face). If `firstVal.title` is a string with `length >= 1`, return `'titled'` (no warning — single-character degenerate case is structurally valid; whitespace-only titles such as `"   "` count as `titled` at this milestone — whitespace-trim normalization is deferred to `M-Caption-Title-Whitespace-Distribution-Index-1`).
>   - Has-own-property check MUST use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(firstVal, 'title')` to distinguish "key absent" from "key present with `undefined` value" — both bucket as `untitled`, mirroring M55/M56's canonical idiom.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_title`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M56 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-descriptor / malformed-first-property-`title` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Six frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present`. The `not_applicable` bucket does NOT emit a warning. The `titled` and `untitled` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes; an absent title is a chat-interface-readiness signal, NOT a malformation; a present non-empty string title is a valid UI-renderable label with no warning regardless of brand-voice alignment. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings. **The sixth code `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present` is the axis-specific reason and covers BOTH the "title present but not a string" AND the "title present as string but empty" malformation paths under a single code (deliberate consolidation matching M56's pattern; per-failure-mode subdivision is deferred to a future milestone).** No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT a chat-interface-readiness ranking, NOT a reviewer-priority ranking, NOT a brand-matched-chat-readiness ranking, NOT a UX-label-completeness ranking, NOT a confirmation-prompt-readability ranking, NOT a form-widget-label-coverage ranking, NOT an embedded-chat-friendliness ranking, NOT a CSS-override-readiness ranking): `titled → untitled → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional "labeled-first, unlabeled-second, irrelevant-third, malformed-last" reading order for chat-interface-compiler review aids. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--caption` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `TITLED`, `Untitled`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property title: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M56 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property title: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M56): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 27 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`, `tusq binding index`, `tusq gloss index`, `tusq hint index`, `tusq choice index`, `tusq preset index`, `tusq sample index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_title` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 40 → 41 commands. New top-level noun `caption` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `binding` and `choice` in the post-`docs` alphabetical block (`binding` (b=98) < `caption` (c=99) at position 0 (b (98) < c (99)); `caption` (c=99, a=97) < `choice` (c=99, h=104) at position 0 same (c = c) AND at position 1 `a` (97) < `h` (104); `caption` < `compile` (c=99, o=111) — though `compile` lives in the semantic-priority block before `auth`, alphabetical relation still holds at position 1 `a` (97) < `o` (111)). Insertion sequence in the post-`docs` alphabetical block: ..., `binding`, `caption`, `choice`, `confidence`, ... The fresh noun `caption` (rather than a sibling subcommand under an existing noun) preserves the M31–M56 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `caption` (a concrete short visual label that the chat surface can render in confirmation prompts and form widgets — distinct from `description` which is the existing M44 noun aggregating the capability-level top-level `description` word-count tier, NOT the per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].title` field this milestone aggregates) is intentional and is distinct from `description` (M44, capability-level top-level description-word-count), `signature` (M49), `obligation` (M50), `binding` (M51), `gloss` (M52, which reads firstKey `.description` — distinct from firstKey `.title` because `description` is a longer prose explanation while `title` is a short UI label), `hint` (M53), `choice` (M54), `preset` (M55), and `sample` (M56) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter UI-renderable short-label (chat-confirmation-prompt-renderable title) presence.
> - **Distinctness from existing `gloss` and `description` commands**: the existing `tusq gloss index` command (M52) aggregates the **per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` field** (a longer prose explanation of the parameter); the existing `tusq description index` command (M44) aggregates the **capability-level top-level `description` field word-count tier** (a longer prose explanation of the entire capability). M57's `tusq caption index` aggregates the **per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].title` field** (JSON-Schema's per-property short-label keyword, distinct from `description` which is the longer prose explanation). The three commands are read-only-invariant peers; none alters the others' output bytes. The naming separation (`description` vs. `gloss` vs. `caption`) is the PM-frozen mechanism for distinguishing these three distinct JSON-Schema and capability-level features.
> - **Deferred 10 successor milestones**: `M-Caption-All-Properties-Title-Index-1`, `M-Caption-Nested-Property-Title-Index-1`, `M-Caption-Output-First-Property-Title-Index-1`, `M-Caption-Title-Length-Tier-Index-1`, `M-Caption-Title-Whitespace-Distribution-Index-1`, `M-Caption-Title-Description-Crossref-1`, `M-Caption-Persistence-1`, `M-Caption-Doc-Contradiction-1`, `M-Caption-LLM-Title-Inferrer-1`, `M-Caption-Brand-Voice-Adapter-1`. Each is its own future milestone with its own future acceptance contract.

- [x] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_TITLE_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `titled | untitled | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_TITLE_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `title_label | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_TITLE_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `titled → untitled → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M56 input-schema-first-property-examples constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitleBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitleAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M56 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [x] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitle(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` — the FIFTH FROZEN REASON CODE carried forward from M55/M56); returns `'untitled'` if `firstVal` does NOT have own property `'title'` OR `firstVal.title === undefined` OR `firstVal.title === null`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.title` is present and NOT a string (`typeof firstVal.title !== 'string'`) (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present` — the SIXTH FROZEN REASON CODE, axis-specific); returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.title` is a string with `length === 0` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present` — same axis-specific code); returns `'titled'` if `firstVal.title` is a string with `length >= 1`. Has-own-property check MUST use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(firstVal, 'title')`. The classifier MUST NOT trim whitespace from `firstVal.title`, MUST NOT enforce a maximum length, MUST NOT validate the title against any brand-voice rule, and MUST NOT cross-reference the title against the property key.
- [x] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitleIndex(manifest)` pure function. Iterates `manifest.capabilities[]` in declared order. For each capability, classifies via `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitle(cap.input_schema)`, accumulates into the four buckets (`titled`, `untitled`, `not_applicable`, `unknown`). For each non-empty bucket, emits the 8-field entry with `aggregation_key` chosen per the closed three-value enum. Returns `{ first_property_titles: [...], warnings: [...] }`. Empty buckets MUST be omitted from the output array. Bucket iteration order: `titled → untitled → not_applicable → unknown`. Within-bucket: declared order. The function MUST NOT mutate the input manifest.
- [x] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitleIndex(indexResult, options)` pure function returning either deterministic plain-text (default) or `JSON.stringify(indexResult, null, 2)` when `options.json === true`. Plain-text format mirrors M56: header line `Input Schema First Property Title Index`, one section per non-empty bucket with capability list and counts. Warnings are appended after the buckets in plain-text mode; in JSON mode the `warnings[]` array is always present (even when empty `[]`) at the top-level alongside `first_property_titles[]`.
- [x] Add `cmdCaption(argv)` CLI router dispatching to `cmdCaptionIndex` for `argv[0] === 'index'`. Unknown subcommand exits 1 with stderr `Unknown caption subcommand: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M51–M56 single-subcommand-noun pattern).
- [x] Add `parseCaptionIndexArgs(argv)` and `cmdCaptionIndex(argv)`. Supports `--manifest <path>` (default `./tusq.manifest.json`), `--json`, `--out <path>` (rejects paths starting with `.tusq/` mirroring M56), `--caption <value>` (case-sensitive lowercase; closed four-value enum `titled | untitled | not_applicable | unknown`). Unknown flags exit 1 with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>`. `--caption` with no value exits 1 with stderr `--caption requires a value`. `--caption` with a value not in the closed four-value set exits 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property title: <value>`. `--caption` with a value in the set but bucket is absent in the manifest exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property title: <value>`.
- [x] Wire `'caption'` case in the dispatch table between `'binding'` and `'choice'` (alphabetical: `binding` (b=98) < `caption` (c=99) at position 0; `caption` (c=99, a=97) < `choice` (c=99, h=104) at position 0 same, position 1 a (97) < h (104)).
- [x] Add `'caption'` line in `printHelp()` between `'binding'` and `'choice'` with help text `Index capabilities by input schema first property title presence for planning review`.
- [x] Add `'caption'` and `'caption index'` entries in `printCommandHelp()` mirroring M56's `'sample'` / `'sample index'` entries: brief synopsis + flag list (`--manifest`, `--json`, `--out`, `--caption`).
- [x] Update all `!== 40` help-count assertions in `tests/smoke.mjs` to `!== 41` (CLI surface growth 40 → 41).
- [x] Add a 24-case M57 smoke test matrix to `tests/smoke.mjs` using a synthetic in-memory manifest fixture covering: (a-d) empty/missing/null/null-prototype `input_schema`; (e-h) malformed `type`; (i) `type: 'array'` → `not_applicable`; (j) `type: 'object'` zero-property → `not_applicable`; (k) `type: 'object'` properties missing → `unknown`; (l) `type: 'object'` properties non-object → `unknown`; (m) `firstVal` not object → `unknown` (5th frozen code); (n) firstVal with `title` absent → `untitled`; (o) firstVal with `title: undefined` → `untitled`; (p) firstVal with `title: 'Subscription ID'` (canonical short label) → `titled`; (q) firstVal with `title: 'X'` (single-char degenerate case) → `titled`; (r) firstVal with `title: '   '` (whitespace-only) → `titled` (M57-SPECIFIC: whitespace-only counts as titled at this milestone — whitespace-trim normalization deferred to successor); (s) firstVal with `title: '🎯 Tag'` (emoji + multibyte) → `titled` (length >= 1 sufficient); (t) firstVal with `title: ''` (M57-SPECIFIC: empty string → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code); (u) firstVal with `title: 42` (number, not string) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (v) firstVal with `title: ['Subscription ID']` (array, not string) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (w) firstVal with `title: null` → `untitled` (null treated as semantically absent per M55/M56 null-as-absent precedent — `untitled`, NOT `unknown`); (x) `--caption TITLED` uppercase → exit 1 case-sensitive enforcement; plus the standard --json shape, --out, byte-identity assertions on sibling commands.
- [x] Add an `input-schema-first-property-title-presence-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval harness 47 → 48 scenarios). Scenario uses an in-memory synthetic manifest with all four bucket-key outcomes represented at least once, asserts byte-identical JSON output across two consecutive runs, and asserts the empty-bucket-MUST-NOT-appear invariant. Add the corresponding `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyTitlePresenceIndexDeterminismScenario` handler in `tests/eval-regression.mjs`.
- [x] Run `node bin/tusq.js caption index --manifest tests/fixtures/express-sample/tusq.manifest.json --json` and assert exit 0 with `first_property_titles[]` containing the expected three-capability bucket distribution (the express-sample fixture's three capabilities have `input_schema.properties` whose first property typically has no `title` field → expected to bucket as `untitled` and/or `not_applicable` depending on per-capability shape; `titled` bucket absent on canonical fixture confirming empty-bucket-MUST-NOT-appear invariant). Fixture MUST NOT be mutated; if the fixture's classification yields a single bucket, the test asserts that bucket's content shape and that absent buckets do not appear.
- [x] Verify byte-identity invariants: `tusq compile` output, manifest SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest`, and all 27 prior peer index commands (`surface plan`, `domain index`, `effect index`, `sensitivity index`, `method index`, `auth index`, `confidence index`, `pii index`, `examples index`, `input index`, `output index`, `path index`, `response index`, `request index`, `description index`, `items index`, `strictness index`, `parameter index`, `shape index`, `signature index`, `obligation index`, `binding index`, `gloss index`, `hint index`, `choice index`, `preset index`, `sample index`) byte-identical pre/post.
- [x] Update `.planning/IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` with M57 implementation summary mirroring M56's structure: classifier rules, frozen enums, frozen warning codes, axis-specific empty-string divergence, CLI surface growth, eval scenarios.
- [x] Update `.planning/SYSTEM_SPEC.md`: add Constraint 50 reserving the M57 boundary as "non-runtime-validator / non-doc-contradiction-detector / non-title-length-tier-distributor / non-title-whitespace-distribution-distributor / non-title-description-crossref / non-LLM-title-inferrer / non-brand-voice-adapter / non-statistical-aggregator." Replace the M57 charter sketch reservation block with a full M57 § "Input Schema First Property Title Presence Index" section detailing: classifier rules; closed bucket enums; six frozen warning codes; bucket iteration order; per-bucket entry shape; CLI surface growth (40 → 41 with `caption` between `binding` and `choice`); read-only invariants (27 peer-index byte-identity); non-persistence rule; the M57-vs-M52-vs-M44 noun-distinctness clarification (`tusq caption index` aggregates per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].title`; `tusq gloss index` aggregates per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description`; `tusq description index` aggregates capability-level top-level `description` word-count tier — orthogonal axes).
- [x] Update `.planning/command-surface.md`: replace the M57 charter sketch reservation with a full `### tusq caption index` entry mirroring M56's `### tusq sample index` entry — synopsis, flags table, output shape, exit codes, peer-index read-only invariant note.
- [x] Update `website/docs/cli-reference.md` to document the new `tusq caption index` command with synopsis, flags, output examples (JSON + plain text), and the M57-vs-M52-vs-M44 distinctness clarification.

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| Reviewers conflate M57 first-property `title` annotation with M52 first-property `description` annotation | Chat-interface-compiler reviewer treats `tusq caption index` and `tusq gloss index` as duplicates; product manager mistakenly retires one of the two commands; downstream chat-interface generators read the wrong field and produce confirmation prompts that conflate short-label and long-prose JSON-Schema annotations | SYSTEM_SPEC § M57 + ROADMAP M57 charter explicitly call out the distinctness: M52 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].description` (longer prose explanation of the parameter); M57 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].title` (short UI-renderable label for the parameter). The two commands are read-only-invariant peers; both are required; CLI-help text on each command MUST cite the orthogonal field; website/docs MUST render both with their distinctness side-by-side |
| Reviewers conflate M57 per-property `title` axis with M44 capability-level top-level `description` word-count tier | Reviewer reads M57's `untitled` bucket as "this capability has no documentation at all" when in reality the capability may have a rich top-level `description` (M44), per-property `description` (M52), and `examples[]` array (M40/M56) — the missing piece is only the per-property short-label `title` field | M57 plain-text and JSON output explicitly label the bucket as `input_schema_first_property_title = untitled` (not "no label" or "no documentation"); SYSTEM_SPEC § M57 includes a "what `untitled` does and does not mean" clarification block; cross-axis flags (`has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`) are surfaced per-bucket so reviewers can prioritize triage by risk-weighted exposure rather than by bucket-identity-alone |
| Empty `title: ''` silently bucketed as `titled` instead of `unknown` with warning | Operators who declared `title: ''` as a placeholder ("I'll fill this in later") see the capability classified as chat-ready when in reality there is no UI-renderable label; downstream chat-interface confirmation prompts render with no field label and no warning | Classifier explicitly checks `typeof firstVal.title === 'string' && firstVal.title.length >= 1` for the `titled` outcome; empty string buckets as `unknown` with 6th frozen warning code `input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present`; smoke matrix case (t) covers this divergence; eval scenario asserts the empty-string-as-unknown invariant; SYSTEM_SPEC and ROADMAP both document this as deliberate alignment with M54's empty-`enum`-is-malformed and M56's empty-`examples`-is-malformed precedents |
| Whitespace-only `title: '   '` ambiguously bucketed | Operators sometimes type a whitespace-only title as a placeholder to satisfy "title is required" UI tooling; classifier could plausibly bucket as `untitled` (treating whitespace as semantically absent) OR `titled` (treating any non-empty string as a valid label) OR `unknown` (treating whitespace as malformed) | PM-frozen rule: whitespace-only titles count as `titled` at this milestone — string `length >= 1` is the only check; whitespace-trim normalization is deferred to `M-Caption-Title-Whitespace-Distribution-Index-1` successor; smoke matrix case (r) asserts this; SYSTEM_SPEC documents the no-whitespace-trim rule explicitly |
| `firstVal.title = null` ambiguously bucketed | JSON-Schema's `title` keyword expects a string, but operators sometimes write `title: null` to mean "explicitly cleared" or "not yet set"; classifier could plausibly bucket as `untitled` (treating null as absent) OR `unknown` (treating null as malformed-not-string) | PM-frozen rule: `firstVal.title === null` is treated as semantically absent — bucket as `untitled` with no warning (mirrors M55's `firstVal.default === undefined` → `undefaulted` precedent and M56's `firstVal.examples === null` → `unexampled` precedent for treating absence-equivalent values as the absent bucket, not the malformed bucket). Smoke matrix case (w) asserts this; SYSTEM_SPEC documents the null-as-absent rule explicitly |
| Object.keys insertion-order mis-implementation drifts the "first property" selection | A capability with `properties: {b: {...}, a: {...}}` could classify on `a` (alphabetical) instead of `b` (insertion); manifest re-serialization could re-order keys | Classifier MUST use `Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` (insertion order); manifest persistence layer (M-Persistence-N) MUST NOT alphabetize property keys; eval scenario inserts a property whose insertion-order-first key differs from its alphabetical-first key and asserts insertion-order classification |
| Non-object inputs (`input_schema.type === 'array'`) bucketed as `unknown` instead of `not_applicable` | Operators with array-typed top-level input slots see their capabilities flagged as malformed when they are semantically valid (just not object-typed); downstream chat-interface-compiler reviewers waste cycles triaging false-positive warnings | Classifier explicitly returns `'not_applicable'` (no warning) for any `input_schema.type` that is a string but not `'object'`; smoke matrix case (i) asserts `type: 'array'` → `not_applicable`; SYSTEM_SPEC explicitly documents the type-other-than-object → not_applicable rule |
| Bucket order described as "chat-readiness-ranked" or "brand-matched-readiness-ranked" | Operators / docs / RELEASE_NOTES infer that the bucket order `titled → untitled → not_applicable → unknown` implies a quality ranking and use it to drive chat-interface-compilation decisions; this is FALSE — the order is a deterministic stable-output convention only | PM-frozen scope explicitly enumerates the ranking interpretations the bucket order is NOT (chat-interface-readiness, reviewer-priority, brand-matched-chat-readiness, UX-label-completeness, confirmation-prompt-readability, form-widget-label-coverage, embedded-chat-friendliness, CSS-override-readiness); SYSTEM_SPEC § M57 reproduces this list verbatim; CLI-help and website/docs MUST NOT describe the order as a ranking |
| `--caption` filter silently lower-cases input or accepts the empty string | Operators piping `--caption $BUCKET` with an unbound shell variable get the empty string; a tolerant filter would accept this and either return all buckets or return the first bucket; both behaviors are surprising | `--caption` is case-sensitive lowercase-only; empty string and any non-enum value exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property title: <value>`; smoke matrix case (x) asserts `--caption TITLED` exits 1 |
| CLI surface alphabetical-insertion miscalculation | `caption` inserted in the wrong slot (e.g., between `auth` and `binding` instead of between `binding` and `choice`) drifts the help-output diff and breaks downstream parsers that depend on stable command order | PM-frozen insertion sequence `..., auth, binding, caption, choice, confidence, ...` is verified character-by-character: `binding` (b=98) < `caption` (c=99) at position 0 (b (98) < c (99)); `caption` (c=99, a=97) < `choice` (c=99, h=104) at position 0 same (c = c) AND at position 1 `a` (97) < `h` (104); `caption` < `confidence` (c=99, o=111) at position 1 `a` (97) < `o` (111). Smoke test asserts the post-`docs` block ordering string-by-string |

### M58: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Deprecated Annotation Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.39 (PROPOSED)

> Charter origin: bound 2026-04-28 in `run_68913f73e2cc30a6` (`turn_46c96427c73e4d4e`, PM attempt 1) at HEAD `fea7501` from intake charter `intent_1777385467583_6504` (vision_scan, category `roadmap_exhausted_vision_open`, charter "Derive next bounded roadmap increment from VISION.md. Current roadmap checked through M57: Static Capability Input Schema First Property Title Presence Index Export from Manifest Evidence (~0.5 day) — V1.38 (PROPOSED)."). VISION sources cited:
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 83–90 (`### Integrations And Side Effects`)** — primary aggregation source; has NOT been the primary aggregation source for any shipped milestone (M28/M33 cite § Data, Risk, And Sensitivity; M32 cites § Code And API Surface + § Capability Overexposure; M34 cites § Code And API Surface; M35 cites § Auth, IAM; M36 cites § Source Inventory And Confidence + § False Governance Confidence; M37 cites § Data Leakage; M38 cites § Bad Workflow Inference; M39 cites § Capability Overexposure; M40 cites § Stale Or Contradictory Docs; M41 cites § Artifact Sprawl; M42 cites § Database, Warehouse, And Data Model; M43 cites § Domain Model And Workflows; M44 cites § Public Docs And Market-Facing Truth; M45 cites § Frontend, Design System, And Product UX; M46 cites § Tools; M47 cites § MCP Server; M48 cites § Developer Artifacts; M49 cites § The Promise; M50 cites § Action Execution Policy; M51 cites § Skills; M52 cites § Internal Docs And Company Operating Knowledge; M53 cites § Knowledge Artifacts; M54 cites § Embeddable Widgets And Command Surfaces; M55 cites § Brand-Matched Voice Interface; M56 cites § Marketplace Packages; M57 cites § Brand-Matched Chat Interface). The section reads: "third-party API calls such as billing, email, CRM, support, analytics, and identity providers; emitted and consumed webhooks; emails, notifications, background jobs, queue messages, and scheduled tasks; external systems that may be affected by an AI-triggered action; integration configs for Slack, Teams, Zendesk, Intercom, Salesforce, HubSpot, Gainsight, Stripe, Segment, data warehouses, and identity providers." The per-`input_schema_first_property_deprecated` capability roll-up answers the integration-side-effect-triage question: "for the object-typed input parameter sets my tools accept, does the FIRST input parameter declare a JSON-Schema `deprecated: true` annotation (a sunset signal that the parameter contract is changing and downstream side-effect emitters — webhooks, queue jobs, billing API calls, email triggers, analytics events, CRM sync configs — bound to this capability need triage before the deprecation lands), is it explicitly active (`deprecated: false` or absent — the JSON-Schema default), not-applicable (non-object input or empty-property object — there is no first property to deprecate), or unknown (malformed)?" A capability such as `update_subscription.legacy_billing_id` whose first input parameter has `deprecated: true` signals to ingestion that any Stripe-integration webhook handler, billing-CRM sync job, or analytics event emitter that binds to this parameter must be migrated to the replacement parameter before the next major release; a capability whose first parameter is `deprecated: false` or absent declares no sunset obligation. The cross-axis flags `has_destructive_side_effect` and `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity` are particularly load-bearing for the `deprecated` bucket where a destructive or restricted-sensitivity capability whose primary input slot is sunset exposes a "lifecycle-migration vs. integration-stability" review obligation that integration-pipeline reviewers MUST surface — a `deprecated` first-property on a `delete_user_account` capability would force the side-effect emitter (audit-log webhook + email-notification queue job + identity-provider sync) to either migrate to the replacement parameter or accept that the capability will break at the deprecation deadline. Distinct from M44 (capability-level top-level `description` word count); distinct from M48 (output-side first-property type); distinct from M49 (firstKey `.type`); distinct from M50 (firstKey ∈ required[] membership); distinct from M51 (firstKey `.source`); distinct from M52 (firstKey `.description`); distinct from M53 (firstKey `.format`); distinct from M54 (firstKey `.enum`); distinct from M55 (firstKey `.default`); distinct from M56 (firstKey `.examples`); distinct from M57 (firstKey `.title`). M58 reads `input_schema.properties[firstKey].deprecated` (the FIRST input property's JSON-Schema `deprecated` annotation, a JSON-Schema 2019-09+ boolean keyword) — a different field entirely from M49–M57. The first milestone to use § Integrations And Side Effects as the primary aggregation source.
> - **`.planning/VISION.md` lines 164–173 (`### Tools`)** — re-cited only as a structural anchor for the lifecycle-stage framing item: "tools" are the primary V1 output, and the per-property `deprecated` annotation is what makes a tool's first parameter a sunset signal for downstream integration emitters. Re-cited only — M58's primary source is § Integrations And Side Effects.
>
> Charter Bound (PM-frozen scope decisions; dev MUST carry these forward verbatim):
> - **Bucket-key enum** (closed four-value): `deprecated | active | not_applicable | unknown`. `deprecated` denotes capabilities whose `input_schema.properties[firstKey]` has own property `'deprecated'` whose value is the boolean `true`. `active` denotes capabilities whose `firstKey` does NOT have own property `'deprecated'` OR `firstKey.deprecated === undefined` OR `firstKey.deprecated === null` (semantically absent — JSON-Schema's stated default for `deprecated` is `false`; null-as-absent precedent from M55/M56/M57) OR `firstKey.deprecated === false` (explicit not-deprecated). `not_applicable` denotes capabilities whose top-level input is not an object (`input_schema.type !== 'object'`) OR is an object with zero declared properties (`Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`) — paralleling M49–M57's `not_applicable` semantics. `unknown` denotes capabilities with malformed/missing `input_schema`, malformed `input_schema.type`, malformed `input_schema.properties` (when `type === 'object'`), `properties[firstKey]` not a plain non-null object, OR `firstKey.deprecated` present-but-not-a-boolean (`typeof firstKey.deprecated !== 'boolean'`). The closed four-value bucket-key set is immutable post-ship. **Critically**, M58 DOES have an axis-specific malformation reason code (the JSON-Schema `deprecated` keyword MUST be a boolean — string `'true'`, number `1`, array, or object are ALL malformations); this aligns with M53's non-string-`format` precedent and M56/M57's axis-specific malformation precedent.
> - **Aggregation key enum** (closed three-value): `lifecycle_stage | not_applicable | unknown`. Capabilities in the two presence buckets (`deprecated`, `active`) carry aggregation_key `lifecycle_stage`; capabilities in `not_applicable` carry aggregation_key `not_applicable`; capabilities in `unknown` carry aggregation_key `unknown`. Three values match the M48–M57 first-property-axis pattern.
> - **Classifier function** (frozen on the manifest's per-capability `input_schema` field):
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema` is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_field_missing` when absent; `input_schema_field_not_object` when present but not a plain non-null object).
>   - `unknown` if `input_schema.type` is missing or non-string (warning reason `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`).
>   - `not_applicable` if `input_schema.type` is a string but is NOT the literal `'object'` — semantically valid, no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'`: `unknown` if `input_schema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array (warning reason `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`).
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `input_schema.properties` is a plain non-null object: if `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length === 0`, return `not_applicable` — semantically valid; no warning.
>   - When `input_schema.type === 'object'` and `Object.keys(input_schema.properties).length > 0`: take `firstKey = Object.keys(input_schema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = input_schema.properties[firstKey]`. If `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object, return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` — the FIFTH FROZEN REASON CODE, carried forward from M55/M56/M57). If `firstVal` IS a plain non-null object: if `firstVal` does NOT have own property `'deprecated'` OR `firstVal.deprecated === undefined` OR `firstVal.deprecated === null`, return `'active'` (no warning — JSON-Schema's stated default for `deprecated` is `false`; null-as-absent precedent from M55/M56/M57). If `firstVal.deprecated === false`, return `'active'` (no warning — explicit not-deprecated). If `firstVal.deprecated === true`, return `'deprecated'` (no warning — the canonical sunset signal). If `firstVal.deprecated` is present and is NOT a boolean (`typeof firstVal.deprecated !== 'boolean'`), return `'unknown'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present` — the SIXTH FROZEN REASON CODE, axis-specific). Strings such as `'true'` or `'false'`, numbers such as `1` or `0`, arrays, and objects ALL bucket as `unknown`; truthy/falsy coercion MUST NOT be performed.
>   - Has-own-property check MUST use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(firstVal, 'deprecated')` to distinguish "key absent" from "key present with `undefined` value" — both bucket as `active`, mirroring M55/M56/M57's canonical idiom.
>   - Object.keys insertion-order semantics are JavaScript's deterministic string-key ordering; the manifest's JSON parsing preserves this order. The classifier MUST NOT sort, re-order, or otherwise mutate the property-key sequence.
> - **Per-bucket entry shape** (frozen 8 fields): `input_schema_first_property_deprecated`, `aggregation_key`, `capability_count`, `capabilities[]`, `approved_count`, `gated_count`, `has_destructive_side_effect`, `has_restricted_or_confidential_sensitivity`. Order matches M31–M57 precedent.
> - **Top-level `warnings[]`** array: present only in `--json` mode; always emitted (even when empty `[]`) for shape stability; per-capability malformed-`input_schema` / malformed-first-property-descriptor / malformed-first-property-`deprecated` warnings carried byte-identically across runs.
> - **Six frozen warning reason codes**: `input_schema_field_missing`, `input_schema_field_not_object`, `input_schema_type_missing_or_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_field_missing_when_type_is_object`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid`, `input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present`. The `not_applicable` bucket does NOT emit a warning. The `deprecated` and `active` buckets do NOT emit warnings — they are valid classified outcomes; a sunset annotation is a lifecycle-stage signal, NOT a malformation; an absent or `false` `deprecated` is the JSON-Schema-default active state with no warning. Only the `unknown` bucket triggers warnings. **The sixth code `input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present` is the axis-specific reason and covers ALL non-boolean malformation paths (string `'true'`, number `1`, array, object, etc.) under a single consolidated code (deliberate consolidation matching M56/M57's pattern; per-failure-mode subdivision is deferred to a future milestone).** No additional reason codes may be introduced without a separate ROADMAP milestone.
> - **Bucket iteration order** (closed-enum, deterministic stable-output convention only — explicitly NOT an integration-readiness ranking, NOT a sunset-priority ranking, NOT a side-effect-emitter-triage ranking, NOT a webhook-migration-priority ranking, NOT a billing-integration-stability ranking, NOT a queue-job-deprecation ranking, NOT an analytics-event-sunset ranking, NOT a CRM-sync-lifecycle-stage ranking): `deprecated → active → not_applicable → unknown`. The order matches the conventional "sunset-flagged-first, active-second, irrelevant-third, malformed-last" reading order for integration-pipeline review aids — sunset signals are surfaced first because they drive integration-migration scheduling. Empty buckets MUST NOT appear.
> - **Within-bucket order**: manifest declared order (capabilities[] index ascending).
> - **`--legacy` filter**: case-sensitive lowercase-only; uppercase or mixed-case values like `DEPRECATED`, `Active`, or any value not in the closed four-value set exit 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property deprecated state: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M35–M57 case-sensitivity precedent). Filtering on a bucket with zero capabilities exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property deprecated state: <value>`.
> - **Read-only invariants** (mirrors § M32–M57): manifest mtime + SHA-256 + every capability's `capability_digest` byte-identical pre/post; `tusq compile` output byte-identical pre/post; all 28 prior peer index commands byte-identical pre/post (`tusq surface plan`, `tusq domain index`, `tusq effect index`, `tusq sensitivity index`, `tusq method index`, `tusq auth index`, `tusq confidence index`, `tusq pii index`, `tusq examples index`, `tusq input index`, `tusq output index`, `tusq path index`, `tusq response index`, `tusq request index`, `tusq description index`, `tusq items index`, `tusq strictness index`, `tusq parameter index`, `tusq shape index`, `tusq signature index`, `tusq obligation index`, `tusq binding index`, `tusq gloss index`, `tusq hint index`, `tusq choice index`, `tusq preset index`, `tusq sample index`, `tusq caption index`).
> - **Non-persistence rule**: `input_schema_first_property_deprecated` MUST NOT be written into `tusq.manifest.json` — it is reviewer-aid metadata only.
> - **CLI surface growth**: 41 → 42 commands. New top-level noun `legacy` with single subcommand `index`, inserted alphabetically between `items` and `method` in the post-`docs` alphabetical block (`items` (i=105) < `legacy` (l=108) at position 0 (i (105) < l (108)); `legacy` (l=108) < `method` (m=109) at position 0 (l (108) < m (109))). Insertion sequence in the post-`docs` alphabetical block: ..., `items`, `legacy`, `method`, `obligation`, ... The fresh noun `legacy` (rather than a sibling subcommand under an existing noun) preserves the M31–M57 single-subcommand-per-noun precedent. The naming choice `legacy` (a concrete short noun denoting lifecycle-stage triage — distinct from `examples` which is the existing M40 noun aggregating capability-level top-level worked-example records, NOT the per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].deprecated` field this milestone aggregates; distinct from `confidence` which is the M36 noun aggregating extraction-source confidence tiers; distinct from `version` which is the M19 noun reporting CLI version; the noun `legacy` is unused elsewhere in the surface) is intentional and is distinct from `description` (M44), `signature` (M49), `obligation` (M50), `binding` (M51), `gloss` (M52), `hint` (M53), `choice` (M54), `preset` (M55), `sample` (M56), and `caption` (M57) — the new noun matches the conceptual axis the index aggregates over: per-capability first-input-parameter sunset/active lifecycle stage.
> - **Distinctness from existing peer commands**: the existing `tusq examples index` command (M40) aggregates the **capability-level top-level `examples` worked-example record array** (a top-level invocation-record list); the existing `tusq sample index` command (M56) aggregates the **per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].examples` array** (per-property example values); M58's `tusq legacy index` aggregates the **per-property `input_schema.properties[firstKey].deprecated` boolean** (per-property sunset annotation). The three commands are read-only-invariant peers reading three orthogonal JSON-Schema and capability-level features under three distinct nouns; none alters the others' output bytes.
> - **Deferred 10 successor milestones**: `M-Legacy-All-Properties-Deprecated-Index-1`, `M-Legacy-Nested-Property-Deprecated-Index-1`, `M-Legacy-Output-First-Property-Deprecated-Index-1`, `M-Legacy-Deprecated-Sunset-Date-Annotation-Index-1`, `M-Legacy-Deprecated-Replacement-Crossref-1`, `M-Legacy-Persistence-1`, `M-Legacy-Doc-Contradiction-1`, `M-Legacy-LLM-Sunset-Inferrer-1`, `M-Legacy-Webhook-Sunset-Adapter-1`, `M-Legacy-Side-Effect-Migration-Planner-1`. Each is its own future milestone with its own future acceptance contract.

- [ ] Add `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DEPRECATED_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `deprecated | active | not_applicable | unknown`), `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DEPRECATED_AGGREGATION_KEY_ENUM` (frozen `Set` of `lifecycle_stage | not_applicable | unknown`), and `INPUT_SCHEMA_FIRST_PROPERTY_DEPRECATED_BUCKET_ORDER` (frozen `Object.freeze([...])` with literal closed-enum order `deprecated → active → not_applicable`, with `unknown` appended only when the bucket is non-empty) constants in `src/cli.js` near the existing M57 input-schema-first-property-title constants. Add synchronous module-init guards `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecatedBucketKey` and `_guardInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecatedAggregationKey` mirroring M35–M57 precedent — the IIFE must throw a descriptive `Error` synchronously at module load if the bucket-order array elements diverge from the enum set or if the enum set is missing required values.
- [ ] Add `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecated(inputSchema)` pure function (no manifest mutation, no `capability_digest` recomputation, no I/O). Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema` that is `null`/`undefined`/missing/not-a-plain-object/array. Returns `'unknown'` for `inputSchema.type` missing or non-string. Returns `'not_applicable'` for `inputSchema.type` that is a string but is NOT literal `'object'`. When `inputSchema.type === 'object'`: returns `'unknown'` if `inputSchema.properties` is missing/`null`/`undefined`/not-a-plain-object/array; returns `'not_applicable'` if `Object.keys(inputSchema.properties).length === 0`; otherwise reads `firstKey = Object.keys(inputSchema.properties)[0]` and `firstVal = inputSchema.properties[firstKey]`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal` is not a plain non-null object (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid` — the FIFTH FROZEN REASON CODE carried forward from M55/M56/M57); returns `'active'` if `firstVal` does NOT have own property `'deprecated'` OR `firstVal.deprecated === undefined` OR `firstVal.deprecated === null` (no warning — null-as-absent per M55/M56/M57); returns `'active'` if `firstVal.deprecated === false`; returns `'deprecated'` if `firstVal.deprecated === true`; returns `'unknown'` if `firstVal.deprecated` is present and `typeof firstVal.deprecated !== 'boolean'` (warning reason `input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present` — the SIXTH FROZEN REASON CODE, axis-specific). Has-own-property check MUST use `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(firstVal, 'deprecated')`. The classifier MUST NOT coerce truthy/falsy values, MUST NOT accept string `'true'`/`'false'`, MUST NOT accept number `0`/`1`, and MUST NOT cross-reference the deprecation flag against any sunset-date annotation.
- [ ] Add `buildInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecatedIndex(manifest)` pure function. Iterates `manifest.capabilities[]` in declared order. For each capability, classifies via `classifyInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecated(cap.input_schema)`, accumulates into the four buckets (`deprecated`, `active`, `not_applicable`, `unknown`). For each non-empty bucket, emits the 8-field entry with `aggregation_key` chosen per the closed three-value enum. Returns `{ first_property_deprecation_states: [...], warnings: [...] }`. Empty buckets MUST be omitted from the output array. Bucket iteration order: `deprecated → active → not_applicable → unknown`. Within-bucket: declared order. The function MUST NOT mutate the input manifest.
- [ ] Add `formatInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecatedIndex(indexResult, options)` pure function returning either deterministic plain-text (default) or `JSON.stringify(indexResult, null, 2)` when `options.json === true`. Plain-text format mirrors M57: header line `Input Schema First Property Deprecated Index`, one section per non-empty bucket with capability list and counts. Warnings are appended after the buckets in plain-text mode; in JSON mode the `warnings[]` array is always present (even when empty `[]`) at the top-level alongside `first_property_deprecation_states[]`.
- [ ] Add `cmdLegacy(argv)` CLI router dispatching to `cmdLegacyIndex` for `argv[0] === 'index'`. Unknown subcommand exits 1 with stderr `Unknown legacy subcommand: <value>` and empty stdout (mirrors M51–M57 single-subcommand-noun pattern).
- [ ] Add `parseLegacyIndexArgs(argv)` and `cmdLegacyIndex(argv)`. Supports `--manifest <path>` (default `./tusq.manifest.json`), `--json`, `--out <path>` (rejects paths starting with `.tusq/` mirroring M57), `--legacy <value>` (case-sensitive lowercase; closed four-value enum `deprecated | active | not_applicable | unknown`). Unknown flags exit 1 with stderr `Unknown flag: <flag>`. `--legacy` with no value exits 1 with stderr `--legacy requires a value`. `--legacy` with a value not in the closed four-value set exits 1 with stderr `Unknown input schema first property deprecated state: <value>`. `--legacy` with a value in the set but bucket is absent in the manifest exits 1 with stderr `No capabilities found for input schema first property deprecated state: <value>`.
- [ ] Wire `'legacy'` case in the dispatch table between `'items'` and `'method'` (alphabetical: `items` (i=105) < `legacy` (l=108) at position 0; `legacy` (l=108) < `method` (m=109) at position 0).
- [ ] Add `'legacy'` line in `printHelp()` between `'items'` and `'method'` with help text `Index capabilities by input schema first property deprecated annotation presence for planning review`.
- [ ] Add `'legacy'` and `'legacy index'` entries in `printCommandHelp()` mirroring M57's `'caption'` / `'caption index'` entries: brief synopsis + flag list (`--manifest`, `--json`, `--out`, `--legacy`).
- [ ] Update all `!== 41` help-count assertions in `tests/smoke.mjs` to `!== 42` (CLI surface growth 41 → 42).
- [ ] Add a 24-case M58 smoke test matrix to `tests/smoke.mjs` using a synthetic in-memory manifest fixture covering: (a-d) empty/missing/null/null-prototype `input_schema`; (e-h) malformed `type`; (i) `type: 'array'` → `not_applicable`; (j) `type: 'object'` zero-property → `not_applicable`; (k) `type: 'object'` properties missing → `unknown`; (l) `type: 'object'` properties non-object → `unknown`; (m) `firstVal` not object → `unknown` (5th frozen code); (n) firstVal with `deprecated` absent → `active`; (o) firstVal with `deprecated: undefined` → `active`; (p) firstVal with `deprecated: false` → `active`; (q) firstVal with `deprecated: true` → `deprecated`; (r) firstVal with `deprecated: null` → `active` (M58-SPECIFIC: null-as-absent per M55/M56/M57 precedent — `active`, NOT `unknown`); (s) firstVal with `deprecated: 'true'` (string, not boolean) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (t) firstVal with `deprecated: 1` (number, not boolean) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (u) firstVal with `deprecated: 0` (number falsy, not boolean) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code (M58-SPECIFIC: NO truthy/falsy coercion); (v) firstVal with `deprecated: []` (array, not boolean) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (w) firstVal with `deprecated: {}` (object, not boolean) → `unknown` WITH 6th frozen warning code; (x) `--legacy DEPRECATED` uppercase → exit 1 case-sensitive enforcement; plus the standard --json shape, --out, byte-identity assertions on sibling commands.
- [ ] Add an `input-schema-first-property-deprecated-index-determinism` eval scenario to `tests/evals/governed-cli-scenarios.json` (eval harness 48 → 49 scenarios). Scenario uses an in-memory synthetic manifest with all four bucket-key outcomes represented at least once, asserts byte-identical JSON output across two consecutive runs, and asserts the empty-bucket-MUST-NOT-appear invariant. Add the corresponding `runInputSchemaFirstPropertyDeprecatedIndexDeterminismScenario` handler in `tests/eval-regression.mjs`.
- [ ] Run `node bin/tusq.js legacy index --manifest tests/fixtures/express-sample/tusq.manifest.json --json` and assert exit 0 with `first_property_deprecation_states[]` containing the expected three-capability bucket distribution (the express-sample fixture's three capabilities have `input_schema.properties` whose first property typically has no `deprecated` field → expected to bucket as `active` and/or `not_applicable` depending on per-capability shape; `deprecated` bucket absent on canonical fixture confirming empty-bucket-MUST-NOT-appear invariant). Fixture MUST NOT be mutated; if the fixture's classification yields a single bucket, the test asserts that bucket's content shape and that absent buckets do not appear.
